name: Deploy New Model to Inference
description: An MLOps engineer is tasked with deploying a newly trained machine learning model to the production inference environment. The goal is to ensure the new model version is safely released, with monitoring and validation steps to confirm correct operation. This process must follow safe deployment practices and maintain the integrity of confidential production data.

invariants: Existing stable production model remains available until full rollout is complete
No direct modification of production datasets is performed
All changes to manifests are committed to version control before deployment
All approvals must be recorded in the designated change management system
refusal_conditions: Required model artifact or checksum is missing
Required acceptance criteria documentation is missing
Required change management or approval systems are unavailable

steps.action: Review deployment requirements and confirm model artifact integrity
steps.verify: Artifact checksum matches and deployment checklist is signed off

steps.action: Validate new model against a holdout test dataset in staging
steps.verify: Test results align with acceptance criteria documented in the ticket

steps.action: Update deployment manifests to reference the new model version
steps.verify: Manifest files are updated and committed to the version control system

steps.action: Obtain manager approval for production deployment
steps.verify: Approval is recorded in the change management system

steps.action: Schedule a maintenance window for deployment
steps.verify: Deployment window is visible on the team calendar

steps.action: Deploy the new model to a canary subset of production traffic
steps.verify: Canary deployment status is reported as healthy in the monitoring dashboard

steps.action: Monitor canary metrics for performance and error rates
steps.verify: No anomalies or regression alerts are observed in metrics

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of canary results and intent to proceed
steps.verify: Notification is acknowledged in the team communication channel

steps.action: Roll out the new model to full production traffic
steps.verify: Production deployment status is healthy and serving live requests

steps.action: Monitor post-deployment metrics and logs for issues
steps.verify: No critical errors or performance regressions are detected

steps.action: Update model registry with new version and deployment metadata
steps.verify: Model registry entry reflects current version and deployment timestamp

steps.action: Document deployment steps and validation results in the operations log
steps.verify: Operations log entry is complete and accessible to the team

abort_conditions: Artifact validation fails or integrity cannot be confirmed
Manager approval is denied or not received
Canary deployment shows regression or critical errors

name: Production IAM Security Audit
description: A security engineer performs a comprehensive audit of Identity and Access Management (IAM) configurations in the production environment. The goal is to identify misconfigurations, excessive permissions, and potential security risks, ensuring compliance with internal and regulatory security standards. The process includes review, documentation, and reporting for follow-up actions.

invariants: No IAM configuration changes are performed
No credentials are created, rotated, or deleted
All findings are documented in the audit report repository
refusal_conditions: Production IAM configuration is not readable

steps.action: Review current user and service account inventory in production IAM.
steps.verify: Inventory list is complete and up to date.

steps.action: Document all privileged roles and their assignments.
steps.verify: Privileged role mapping is documented and reviewed.

steps.action: Analyze group memberships for excessive privilege escalation paths.
steps.verify: Potential privilege escalation paths are flagged in the audit report.

steps.action: Check for orphaned accounts or unused credentials.
steps.verify: List of orphaned or unused accounts is created.

steps.action: Review recent IAM configuration changes in production.
steps.verify: Change log is reviewed and anomalies are noted.

steps.action: Assess adherence to least privilege principles for all roles.
steps.verify: Roles with excessive permissions are identified.

steps.action: Cross-reference access logs for unusual activity related to IAM.
steps.verify: Unusual activity is noted in the audit findings.

steps.action: Check compliance of IAM settings with internal and regulatory policies.
steps.verify: Compliance checklist is completed.

steps.action: Identify accounts lacking multi-factor authentication.
steps.verify: List of accounts without MFA is generated.

steps.action: Review third-party integrations with production IAM.
steps.verify: Documentation of integrations is updated and reviewed.

steps.action: Validate IAM audit logging is enabled and functioning.
steps.verify: Audit log status is confirmed.

steps.action: Summarize all findings and risk areas in an audit report.
steps.verify: Audit report draft is completed.

steps.action: Share the draft report with relevant security and compliance stakeholders.
steps.verify: Stakeholders acknowledge report receipt.

steps.action: Document recommended remediation actions for identified risks.
steps.verify: Remediation plan is attached to the audit report.

steps.action: Archive audit artifacts in a secure documentation repository.
steps.verify: Artifacts are stored and access is restricted.

steps.action: Schedule follow-up review meeting with security and operations teams.
steps.verify: Meeting is scheduled and calendar invite is sent.

abort_conditions: IAM inventory is incomplete or inaccessible
Access to audit logs is denied
Verification identifies unsafe or non-compliant environment

name: Routine Database Maintenance
description: A site reliability engineer performs scheduled maintenance on the production database to ensure optimal performance and reliability. The process is conducted with production-admin access and must not disrupt live services. All actions are logged and verified to comply with internal controls.

invariants: No schema changes are performed
No data deletion operations are executed
Backups must be validated before any maintenance tasks are started
refusal_conditions: Database health metrics indicate critical state
Maintenance window not confirmed in change calendar

steps.action: Review the maintenance schedule and confirm the maintenance window with stakeholders
steps.verify: Maintenance window is acknowledged in the change calendar

steps.action: Notify affected teams about the upcoming database maintenance
steps.verify: Notification is visible in team communication channels

steps.action: Check current database health metrics and active connections
steps.verify: Health metrics are recorded and show normal operation

steps.action: Ensure recent backups are available and validated
steps.verify: Backup status report confirms successful backup and integrity check

steps.action: Place the database in maintenance mode if supported
steps.verify: Maintenance mode status is reflected in the database dashboard

steps.action: Apply scheduled maintenance tasks such as index optimization and log rotation
steps.verify: Maintenance log reflects successful completion of tasks

steps.action: Monitor database performance metrics during maintenance activities
steps.verify: Performance metrics remain within expected thresholds

steps.action: Remove the database from maintenance mode
steps.verify: Database is confirmed to be back in normal operation mode

steps.action: Notify stakeholders that maintenance is complete
steps.verify: Post-maintenance notification is visible to all relevant teams

steps.action: Document the maintenance activities and any findings in the operational log
steps.verify: Maintenance record is complete and accessible in documentation system

abort_conditions: Backup validation fails
Database health check indicates critical issues
Unexpected errors occur during maintenance steps

name: Rollback Production Deployment
description: Initiate and execute a rollback of a production deployment in Kubernetes following a failed release. The goal is to restore application stability while minimizing downtime and data loss. This process follows established safety and approval protocols to ensure production integrity.

invariants: Rollback targets only the specified deployment and namespace
Persistent data stores are not modified or deleted
All rollback actions are linked to an incident ticket
refusal_conditions: Manager approval not recorded in incident ticket
Target deployment or namespace cannot be resolved
Cluster health indicates existing critical outage unrelated to target deployment

steps.action: Review the release notes and incident reports to confirm the need for rollback
steps.verify: Incident documentation and release logs confirm rollback requirement

steps.action: Notify the oncall manager and request explicit approval to proceed with rollback
steps.verify: Manager approval is received and documented in the incident ticket

steps.action: Communicate planned rollback to impacted stakeholders and affected teams
steps.verify: Stakeholders acknowledge receipt of rollback notification

steps.action: Identify the last stable deployment version to target for rollback
steps.verify: Stable deployment version is documented in the change management system

steps.action: Validate current cluster health and resource availability before initiating rollback
steps.verify: Cluster health metrics and monitoring dashboards show no critical issues

steps.action: Update deployment configuration to reference the last stable version
steps.verify: Configuration management system reflects the correct deployment version

steps.action: Initiate the rollback process in the production Kubernetes cluster
steps.verify: Deployment status in the cluster dashboard shows rollback in progress

steps.action: Monitor pod restarts, scaling events, and service endpoints during rollback
steps.verify: Pod status and service endpoints stabilize at expected configuration

steps.action: Check application logs and error rates for signs of recovery or continuing issues
steps.verify: Application logs and metrics indicate error rates are returning to normal

steps.action: Validate that all user-facing services are functional and meet baseline SLAs
steps.verify: Synthetic monitoring and end-user tests confirm service availability

steps.action: Communicate rollback completion and new deployment status to stakeholders
steps.verify: Stakeholders confirm receipt of rollback completion update

steps.action: Document the rollback process, outcomes, and lessons learned in the incident report
steps.verify: Incident report is updated and approved in the incident management system

abort_conditions: Manager approval for rollback is denied
Cluster health checks fail prior to rollback
Critical verification step fails or new incidents are detected during rollback

name: Quarterly Marketing Compliance Reporting
description: The marketing operations specialist prepares and submits the quarterly compliance report for all active campaigns. This includes collecting campaign data, verifying compliance with regulations, documenting findings, and submitting the final report for internal review. The process ensures all marketing activities adhere to company and legal standards.

invariants: No changes are made to live campaign configurations
Compliance assessments are based only on documented guidelines
Final report is stored in the designated docs_wiki folder
refusal_conditions: Required campaign list for the quarter is unavailable

steps.action: Access the latest list of active marketing campaigns for the quarter
steps.verify: Campaign list is current and complete in the documentation repository

steps.action: Collect campaign performance and compliance data from relevant marketing tools
steps.verify: All required data fields are populated and match source systems

steps.action: Review campaign content for adherence to company and regulatory guidelines
steps.verify: Compliance checklist is fully completed for each campaign

steps.action: Document any compliance incidents or exceptions found during the review
steps.verify: Incident log is updated and referenced in the draft report

steps.action: Summarize compliance status and findings for all campaigns
steps.verify: Summary section in the report accurately reflects findings

steps.action: Draft the quarterly compliance report in the documentation system
steps.verify: Draft report is saved and accessible in the docs_wiki

steps.action: Share the draft report with the marketing manager for review
steps.verify: Notification sent and report access granted to the manager

steps.action: Incorporate manager feedback and finalize the compliance report
steps.verify: Final report reflects all required changes and is marked as complete

steps.action: Archive the finalized compliance report in the designated docs_wiki folder
steps.verify: Report is stored in the correct location with proper access controls

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of report completion and location
steps.verify: Stakeholders confirm receipt of notification and access to the report

abort_conditions: Required campaign data is incomplete or unavailable
Compliance incident cannot be resolved or documented
Manager approval for report submission is denied

name: Handle Customer Escalation
description: A customer success manager addresses a high-priority customer escalation. This includes reviewing the ticket details, coordinating with support, and ensuring timely resolution with proper documentation. Data involved is confidential and actions must be traceable in the CRM.

steps.action: Acknowledge receipt of the escalation ticket in the CRM system
steps.verify: Ticket status updated to acknowledged and customer notified

checks: communication log contains an entry referencing ticket_id and template_id 'escalation_acknowledged_v1'

steps.action: Review all available ticket details, customer notes, and previous communications
steps.verify: Relevant ticket history and notes displayed in the CRM

checks: communication_history section is present (may be empty)

steps.action: Prioritize the escalation according to urgency and business impact
steps.verify: Ticket priority field set to high and visible in the CRM

steps.action: Assign dedicated support resources to the escalation
steps.verify: Assigned support agents and ownership reflected in the CRM

steps.action: Contact the customer to communicate next steps and expected timelines
steps.verify: CRM records updated with customer communication logs

checks: communication log contains new entry referencing ticket_id

steps.action: Coordinate with technical or product teams for required investigation or resolution steps
steps.verify: Internal collaboration notes and action items documented in the CRM

checks: internal note log contains new entry referencing ticket_id

steps.action: Monitor progress and ensure updates are provided at regular intervals in the CRM
steps.verify: Time-stamped progress updates visible in the escalation record

checks: progress log contains new entry referencing ticket_id

steps.action: Escalate internally to management for additional support or approval if required
steps.verify: Escalation request submitted and manager approval status recorded in the CRM

checks: escalation request exists referencing ticket_id and manager_id

steps.action: Review and validate the proposed resolution with support and technical teams
steps.verify: Resolution summary reviewed and documented in the CRM

checks: resolution summary entry exists referencing ticket_id

steps.action: Communicate the resolution to the customer and confirm satisfaction
steps.verify: Customer confirmation and feedback logged in the CRM

checks: communication log contains new entry referencing ticket_id
feedback field stored with one of allowed_values

steps.action: Close the escalation ticket and update resolution notes
steps.verify: Ticket status set to closed and resolution details saved

steps.action: Document key learnings and update internal knowledge base if applicable
steps.verify: Knowledge base article or internal note updated with escalation insights

checks: knowledge entry exists and references ticket_id

abort_conditions: Verification of customer communication fails
Manager approval for escalation is denied
CRM system is unavailable or data integrity cannot be confirmed

name: Project Status Update
description: The project manager prepares and submits a project status update for ongoing delivery work. This process includes gathering progress data, updating documentation, and notifying stakeholders through official channels.

steps.action: Collect the latest progress reports from team leads
steps.verify: All relevant reports are received and logged in the tracking document

checks: for each team_lead_id in list_of_team_lead_ids, tracking_document has a corresponding report entry

steps.action: Review current project milestones and deadlines
steps.verify: Milestone and deadline statuses are confirmed in the project tracker

checks: milestone_view is non_empty if project has milestones

steps.action: Summarize key accomplishments since the last update
steps.verify: Summary section is drafted and visible in the update document

checks: status_update_document.accomplishments content length is greater than 0

steps.action: Identify and list any current risks or blockers
steps.verify: Risk and blocker section is populated with up-to-date information

checks: status_update_document.risks_and_blockers content length is greater than 0

steps.action: Update the project status page on the docs wiki
steps.verify: Docs wiki page reflects the new status and timestamp

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of the updated status via official communication channel
steps.verify: Notification is sent and receipt is confirmed in the communication log

checks: for each stakeholder in stakeholder_list, communication_log has an entry referencing project_id
no more than length(stakeholder_list) log entries created

steps.action: Archive previous status update for recordkeeping
steps.verify: Previous status document is moved to the designated archive location

abort_conditions: Missing or incomplete progress reports
Unable to access docs wiki
Verification of update fails

name: Triage Production Alert
description: A site reliability engineer on call receives a high-urgency alert from the monitoring system affecting production. The engineer must triage the alert, assess impact, escalate as needed, and ensure system health is restored or stabilized. This process involves systematic data gathering, communication, and validation steps to resolve or mitigate the incident.

steps.action: Acknowledge the alert in the monitoring system
steps.verify: Alert status updated to acknowledged in dashboard

steps.action: Review alert details and associated metadata
steps.verify: Alert summary and metadata displayed in monitoring tool

steps.action: Check related dashboards for system and application health indicators
steps.verify: Dashboards display current metrics and trends

checks: for each dashboard_id in dashboard_ids, a dashboard_view exists

steps.action: Identify affected services and components
steps.verify: List of impacted services/components documented in incident log

steps.action: Assess alert severity and potential customer impact
steps.verify: Severity and impact assessment recorded in incident ticket

steps.action: Communicate initial findings to incident response channel
steps.verify: Incident channel contains update with findings

checks: incident_channel_message exists with channel_id and incident_ticket_id

steps.action: Correlate alert with recent changes or deployments
steps.verify: Change log or deployment history reviewed and noted

steps.action: Gather relevant logs and error traces for deeper analysis
steps.verify: Log and trace data attached to incident documentation

checks: incident_ticket has at least one log attachment
incident_ticket has at least one trace attachment

steps.action: Check for similar or related alerts in the recent past
steps.verify: History of related alerts reviewed and summarized

steps.action: Escalate to relevant engineering teams if root cause is outside SRE scope
steps.verify: Escalation documented and responsible parties notified

checks: escalation record exists referencing incident_ticket_id and target_team_id

steps.action: Monitor for alert recurrence or new correlated alerts
steps.verify: Ongoing alert status monitored and tracked in dashboard

steps.action: Update incident ticket with ongoing status and actions taken
steps.verify: Incident ticket reflects real-time status and actions

checks: incident_ticket.timeline has a new entry containing status_update_text

steps.action: Communicate status updates to stakeholders on defined intervals
steps.verify: Stakeholder communication logs updated

checks: for each stakeholder in stakeholder_list, communication_log has an entry referencing incident_ticket_id

steps.action: Confirm mitigation or resolution steps have restored system health
steps.verify: System and service metrics return to normal thresholds

checks: for each dashboard_id in health_dashboard_ids, metrics are within thresholds defined by normal_threshold_definition_id

steps.action: Document lessons learned and improvement actions
steps.verify: Post-incident documentation completed and shared

steps.action: Close the alert and incident ticket after verification
steps.verify: Incident ticket and alert status marked as resolved

abort_conditions: Verification of system health fails after mitigation
Incident ticket approval or required escalation is denied
Production environment is determined to be unsafe for further action

name: Restore Database from Backup After Incident
description: A site reliability engineer is tasked with restoring the production database from the most recent backup following a critical incident. This process requires careful validation of backup integrity and coordination with relevant teams to ensure a safe and compliant recovery. The operation is urgent and must comply with regulated data handling requirements.

steps.action: Acknowledge the incident alert and initiate the recovery process
steps.verify: Incident ticket status updated to show restoration in progress

steps.action: Notify relevant stakeholders about the planned database restoration
steps.verify: Notifications sent and receipt confirmed by stakeholders

checks: for each stakeholder in stakeholder_list, communication_log has an entry referencing incident_ticket_id

steps.action: Obtain two-person approval for destructive recovery actions
steps.verify: Approval documented in change management system

checks: both approver_user_id_1 and approver_user_id_2 in authorized_approvers_list

steps.action: Identify and select the most recent valid backup suitable for restoration
steps.verify: Backup selection documented and validated in backup inventory

checks: selected_backup_id exists in backup_inventory_id for database_id

steps.action: Validate backup integrity and completeness
steps.verify: Backup validation report reviewed and confirmed

steps.action: Isolate the affected database environment to prevent further changes
steps.verify: Database access logs show environment is isolated

checks: database access logs show no new write operations after isolation timestamp

steps.action: Communicate downtime window and expected impact to all affected teams
steps.verify: Downtime notice acknowledged by all teams

checks: for each team in affected_team_list, communication_log has an entry referencing incident_ticket_id

steps.action: Initiate the database restoration process from the selected backup
steps.verify: Restoration progress tracked and status updated in ticket

steps.action: Monitor system logs and metrics for errors or anomalies during restoration
steps.verify: No critical errors appear in real-time monitoring dashboards

steps.action: Validate data integrity and consistency after restoration completes
steps.verify: Data integrity checks and consistency reports indicate no discrepancies

steps.action: Coordinate with application owners to perform functional tests
steps.verify: Functional test results documented and reviewed

steps.action: Re-enable database environment for regular operations
steps.verify: Database access restored and confirmed operational

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of restoration completion and system status
steps.verify: Stakeholders confirm receipt of restoration completion notice

checks: for each stakeholder in stakeholder_list, communication_log has an entry referencing incident_ticket_id

steps.action: Document the entire restoration process and update incident records
steps.verify: Post-incident documentation reviewed and closed in ticketing system

abort_conditions: Backup integrity validation fails
Required approval is not granted
Unexpected errors threaten data safety during restoration

name: Perform Security Audit
description: A security engineer initiates and conducts a comprehensive security audit on identity and access management systems. The process involves reviewing current access policies, identifying potential vulnerabilities, compiling findings, and recommending improvements to maintain compliance and mitigate risk.

steps.action: Obtain formal approval to initiate the security audit
steps.verify: Approval is documented in the ticketing system

steps.action: Collect current access control policies from the IAM system
steps.verify: Access policy documents are retrieved and stored in the audit workspace

checks: audit_workspace has at least one policy document for iam_policy_scope

steps.action: Review user roles and permissions for excessive or outdated privileges
steps.verify: List of roles with excessive or outdated permissions is generated

checks: excessive_roles_list is generated (list may be empty)

steps.action: Analyze audit logs for suspicious access patterns over the past quarter
steps.verify: Summary report of anomalous access patterns is compiled

steps.action: Validate multi-factor authentication enforcement on privileged accounts
steps.verify: MFA status for all privileged accounts is included in the audit report

checks: mfa_status_report includes an entry for each account in privileged_account_scope

steps.action: Check for accounts that have not been used in over 90 days
steps.verify: Inactive account list is generated and reviewed

steps.action: Review service accounts for least privilege adherence
steps.verify: Service account privileges are mapped and deviations noted

steps.action: Verify that password policies meet compliance requirements
steps.verify: Password policy configuration is compared with compliance standards

steps.action: Inspect recent changes to IAM configuration for unauthorized modifications
steps.verify: Change log audit is completed and discrepancies highlighted

steps.action: Document findings and categorize them by risk level
steps.verify: Findings document includes risk categories for each issue

checks: each finding in findings_document has a risk_category assigned

steps.action: Draft recommendations for remediation of identified issues
steps.verify: Recommendations section is added to the audit report

steps.action: Share preliminary audit report with security team for peer review
steps.verify: Peer review feedback is received and documented

steps.action: Incorporate feedback and finalize the security audit report
steps.verify: Final report is approved by the security team

steps.action: Present final audit findings and recommendations to management
steps.verify: Management acknowledges receipt of the audit report

steps.action: Log the completion of the security audit in the compliance tracking system
steps.verify: Audit completion is recorded and timestamped

steps.action: Archive all audit artifacts securely for future reference
steps.verify: Audit artifacts are stored in an approved secure repository

abort_conditions: Approval to initiate audit is denied
Access to required audit data is unavailable
Evidence of active compromise is detected and escalated

name: Kubernetes Release Rollback
description: A site reliability engineer initiates a rollback of a recent Kubernetes release in the production environment in response to a critical incident. The process involves validating the current state, coordinating with stakeholders, executing the rollback, and verifying system stability post-rollback. This ensures service restoration to a known good state while following safe operational practices.

refusal_conditions: requested rollback target is not present in deployment history
requested rollback target is newer than current deployment
requested rollback target is marked as bad or blocked in change records

steps.action: Review the incident report and confirm the affected services and release version.
steps.verify: Incident ticket contains clear description and affected components are identified.
refusal_conditions: no affected services are listed in the incident ticket

steps.action: Notify relevant stakeholders about the planned rollback and expected impact.
steps.verify: Stakeholders acknowledge notification via incident communication channel.

steps.action: Obtain manager approval for the rollback as required by policy.
steps.verify: Approval is documented in the incident management system.

steps.action: Assess the current state of the Kubernetes cluster and health of critical workloads.
steps.verify: Cluster and workloads status is captured in monitoring dashboard.

steps.action: Identify the last known good deployment revision for the affected workloads.
steps.verify: Deployment history logs confirm valid rollback target.

steps.action: Prepare and stage the rollback plan, specifying target revisions and rollback steps.
steps.verify: Rollback plan is reviewed and documented in the incident ticket.

steps.action: Temporarily suspend automated deployment pipelines for the affected workloads.
steps.verify: Pipeline status reflects suspension and no new deployments are in progress.

steps.action: Initiate the rollback of workloads to the identified stable revision in the cluster.
steps.verify: Workload deployment status in cluster reflects rollback to intended revision.

steps.action: Monitor application and infrastructure metrics for errors or anomalies post-rollback.
steps.verify: Metrics dashboards show error rates and resource usage return to baseline.

steps.action: Perform functional checks to validate service health and customer impact resolution.
steps.verify: Health checks and synthetic transactions succeed as expected.

steps.action: Communicate rollback status and outcome to stakeholders.
steps.verify: Status update is logged in incident channel and acknowledged by stakeholders.

steps.action: Resume automated deployment pipelines for the affected workloads.
steps.verify: Pipeline status reflects resumed state and new deployments are possible.

steps.action: Document the rollback actions, observations, and lessons learned in the incident ticket.
steps.verify: Incident ticket is updated with a detailed post-rollback summary.

abort_conditions: Manager approval is denied or delayed beyond response window.
Verification of rollback target revision fails.
Cluster health check reveals unsafe environment for rollback.

name: Debug Production Issue
description: A senior backend developer investigates and troubleshoots an urgent production issue affecting the payments system. The process involves gathering evidence, analyzing logs and metrics, identifying the root cause, and coordinating with oncall and relevant teams to resolve the problem while minimizing risk to production data.

steps.action: Acknowledge the production issue alert and document initial impact assessment
steps.verify: Incident ticket is updated with acknowledgment and impact details

steps.action: Gather relevant logs from the monitoring system for affected services
steps.verify: Log entries are collected and attached to the incident ticket

steps.action: Review recent deployment and configuration changes related to the payments system
steps.verify: Change logs and deployment records are reviewed and findings noted

steps.action: Examine system metrics for anomalies (latency, error rates, resource utilization)
steps.verify: Metrics dashboard shows evidence of anomaly correlated to the incident

steps.action: Correlate user reports and support tickets with system events
steps.verify: Timeline of user impact is matched with system events in documentation

steps.action: Consult with oncall team members for additional context or recent issues
steps.verify: Oncall communication is logged in the incident ticket

steps.action: Document a clear problem statement based on the evidence collected
steps.verify: Incident ticket contains a concise problem statement

steps.action: Formulate a hypothesis for the root cause based on available data
steps.verify: Root cause hypothesis is documented in the incident ticket

steps.action: Validate hypothesis by cross-referencing additional logs or metrics
steps.verify: Supporting or contradicting evidence is recorded in the ticket

steps.action: Escalate to relevant engineering or SRE teams if outside backend scope
steps.verify: Escalation is reflected in ticket ownership or notifications

steps.action: Summarize findings and recommended remediation steps
steps.verify: Summary and recommendations are added to the incident ticket

steps.action: Communicate status and next steps to stakeholders
steps.verify: Stakeholder communication is logged in the incident record

abort_conditions: Verification of evidence fails to support a root cause
Access to required logs or metrics is denied
Production environment is deemed unsafe for investigation

name: Production IAM Security Audit
description: A security engineer conducts a comprehensive security audit of production IAM (Identity and Access Management) settings to ensure compliance with internal security policies and regulatory requirements. This includes reviewing access privileges, identifying potential risks, and recommending remediations. The process must be thorough, with every step documented and findings validated.

steps.action: Obtain formal approval from the security manager to begin the IAM security audit in production
steps.verify: Approval is documented in the ticketing system

steps.action: Review and confirm the scope of the audit based on current security policies and compliance requirements
steps.verify: Audit scope is documented and approved

steps.action: Collect a current inventory of all IAM users, groups, and roles in the production environment
steps.verify: Complete list is generated and stored securely

steps.action: Identify accounts with elevated privileges and document their intended business justification
steps.verify: List of privileged accounts and justifications is documented

steps.action: Analyze group and role memberships for excessive or anomalous permissions
steps.verify: Analysis results are recorded in the audit log

steps.action: Check for unused or stale IAM accounts and note accounts with no recent activity
steps.verify: Inactive accounts are listed in the audit findings

steps.action: Review service accounts and their permission scopes for compliance with least privilege principles
steps.verify: Service account permissions are documented and flagged if non-compliant

steps.action: Assess MFA (Multi-Factor Authentication) enforcement status for all applicable accounts
steps.verify: MFA status per account is reported in the findings

steps.action: Evaluate IAM policy documents for overly permissive or deprecated rules
steps.verify: Policy audit results are included in the report

steps.action: Cross-verify IAM settings against the most recent compliance checklist
steps.verify: Checklist compliance status is updated and stored

steps.action: Document all identified risks, vulnerabilities, and non-conformities with supporting evidence
steps.verify: Findings are detailed in the audit report

steps.action: Draft actionable remediation recommendations for each identified issue
steps.verify: Recommendations are documented alongside findings

steps.action: Present preliminary findings and recommendations to the security manager for review
steps.verify: Manager review is logged and feedback is received

steps.action: Incorporate manager feedback and finalize the IAM security audit report
steps.verify: Final report is approved and securely archived

steps.action: Communicate key findings and next steps to relevant stakeholders
steps.verify: Stakeholders acknowledge receipt of the summary

abort_conditions: Manager approval is not granted
Verification of scope or permissions fails
Detection of unsafe or unauthorized access during the audit

name: Scale Production Deployment
description: A site reliability engineer needs to scale up a production deployment in Kubernetes to handle increased traffic following an alert. This operation must be executed safely in a production environment, requiring careful monitoring and verification at each step to avoid service disruption.

steps.action: Review the alert details and impacted services
steps.verify: Incident management dashboard displays correct alert context and affected deployment

steps.action: Notify stakeholders about intended scaling operation
steps.verify: Notification is visible in relevant communication channels

steps.action: Assess current resource utilization for the target deployment
steps.verify: Resource dashboards show accurate CPU, memory, and replica counts

steps.action: Check for any ongoing related incidents or maintenance windows
steps.verify: Incident logs and maintenance schedules confirm no conflicting operations

steps.action: Validate current deployment health and readiness status
steps.verify: Deployment monitoring indicates all pods are healthy and ready

steps.action: Determine the new desired replica count based on traffic and resource analysis
steps.verify: Capacity planning documentation reflects the updated scaling requirement

steps.action: Update the deployment specification to reflect the new replica count
steps.verify: Deployment configuration in version control or dashboard shows intended replica value

steps.action: Apply the updated deployment configuration in the Kubernetes cluster
steps.verify: Kubernetes dashboard and monitoring tools display new replicas being created

steps.action: Monitor pod creation and startup events for errors or warnings
steps.verify: Pod events log shows successful pod scheduling and startup

steps.action: Verify new pods reach ready state and pass health checks
steps.verify: Readiness and liveness probes indicate all new pods are healthy

steps.action: Monitor service-level metrics for latency, error rates, and throughput
steps.verify: Service monitoring dashboards reflect improved or stable metrics post-scaling

steps.action: Confirm traffic is evenly distributed across all pods
steps.verify: Load balancing metrics show balanced request distribution

steps.action: Document the scaling action and observed outcomes in the incident record
steps.verify: Incident management system contains updated notes and resolution details

steps.action: Communicate completion of scaling operation to stakeholders
steps.verify: Stakeholders acknowledge receipt of the completion notification

name: Handle Customer Escalation
description: A customer support agent addresses a high-priority customer escalation by gathering all relevant ticket details, coordinating with internal teams, and ensuring the customer's issue is resolved and communicated clearly. The process is tracked and documented in the ticketing system to maintain accountability and transparency. The agent follows internal protocols to safeguard any confidential information.

steps.action: Review the escalated ticket in the ticketing system
steps.verify: Escalated ticket is visible and contains all initial details

steps.action: Acknowledge receipt of the escalation to the customer
steps.verify: Customer receives acknowledgment and it is logged in the ticket

steps.action: Gather all related communication and history from the ticket
steps.verify: All relevant correspondence and updates are attached to the escalation

steps.action: Identify the internal teams or stakeholders required for resolution
steps.verify: List of relevant stakeholders is included in the ticket

steps.action: Notify the assigned manager for escalation approval
steps.verify: Escalation approval request is logged and manager is notified

steps.action: Confirm manager approval before proceeding
steps.verify: Manager approval is recorded in the ticketing system

steps.action: Share the escalation details with internal teams
steps.verify: Internal teams confirm receipt of escalation details

steps.action: Monitor internal team responses and update the ticket accordingly
steps.verify: Ticket reflects ongoing internal communications and status

steps.action: Communicate progress updates to the customer at regular intervals
steps.verify: Customer receives timely updates and acknowledgments are logged

steps.action: Document all actions taken and responses received in the ticket
steps.verify: Ticket contains a complete activity log related to the escalation

steps.action: Verify that the customer’s issue has been fully resolved
steps.verify: Customer confirms resolution and satisfaction

steps.action: Close the escalation ticket and summarize the resolution
steps.verify: Ticket status is set to closed with a resolution summary

abort_conditions: Manager approval is denied
Ticket verification fails or required information is missing
Confidential information exposure risk is detected

name: Perform Release Validation
description: A QA engineer conducts a comprehensive validation of a new software release in the staging environment. The process ensures all test cases are executed, defects are documented, and release readiness is confirmed prior to deployment. This helps maintain product quality and reduces risk of regressions.

invariants: do_not_modify_production_resources
do_not_deploy_or_trigger_release_to_production
all_defects_found_must_be_logged_before_step_10_completion

steps.action: Review release notes and test plan for the upcoming deployment
steps.verify: Test plan and release notes are accessible and up-to-date in the documentation system

validation_criteria.documents_accessible: both release_notes_accessible and test_plan_accessible must be true

steps.action: Confirm staging environment is provisioned and reflects the intended release build
steps.verify: Staging environment build version matches release candidate in environment dashboard

steps.action: Execute automated regression test suite on the staging environment
steps.verify: Automated test suite results are available and all critical tests pass in CI/CD dashboard

steps.action: Perform manual exploratory testing based on risk areas identified in the release notes
steps.verify: Manual testing outcomes are documented and issues are logged in the ticketing system

steps.action: Validate user-facing features and workflows for expected functionality
steps.verify: Feature acceptance criteria are met and results are recorded in the test management tool

steps.action: Check integration points with dependent systems for compatibility
steps.verify: Integration tests complete successfully and results are visible in integration monitoring

steps.action: Log any defects or anomalies discovered during testing
steps.verify: Defect tickets are created and tracked in the ticketing system

steps.action: Retest resolved defects to confirm fixes
steps.verify: Defect status updated to indicate verified fix in ticketing system

steps.action: Update test documentation with any changes or new scenarios identified
steps.verify: Test documentation reflects current release scope in docs wiki

steps.action: Summarize test results and overall release readiness in a validation report
steps.verify: Validation report is uploaded and accessible to stakeholders in the documentation system

steps.action: Submit release validation report for manager approval
steps.verify: Manager approval is recorded and visible in the approval workflow tool

abort_conditions: Major defects are discovered that block critical functionality
Staging environment build does not match release candidate
Manager approval for release validation is denied

name: Rollback a Kubernetes Release in Production
description: The site reliability engineer is responding to an alert indicating degraded service after a recent deployment in the production Kubernetes cluster. The intent is to roll back the affected deployment to the previous stable version while ensuring service reliability and compliance with operational constraints. This process is governed by a two-person rule due to its potentially disruptive impact.

invariants: two_person_approval_must_be_recorded_before_step_6
rollback_must_target_only_single_identified_deployment
do_not_change_resource_limits_or_requests

steps.action: Acknowledge the alert and notify the incident response channel
steps.verify: Incident response channel shows notification and acknowledgment recorded

steps.action: Review recent deployment history and identify the affected release
steps.verify: Deployment logs and dashboard reflect the recent change and its impact

steps.action: Gather rollback approval from a second authorized engineer
steps.verify: Approval is documented in the incident management system

steps.action: Assess current system health and resource utilization in the cluster
steps.verify: Monitoring dashboard displays real-time health metrics and resource status

steps.action: Identify the previous stable version of the deployment
steps.verify: Release registry or deployment history confirms the stable version

steps.action: Initiate the rollback procedure for the affected deployment
steps.verify: Deployment status in the Kubernetes dashboard transitions to previous version

steps.action: Monitor pod restarts and readiness during rollback
steps.verify: Pod status indicators show all pods for the deployment are running and ready

steps.action: Check service endpoints to confirm traffic is directed to healthy pods
steps.verify: Load balancer or service metrics confirm healthy endpoints

steps.action: Validate core application functionality post-rollback
steps.verify: Application health checks and synthetic tests pass successfully

steps.action: Monitor for recurrence of alerts or errors after rollback
steps.verify: Alerting system shows no new critical alerts related to the deployment

steps.action: Document the rollback action and outcomes in the incident management system
steps.verify: Incident record contains rollback details and resolution notes

steps.action: Communicate rollback completion and status to stakeholders
steps.verify: Stakeholder notification channels confirm receipt of rollback update

abort_conditions: Approval from second authorized engineer is not obtained
System health check reveals unsafe cluster conditions
Rollback verification steps fail to restore service stability

name: Routine Data Pipeline Maintenance
description: Perform scheduled maintenance on production data pipelines to ensure reliability and data quality. This includes reviewing pipeline health, addressing minor issues, and documenting any changes. The process is conducted with read-only access to production systems for safety.

steps.verify: Log review shows recent runs are free from critical errors or unusual patterns.

steps.action: Validate the integrity of data outputs by comparing expected and actual row counts.
steps.verify: Row counts match expectations as documented in pipeline specifications.

steps.verify: Data quality dashboards show metrics within acceptable thresholds.

steps.action: Document any observed minor issues or maintenance needs in the team tracking system.
steps.verify: Tracking system contains up-to-date entries for all observed issues.

steps.action: Notify relevant team members about any findings that require follow-up or escalation.
steps.verify: Notification sent and visible in team communication channels.

steps.action: Update maintenance log with details of the review and any actions taken.
steps.verify: Maintenance log reflects the current review session and its outcomes.

abort_conditions: Verification step fails indicating pipeline health is at risk
Access to production database is unavailable
Observed issue requires write or administrative intervention

name: Contract Review for Vendor Renewal
description: A legal counsel is tasked with reviewing a vendor contract ahead of a renewal decision. The process involves assessing the document for compliance, identifying risks, and coordinating required approvals. This ensures the contract aligns with company policies and regulatory requirements before renewal.

invariants: all_edits_must_be_limited_to_internal_notes_or_annotations
original_contract_document_must_remain_unchanged

steps.action: Locate the current vendor contract in the centralized documentation system
steps.verify: Contract document is accessible and up-to-date in docs_wiki

steps.action: Read through the entire contract to identify key terms, obligations, and renewal clauses
steps.verify: All relevant sections are noted and summarized in review notes

steps.action: Check for compliance with internal company policies and regulatory standards
steps.verify: Compliance checklist is completed and attached to the contract review notes

steps.action: Highlight any deviations, risks, or ambiguous clauses within the contract

steps.action: Consult with relevant stakeholders (such as procurement or finance) for feedback on contract performance
steps.verify: Stakeholder comments are collected and documented

steps.action: Draft a summary report outlining findings, recommendations, and any required changes
steps.verify: Summary report is created and filed alongside the contract

steps.action: Submit the summary report and annotated contract to the manager for approval
steps.verify: Submission confirmation is logged in the docs_wiki or contract management system

steps.action: Respond to manager feedback and revise documentation as needed
steps.verify: Manager's comments are addressed and documented changes are made

steps.action: Record final approval or further action required in the contract review workflow
steps.verify: Approval status is updated and visible in the workflow system

steps.action: Notify procurement team of review outcome and next steps for vendor renewal
steps.verify: Notification is sent and acknowledged by procurement

abort_conditions: Contract document is missing or inaccessible
Manager approval is denied
Material compliance or legal risks are identified that cannot be resolved

name: Security Audit of Database Access
description: A security engineer conducts a comprehensive security audit focused on database access patterns and permissions. This process ensures that only authorized users have the appropriate level of access and identifies potential risks or policy violations. The audit includes review, documentation, and remediation steps to maintain compliance and minimize exposure.

invariants: all_operations_must_be_read_only_on_production_databases
any_required_permission_changes_must_be_requested_via_ticket_not_applied_directly

steps.action: Obtain manager approval for initiating the database access audit.
steps.verify: Approval record is available in the ticketing or approval system.

steps.action: Collect a list of all active user accounts with database access.
steps.verify: User access list is compiled and cross-checked with IAM records.

steps.action: Review current roles and permissions assigned to each user account.
steps.verify: Permission mappings are documented and match policy standards.

steps.action: Identify accounts with privileged or administrative access.
steps.verify: Privileged account inventory is complete and up-to-date.

steps.action: Analyze recent access logs for unusual or unauthorized access attempts.
steps.verify: Access anomalies are flagged in an audit report.

steps.action: Validate that inactive or terminated users do not retain access.
steps.verify: Inactive accounts are confirmed as disabled or removed.

steps.action: Check for compliance with least privilege and segregation of duties principles.
steps.verify: Compliance gaps are identified and documented.

steps.action: Verify that database access is logged and audit trails are intact.
steps.verify: Logging configuration and retention policies are reviewed and confirmed.

steps.action: Document all findings, risks, and recommended remediations.
steps.verify: Comprehensive audit report is prepared and reviewed.

steps.action: Share audit results with relevant stakeholders, including management and database owners.
steps.verify: Stakeholder acknowledgment or feedback is logged.

steps.action: Initiate remediation requests for identified issues through the appropriate ticketing system.
steps.verify: Remediation tickets are created and tracked.

steps.action: Schedule a follow-up review to confirm remediation actions are completed.
steps.verify: Follow-up date is set and documented.

steps.action: Update security documentation and audit logs with the outcomes of the audit.
steps.verify: Documentation reflects most recent audit and remediation status.

steps.action: Conduct a debrief with the security team to discuss lessons learned.
steps.verify: Debrief notes are captured in team knowledge base.

abort_conditions: Manager approval is not granted.

description: A sales operations specialist conducts a routine review of pipeline data in the CRM to ensure accuracy and completeness. name: Fulfill Production Access Request

name: Routine Network Maintenance
description: A site reliability engineer schedules and performs routine network maintenance on the production infrastructure to ensure reliability and uptime. The process includes planning, communication, execution of safe changes, and verification of network health. This task is performed with high trust and requires careful coordination to avoid service disruptions.

execution_constraints_detail.safe_write_definition: Only apply configuration changes explicitly listed in the maintenance plan document and already approved in change management system.

steps.action: Review the upcoming maintenance schedule and planned network changes
steps.verify: Maintenance window is confirmed on the shared calendar and documentation

Maintenance plan document missing required fields

steps.action: Notify all relevant teams about scheduled maintenance and expected impact
steps.verify: Notification is visible in team communication channels and acknowledged
refusal_conditions: Required communication channels are not accessible

steps.action: Check current network health metrics and baseline performance
steps.verify: Network monitoring dashboard shows all systems operational

steps.action: Confirm maintenance approvals and pre-checklist completion

Pre-change checklist is incomplete or missing

steps.verify: Connectivity tests show successful communication between nodes

steps.action: Monitor network latency and throughput during and after changes
steps.verify: Performance graphs remain within normal thresholds
refusal_conditions: Monitoring data unavailable for observation period

steps.action: Check security group and firewall rule integrity post-update
steps.verify: Access policies are consistent with approved configurations
refusal_conditions: Security configuration source of truth is not accessible

steps.action: Schedule follow-up review to assess any delayed effects

refusal_conditions: Calendar system cannot create new events

Unexpected service disruptions detected
Required approvals or pre-checklist are incomplete

steps.action: Analyze trends and variances compared to previous periods
steps.verify: Trend analysis outputs are reviewed and anomalies flagged
refusal_conditions: Data for comparison periods is not available

steps.action: Generate visualizations (charts, graphs) for key financial indicators
steps.verify: Visualizations are created and display correct metrics
refusal_conditions: Business intelligence tool cannot generate charts

steps.action: Compile the analysis and visualizations into a draft report
steps.verify: Draft report is assembled with all required sections

steps.action: Review the draft report for accuracy and clarity
steps.verify: Peer or manager review confirms accuracy and completeness
refusal_conditions: Required reviewer cannot access the draft report

steps.action: Finalize the report and store it in the designated confidential repository
steps.verify: Final report is uploaded and access is restricted to authorized users
refusal_conditions: Confidential repository cannot be written to

steps.action: Notify stakeholders that the financial performance report is available
steps.verify: Notification is sent and receipt is confirmed by stakeholders

abort_conditions: Verification fails at any data integrity or validation step
Unauthorized access or permissions error encountered
Data sensitivity or compliance concerns identified

name: Contract Review for Vendor Renewal
description: A legal counsel reviews a vendor contract in preparation for renewal. The process ensures the contract terms comply with company policies and legal requirements, and that any risks are identified and addressed before approval.

refusal_conditions: Requested contract is not a vendor renewal
Requested contract is outside legal counsel's assigned region or business unit

steps.action: Access the contract repository in the documentation system
steps.verify: Contract document is visible and accessible

steps.action: Locate the vendor contract scheduled for renewal
steps.verify: Correct contract file is identified

steps.action: Examine contract terms for compliance with current company policies
steps.verify: Compliance checklist is completed and documented

steps.action: Review legal obligations and liabilities outlined in the contract
steps.verify: Summary of obligations and liabilities is documented

steps.action: Check for updates in relevant laws or regulations since the last review
steps.verify: Notes on applicable legal changes are added to the review

steps.action: Identify any risks or ambiguities in contract language
steps.verify: Risk and ambiguity report is attached to contract file

steps.action: Consult with stakeholders for input on contract performance and issues
steps.verify: Stakeholder feedback is collected and logged

steps.action: Draft recommendations for contract amendments or approval
steps.verify: Recommendation document is created and linked to contract

steps.action: Submit reviewed contract and recommendations to manager for approval
steps.verify: Manager receives notification and access to the review package

steps.action: Document the review process in the contract management system
steps.verify: Review log is updated with timestamps and reviewer details

abort_conditions: Contract document is missing or inaccessible
Legal compliance cannot be verified
Manager approval is denied

name: Campaign Performance Review
description: The marketing operations team member reviews the performance of a recent marketing campaign. The intent is to analyze key metrics, update documentation, and prepare a summary for stakeholders. This process ensures that campaign insights are captured and shared for future planning.

refusal_conditions: Requested campaign is not yet completed

steps.action: Access the CRM platform and navigate to the campaign analytics dashboard.
steps.verify: Dashboard displays campaign data and metrics.

steps.action: Identify the target campaign by name and date range.
steps.verify: Campaign selection matches the intended review period.

steps.action: Review key performance indicators such as open rates, click-through rates, and conversions.
steps.verify: Metrics for the campaign are visible and accurate.

steps.action: Download or capture relevant reports and data visualizations.
steps.verify: Reports are exported or saved in the appropriate repository.

steps.action: Document notable trends, anomalies, or performance highlights in the campaign review document.
steps.verify: Review document is updated with new findings.

steps.action: Compare campaign performance to predefined goals or benchmarks.
steps.verify: Comparative analysis is present in the review notes.

steps.action: Summarize key insights and actionable recommendations.
steps.verify: Summary and recommendations are included in the report.

steps.action: Share the campaign performance review with relevant stakeholders via the appropriate communication channel.
steps.verify: Stakeholders confirm receipt of the review.

abort_conditions: Unable to access campaign data or analytics dashboard.
Metrics appear incomplete or inconsistent.
Required documentation or reports cannot be generated.

name: Execute Release Regression Suite
description: A QA engineer performs a full regression suite on the staging environment prior to a major release. The process ensures that new and existing features work as expected and no critical issues are present before deployment. The outcome is validated through updated test reports and documented results.

refusal_conditions: Release candidate build is not marked as ready_for_regression
Staging environment is not designated for this release

steps.action: Review the release candidate build notes and changelog for new features and bug fixes.
steps.verify: Release notes and changelog are available and up to date.

steps.action: Validate that the staging environment is operational and matches production configuration.
steps.verify: Staging environment health checks pass and configuration matches documented baseline.

steps.action: Select and schedule the full regression test suite in the continuous integration platform.
steps.verify: Test suite is scheduled and queued for execution in the platform dashboard.

steps.action: Execute automated regression tests covering all major product areas.
steps.verify: Automated test run completes with results available in the test reporting system.

steps.action: Manually execute key exploratory and edge case tests not covered by automation.
steps.verify: Manual test results are logged in the test management tool.

steps.action: Review all test failures and categorize them as new, known, or environment-related.
steps.verify: Test failure report is updated with categorizations and linked to relevant tickets.

steps.action: Document and escalate any critical or blocking defects according to severity.
steps.verify: Critical defects are logged as tickets and marked with appropriate severity in the tracking system.

steps.action: Retest resolved defects from prior cycles to confirm fixes.
steps.verify: Retest results are updated and defects are marked as verified or reopened as needed.

steps.action: Compile a summary report of regression results, including pass/fail rates and outstanding issues.
steps.verify: Regression summary report is published and shared with the release and development teams.

abort_conditions: Staging environment is unavailable or unstable.
Critical regression failures cannot be resolved or triaged.
Test data or configuration is incomplete or inconsistent.

name: Resolve Employee Access Issue
description: An IT admin assists an employee who is unable to access a required corporate system. The process involves validating the request, checking permissions, resolving the issue, and confirming access restoration. This ensures business continuity while maintaining compliance with internal security policies.

refusal_conditions: Request is not associated with a valid ticket

steps.action: Acknowledge and review the reported access issue ticket
steps.verify: Ticket status updated to 'in progress' and employee contact confirmed

steps.action: Validate the employee's identity using approved verification methods
steps.verify: Identity verification logged in the ticket

steps.action: Confirm the employee's current access rights in the identity management system
steps.verify: Access rights audit trail reviewed and documented

steps.action: Check recent changes or incidents affecting the employee's access
steps.verify: Change logs and incident records reviewed for relevant entries

steps.action: Consult with the employee's manager for approval to modify access
steps.verify: Manager approval received and logged in the ticket

steps.action: Update or correct the employee's access permissions as required
steps.verify: Access permissions updated and reflected in the IAM system

steps.action: Notify the employee to attempt access to the required system
steps.verify: Employee confirms successful access or provides feedback

steps.action: Document the resolution steps and outcomes in the ticketing system
steps.verify: Ticket includes detailed resolution notes and is marked as 'resolved'

steps.action: Inform the manager and relevant stakeholders of issue resolution
steps.verify: Manager and stakeholders acknowledge notification

abort_conditions: Identity verification fails
Manager approval is denied
IAM system is unavailable or unresponsive

name: Security Audit Access Review
description: A security engineer conducts a periodic access review as part of a security audit. The intent is to ensure that user privileges in the IAM system comply with policy and that any excessive or outdated access is identified and remediated. The review is performed with high urgency due to upcoming compliance deadlines and involves handling confidential data.

refusal_conditions: Requested review scope is outside assigned business_unit_or_region

steps.action: Initiate access review process in the IAM system
steps.verify: Access review session is logged and visible in audit dashboard

steps.action: Retrieve a list of all active users and their assigned roles
steps.verify: User-role mapping report is generated and available for analysis

steps.action: Export current access privileges for all users
steps.verify: Privileges export file is present and matches user records

steps.action: Compare user privileges against approved access policies
steps.verify: Discrepancies between actual and approved privileges are highlighted in the review report

steps.action: Identify users with excessive or outdated access rights
steps.verify: List of users with excessive privileges is created and validated

steps.action: Document findings of non-compliance and exceptions
steps.verify: Non-compliance report is completed and stored in audit trail

steps.action: Notify relevant managers of users with excessive access
steps.verify: Notification logs confirm delivery to appropriate managers

steps.action: Recommend remediation actions for non-compliant accounts
steps.verify: Remediation recommendations are attached to the audit documentation

steps.action: Review privileged access group memberships for accuracy
steps.verify: Membership audit results are recorded and any anomalies are flagged

steps.action: Summarize overall access compliance status
steps.verify: Summary report is generated and included in compliance dashboard

abort_conditions: Access to IAM system is unavailable
Verification of user-role mapping fails
Sensitive data exposure risk detected

name: Perform Release Regression Testing
description: A QA engineer needs to conduct regression testing on the staging environment as part of the release preparation process. This ensures new changes do not break existing functionality and that the system is stable before deployment. The process must follow staging constraints and validate key workflows.

preconditions: Staging environment URL and access credentials are available
Release notes and associated tickets exist in the tracking system
CI/CD system is reachable and operational
invariants: No changes are made to production environments
No test step modifies user data outside staging
All actions are logged in the respective systems (CI/CD, test management, issue tracker)
refusal_conditions: Requested action would modify any non-staging environment
Requested action would delete or anonymize real customer data
Required artifacts (release notes, tickets, test suites) are missing

steps.action: Review the release notes and associated tickets for the upcoming deployment
steps.verify: Release notes and tickets are accessible and up to date
inputs_required: Link or path to release notes document
Link or query to issue tracking system for associated tickets
expected_outputs: A locally stored list of ticket IDs associated with the release
A recorded timestamp confirming that release notes were accessed
validation_criteria: Release notes document exists and has a last_modified date within the last 7 days
At least one associated ticket is returned from the issue tracking system
No access errors are returned when opening release notes or tickets
guardrails: Do not edit or modify any release notes or tickets
Do not change ticket status or fields
Abort this step if any referenced document returns a permission error
refusal_conditions: Release notes document cannot be opened
Issue tracking query returns zero tickets for the provided release identifier

steps.action: Identify core workflows and test cases impacted by the release
steps.verify: A list of regression test cases is prepared and documented
inputs_required: List of ticket IDs from step 1
Access to test case repository or test management system
Predefined mapping between components/modules and test cases (if available)
expected_outputs: A documented list of regression test case IDs stored in the test management system or a designated document
Each test case in the list is tagged or labeled with the current release identifier
validation_criteria: The resulting list contains at least one test case
Each test case ID in the list exists in the test management system
No new test cases are created; only existing ones are referenced or tagged
guardrails: Do not delete or modify existing test case steps or expected results
Do not change ownership or priority of any test case
If mapping between tickets and test cases is not found, record this fact and proceed with a minimal set of smoke tests if defined
refusal_conditions: Test management system is not accessible
No test cases can be found for any of the components referenced in the tickets
User attempts to create new test cases instead of selecting existing ones

steps.action: Configure the staging environment to match the release candidate configuration
steps.verify: Staging environment settings match the release requirements
inputs_required: Release candidate build identifier or artifact reference
Configuration specification document for the release
expected_outputs: A recorded configuration snapshot of the staging environment after applying release settings
A log entry indicating the applied release candidate build identifier
validation_criteria: Staging environment reports the same build identifier as the release candidate
All configuration keys listed in the release specification match the values in staging
No configuration keys outside the allowed list are modified
guardrails: Apply only configuration values explicitly listed in the release specification
Do not modify environment variables not mentioned in the release specification
Abort this step if the target environment is detected as production or unknown

steps.action: Execute automated regression test suite using the CI/CD system
steps.verify: Automated test results are generated and logged in the CI/CD dashboard
inputs_required: CI/CD pipeline identifier for regression tests
List of regression test suites or groups to run
expected_outputs: A completed CI/CD pipeline run record with status (success/failure)
A test results artifact or dashboard entry with pass/fail counts
validation_criteria: Pipeline run reaches a terminal state (success or failure)
Test results artifact is generated and accessible
Number of executed tests is greater than zero
guardrails: Trigger only predefined regression pipelines; do not create or modify pipelines
Do not rerun pipelines automatically; each run must be explicitly triggered
Abort this step if the pipeline identifier is not recognized by the CI/CD system
refusal_conditions: Provided pipeline identifier does not exist
User attempts to modify pipeline configuration instead of triggering a run

steps.action: Manually test high-priority user flows not covered by automation
steps.verify: Manual test outcomes are documented in the test management system
inputs_required: List of high-priority user flows or scenarios
Access to staging environment application UI or APIs
Access to test management system for recording results
expected_outputs: A set of manual test execution records linked to the release identifier
Each record includes scenario identifier, steps executed, and pass/fail status
validation_criteria: Each high-priority flow in the input list has a corresponding execution record
Each execution record includes a binary outcome (pass or fail) with no partial statuses
All records are timestamped and associated with the tester identity
guardrails: Use only test accounts or anonymized data in staging
Do not modify configuration or deployment settings during manual testing
If a scenario cannot be executed, record the reason instead of skipping silently
refusal_conditions: Test management system does not allow writing new execution records
List of high-priority flows is empty

steps.action: Log any defects or unexpected behaviors found during testing
steps.verify: Defects are visible in the issue tracking system
inputs_required: List of failed automated tests from step 4
List of failed manual tests from step 5
expected_outputs: One issue record per distinct defect, created or updated in the issue tracking system
Each defect record includes steps to reproduce, expected result, actual result, and environment 'staging'
validation_criteria: Each failed test case is linked to at least one defect ID
All newly created defects are in an initial status such as 'Open' or equivalent
All defects are tagged with the current release identifier
guardrails: Do not close or resolve existing defects
Do not modify defects not related to this release
If a similar defect already exists, link the test failure to the existing defect instead of creating a duplicate
refusal_conditions: Issue tracking system is not accessible
User lacks permission to create or update issues
No failed tests are present but a defect creation is requested

steps.action: Re-run failed automated tests after defect fixes are applied
steps.verify: Test rerun results are updated in the CI/CD dashboard
inputs_required: List of failed automated test identifiers from step 4
CI/CD pipeline identifier or test suite identifier for reruns
List of defect IDs that have been marked as fixed
expected_outputs: A CI/CD pipeline run or targeted test run containing only previously failed tests or their suites
Updated test results showing new pass/fail status for each rerun test
validation_criteria: Only tests that previously failed are included in the rerun set
Each rerun test has a recorded result in the CI/CD dashboard
Rerun execution completes with a terminal status
guardrails: Do not rerun the entire regression suite; restrict to failed tests or their minimal suites
Do not modify test definitions or code
Abort this step if no failed tests are recorded from the previous run
refusal_conditions: List of failed tests is empty
User attempts to rerun tests more times than the configured max_reruns_per_test

steps.action: Validate that all critical workflows pass without regression
steps.verify: All core workflows are marked as passed in the test management tool
inputs_required: List of core workflows from step 2
Automated and manual test results associated with these workflows
expected_outputs: An updated status field for each core workflow indicating 'passed' or 'failed'
A summary record or tag indicating overall regression status for the release
validation_criteria: Each core workflow has at least one associated test result
No workflow is left without a status value
If any associated test failed, the workflow status is set to 'failed'
guardrails: Do not override a 'failed' status with 'passed' without a corresponding passing test result
Do not delete any historical test records
If test results are missing for a workflow, mark it as 'failed' and record the reason
refusal_conditions: Test management system is not accessible
User attempts to set a status value outside the allowed_status_values list

steps.action: Compile a summary report of regression test results and defects
steps.verify: Regression summary report is shared with the release and product teams
inputs_required: Automated test results from CI/CD system
Defect list from issue tracking system
expected_outputs: A summary report document stored in a predefined location (e.g., shared folder or documentation system)
The report includes sections for scope, test coverage, pass/fail statistics, defect overview, and release readiness recommendation
validation_criteria: Report file exists at the configured storage location
Report contains non-empty sections for test results and defects
Report is timestamped and labeled with the release identifier
guardrails: Do not include confidential production data in the report
Do not alter source test or defect records while compiling the report
If any required input data is missing, explicitly note this in the report
refusal_conditions: Report storage location is not writable
None of the test result or defect data sources are accessible
Release identifier is missing for labeling the report

abort_conditions: Staging environment is not available or misconfigured
Critical regression test fails and cannot be resolved
Release scope or tickets are incomplete

name: Onboard New Employee
description: The HR generalist initiates and completes the process of onboarding a new employee. This includes entering employee details, provisioning access, and ensuring compliance with company policies. The process is governed to protect confidential information and maintain regulatory requirements.

preconditions: A signed offer letter exists for the new employee
Hiring manager identity and department are defined
HRIS and related systems are reachable
invariants: Only the target employee's data is created or modified
No bulk operations are performed on existing employee records
All personal data fields are populated strictly from approved source documents
refusal_conditions: Requested action involves modifying records of employees other than the new hire
Requested action involves exporting or sharing bulk HR data
Regulatory-required fields are missing from the provided documentation

steps.action: Receive and review the approved offer letter and new hire documentation
steps.verify: Offer letter and documentation are present in the onboarding ticket
inputs_required: New hire documentation files (e.g., ID, tax forms, background check confirmation)
expected_outputs: A checklist in the onboarding ticket marking each required document as 'present' or 'missing'
A recorded confirmation that the offer letter status is 'approved'
