validation_criteria: Signed offer letter is attached to the onboarding ticket
Offer letter status field equals 'approved' or equivalent
All mandatory documents in required_documents_list are marked as 'present'
guardrails: Do not modify the content of any uploaded documents
Do not proceed to create an HRIS record if the offer letter is not approved
If any mandatory document is missing, record this in the ticket
refusal_conditions: Signed offer letter is not attached

steps.action: Create a new employee record in the HRIS system
steps.verify: New employee profile appears in the HRIS dashboard
inputs_required: Unique employee identifier or temporary ID
expected_outputs: A single new employee record in HRIS with a unique system-generated employee ID
The record is in an initial employment status such as 'Pre-boarding' or 'Active_on_start_date'
validation_criteria: Exactly one new employee record is created
The record is searchable by full legal name and start date
The record is linked to the correct hiring manager identifier
guardrails: Verify that no existing employee record with the same legal name and start date exists before creation
If a potential duplicate is found, stop and record the issue instead of creating a new record
Do not assign production admin roles during initial record creation
refusal_conditions: Mandatory fields (name, start date, manager) are missing
A matching active employee record already exists

steps.action: Input personal and employment details as provided
steps.verify: Employee details are complete and accurate in the HRIS
inputs_required: Personal details from approved documents (name, date of birth, address, contact info)
Employment details (job title, employment type, compensation data as allowed, location)
expected_outputs: All mandatory HRIS fields for the employee record are populated
Sensitive fields (e.g., SSN or national ID) are stored only in designated secure fields
validation_criteria: All mandatory fields defined by HRIS configuration are non-empty
Field formats (e.g., date, phone number) match system validation rules
No placeholder values such as 'TBD' or 'UNKNOWN' are used in mandatory fields
guardrails: Use only data from verified documents and approved internal sources
Do not copy data from unrelated employee records
If any required data is missing, flag the record as 'incomplete' and note missing fields
refusal_conditions: HRIS validation errors cannot be resolved with provided data
User attempts to bypass validation by entering dummy values
Sensitive identifiers are requested to be stored in non-secure fields

steps.action: Assign the employee to the correct department and reporting manager
steps.verify: Department and manager fields are populated in the employee profile
inputs_required: Employee HRIS record ID from step 2
expected_outputs: Employee record has department and manager fields populated with valid references
Organizational hierarchy view reflects the new reporting line
validation_criteria: Department identifier exists in the organizational structure
Manager employee ID refers to an active employee
Employee is not assigned to more than one primary department
guardrails: Do not create new departments or manager records
Do not assign the new employee as their own manager
If department or manager cannot be validated, leave fields unset and record the issue
refusal_conditions: Provided department identifier is not recognized
Manager record is inactive or missing
Employee HRIS record ID does not exist

steps.action: Trigger provisioning workflow for IT, facilities, and payroll access
steps.verify: Provisioning requests are logged and visible in the workflow tracker
inputs_required: Role or job family for access profile selection
expected_outputs: Provisioning tickets or workflow items created for IT, facilities, and payroll
Each provisioning item references the employee HRIS ID and start date
validation_criteria: At least one provisioning item exists for each of IT, facilities, and payroll
Each item is in an initial status such as 'Open' or 'Pending'
Each item includes the correct employee HRIS ID
guardrails: Use only predefined role-based access profiles
Do not manually assign system-specific permissions outside the workflow
If a provisioning category is not applicable, record the reason in the workflow system
refusal_conditions: Provisioning workflow system is not accessible
Employee HRIS record ID is missing or invalid
Requested access profile is not in the predefined list

steps.action: Send onboarding welcome email with relevant resources and instructions
steps.verify: Welcome email is sent and receipt confirmed by the new employee
inputs_required: New employee corporate email address (or personal email if corporate not yet active, as allowed by policy)
List of onboarding resources and links
expected_outputs: An email message sent to the new employee using the standard template
A log entry in the onboarding ticket or HRIS noting the send timestamp
validation_criteria: Email system reports a 'sent' status for the message
Email subject and body match the approved template except for allowed placeholders (name, start date, links)
A log entry exists referencing the email send event
guardrails: Do not include sensitive personal identifiers in the email body
Do not modify the approved template structure beyond placeholder substitution
If delivery failure is reported, record the failure and do not attempt more than two resend attempts
refusal_conditions: No valid email address is available
Approved onboarding email template cannot be retrieved

steps.action: Schedule mandatory training sessions in the calendar system
steps.verify: Training sessions are visible in the new employee's calendar
expected_outputs: Calendar events created for each mandatory training course
Each event includes title, time, location or link, and organizer
validation_criteria: Each mandatory training course has at least one corresponding calendar event
Events do not overlap in time for the same employee
Events are assigned to the correct employee calendar
guardrails: Use only approved training session time slots and resources
Do not modify or delete existing calendar events unrelated to onboarding
If a time conflict is detected, select the next available predefined slot

steps.action: Ensure all compliance and policy documents are assigned for review and signature
steps.verify: Document acknowledgment status is updated in the HRIS
inputs_required: List of required compliance and policy documents
Access to document acknowledgment or e-signature system
expected_outputs: Assignments created for each required document for the employee
HRIS or document system shows pending or completed status for each document
validation_criteria: Each required document is assigned exactly once to the employee
Each assignment is linked to the correct employee HRIS ID
Status values are one of 'not_assigned', 'pending', 'completed'
guardrails: Do not alter the list of required documents defined by compliance
If a document cannot be assigned, record the reason and notify HR compliance
Do not mark any document as 'completed' without system-confirmed acknowledgment
refusal_conditions: Required documents list is not available
Employee HRIS record ID is invalid

steps.action: Confirm completion of all onboarding steps with the hiring manager
steps.verify: Hiring manager acknowledges onboarding completion in the ticket
inputs_required: Onboarding checklist status from previous steps
expected_outputs: A summary of completed onboarding tasks sent to the hiring manager
An acknowledgment entry from the hiring manager recorded in the onboarding ticket
validation_criteria: All mandatory onboarding steps are marked as completed in the checklist before requesting acknowledgment
Hiring manager acknowledgment is present as a ticket comment or status change
Ticket status is updated to 'Onboarding Complete' or equivalent
guardrails: Do not close the onboarding ticket without explicit manager acknowledgment
If manager does not respond within a defined SLA, record the non-response but do not fabricate acknowledgment
Do not modify previous step records when requesting acknowledgment
refusal_conditions: Onboarding checklist shows incomplete mandatory steps
Hiring manager contact information is missing

abort_conditions: Offer letter or required documentation is missing or invalid
Manager approval for onboarding is denied
System verification fails at any onboarding step

name: Monthly Billing Data Audit
description: The finance analyst conducts a monthly audit of billing data to ensure accuracy and identify any discrepancies. The process involves reviewing data exports, reconciling with financial records, and preparing a summary report for management review. This helps maintain data integrity for compliance and reporting purposes.

preconditions: Month-end period to be audited is defined
Read-only access to billing and financial systems is granted
invariants: No write operations are performed on any finance or billing data source
All exports are stored in approved secure locations
All calculations are reproducible from stored exports
refusal_conditions: Requested action involves modifying billing or financial records
Requested action involves exporting data to unapproved locations
Requested action involves using personal devices or storage for confidential data

steps.action: Log into the finance systems with read-only access
steps.verify: Access to required billing data tables is confirmed
inputs_required: Target audit period (start and end dates)
expected_outputs: Established read-only sessions to billing and financial systems
Confirmation that required schemas and tables are visible
validation_criteria: Connection tests to each required system succeed
User permissions are verified as read-only by system metadata or role
Required tables or views for the audit period are listed
guardrails: Do not store credentials in plain text in any export or report
Do not attempt any write operation even if technically permitted
If access is not read-only, stop and record the issue
refusal_conditions: Connection to any required system fails
User role is not restricted to read-only
Required tables or views are not present

steps.action: Export billing transactions for the current month
steps.verify: Exported data file is generated and accessible
inputs_required: Query or export procedure for the target month
Approved secure storage location for exports
expected_outputs: A data export file (e.g., CSV or parquet) containing all billing transactions for the month
Metadata log including export timestamp, row count, and query used
validation_criteria: Export file exists in the secure storage location
Row count in the export matches the count from a direct database count query
Export covers the full date range for the month with no gaps
guardrails: Do not export data to local or unencrypted storage
Do not include unnecessary personally identifiable information beyond what is required for reconciliation
If export exceeds max_rows, split into multiple files with clear naming
refusal_conditions: Secure storage location is not writable
Export query fails or returns zero rows unexpectedly
Export file format is not one of the allowed formats

steps.action: Retrieve reference financial records for the same period
steps.verify: Reference records are downloaded and available for comparison
inputs_required: Report or query definition for the same month
Secure storage location for reference records
expected_outputs: A reference report or data file with financial totals for the month
Metadata log including report parameters, timestamp, and totals
validation_criteria: Reference file exists in secure storage
Report period matches the billing export period exactly
Report includes total billing amount and count of transactions or invoices
guardrails: Use only approved standard financial reports for reference
Do not modify or recalculate values inside the reference report
If multiple versions of the report exist, select the one labeled as final or approved
refusal_conditions: Financial reporting system is not accessible
Report for the specified period is not available
Reference file cannot be stored in secure storage

steps.action: Compare transaction totals between billing export and financial records
steps.verify: Reconciliation summary shows matching or flagged discrepancies
expected_outputs: A reconciliation summary file containing total amounts and counts from both sources
A computed difference for each metric (amount and count)
validation_criteria: Totals from billing export are computed deterministically using sum and count operations
Totals from reference report are extracted without modification
Differences are calculated as billing_total minus reference_total
guardrails: Do not adjust or normalize source data during comparison
Do not change tolerance thresholds; treat any non-zero difference as a discrepancy
If any metric cannot be computed, mark it explicitly as 'not_available'
refusal_conditions: Either input file is missing or unreadable
Totals cannot be computed due to format issues
Reconciliation summary cannot be written to secure storage

steps.action: Identify any outlier transactions or unexpected variances
steps.verify: List of anomalies is compiled for further review
inputs_required: Defined rules or thresholds for outliers (e.g., amount > X, negative amounts, unusual tax rates)
expected_outputs: An anomalies file listing all transactions that meet any outlier rule
Each anomaly record includes transaction ID, reason code, and rule triggered
validation_criteria: Each anomaly in the output corresponds to a transaction in the export file
Each anomaly has at least one associated rule identifier
The number of anomalies does not exceed max_anomalies; if it does, processing is stopped and flagged
guardrails: Use only predefined, documented rules for anomaly detection
Do not manually exclude anomalies from the output list
If no rules are provided, record that anomaly detection was not performed
refusal_conditions: Outlier rules are not available or are ambiguous
Anomalies file cannot be stored in secure storage

steps.action: Document findings and any discrepancies in an audit log
steps.verify: Audit log is updated and saved in the designated location
inputs_required: Defined audit log template or schema
expected_outputs: An audit log entry or file containing reconciliation results, anomalies summary, and timestamp
Each discrepancy is recorded with type, magnitude, and reference IDs
validation_criteria: Audit log entry includes the audit period, analyst identifier, and creation timestamp
All discrepancies from reconciliation and anomaly detection are referenced
Audit log is stored in the designated audit_log_repository path
guardrails: Do not alter or delete previous audit log entries
Do not include raw personal identifiers beyond what is required for traceability
If any input artifact is missing, note this explicitly in the log
refusal_conditions: Audit log repository is not writable
Audit log template or schema is missing
Required input artifacts (reconciliation summary or anomalies list) are missing

steps.action: Notify relevant stakeholders of any significant discrepancies
steps.verify: Stakeholder notifications are sent and acknowledged
inputs_required: List of stakeholder email addresses or distribution lists
expected_outputs: Notification messages sent to all listed stakeholders
A record of notifications and any acknowledgments stored in the audit log or tracking system
validation_criteria: If discrepancies exceed the defined significance threshold, at least one notification is sent
Notification content references the audit period and location of the audit log
Notification send status is recorded for each stakeholder
guardrails: Do not include full raw data exports in the notification body
Use only approved distribution lists for finance and compliance stakeholders
If acknowledgments are not received within the defined SLA, record the non-acknowledgment but do not resend indefinitely
refusal_conditions: Stakeholder list is empty when significant discrepancies exist

steps.action: Prepare a summary report of the audit process and findings
steps.verify: Summary report is reviewed and finalized
expected_outputs: A summary report document describing scope, methods, discrepancies, and conclusions
Report stored in a designated management reporting location
validation_criteria: Report includes sections for methodology, reconciliation results, anomalies, and next steps
Report references the audit period and all key input artifacts by filename or ID
Report file is accessible to management with read-only permissions
guardrails: Do not alter underlying audit log or data exports while preparing the report
Do not include unnecessary personally identifiable information
If any section cannot be completed due to missing data, explicitly mark it as incomplete
refusal_conditions: Report storage location is not writable

steps.action: Submit the summary report to management for review
steps.verify: Submission is confirmed and receipt is acknowledged
inputs_required: Management distribution list or designated recipients
Submission channel (e.g., email, document workflow system)
expected_outputs: Report submitted via the designated channel to management
Receipt or acknowledgment recorded in the audit log or workflow system
validation_criteria: Submission event is logged with timestamp and recipients
At least one acknowledgment or system receipt is recorded
Report version submitted matches the finalized version from step 8
guardrails: Do not modify the report content after submission without creating a new version
Do not send the report to recipients outside the approved management list
If acknowledgment is not received within the defined SLA, record this fact but do not alter the report
refusal_conditions: Summary report file is missing or unreadable

abort_conditions: Required data exports are unavailable or incomplete
Significant discrepancies cannot be reconciled
Access to finance systems is denied

name: Update Project Status Report
description: The project manager needs to update the status of an ongoing project in the project management system. This involves collecting the latest progress from team members, documenting risks, and ensuring the project status is accurately reflected for stakeholders. The update must be performed in a read-only access context for governance.

preconditions: Access to project dashboard and docs wiki is available
invariants: No changes are made to underlying task or issue data in project tracking tools
Only documentation artifacts (status reports) are created or updated
All status updates are traceable to source information from dashboards and team updates
refusal_conditions: Requested action involves changing task assignments or dates in the project tracking system
Requested action involves deleting existing status reports
Requested action involves editing documents outside the designated project space

steps.action: Review the latest project milestones and deliverables from the team dashboard
steps.verify: Milestone completion status is visible and up to date on the dashboard
inputs_required: URL or access path to the team dashboard
expected_outputs: A captured snapshot or notes of current milestone statuses and key deliverables
List of milestones with their current completion percentage or status
validation_criteria: Dashboard is accessible and loads without error
Milestone list is non-empty for the project
Each milestone has a status or completion indicator visible
guardrails: Do not modify any data in the dashboard system
If dashboard data appears stale (e.g., last updated date older than 14 days), record this fact in notes
Do not infer milestone completion beyond what is explicitly shown
refusal_conditions: No milestones are defined for the project

steps.action: Collect progress updates from key team members via official communication channels
steps.verify: All assigned team members have submitted their progress updates
inputs_required: List of key team member identifiers
Approved communication channel (e.g., email, chat, form)
Standard progress update template or questions
expected_outputs: A set of progress update messages or forms from each key team member
A consolidated record mapping each team member to their latest update
validation_criteria: Each key team member has at least one update recorded for the current reporting period
Updates are timestamped within the current reporting window
Updates are stored in a retrievable format
guardrails: Use only official channels defined by the organization
Do not alter the content of team member updates
If a team member does not respond, record 'no update received' instead of fabricating content
refusal_conditions: Standard update template is not available

steps.action: Summarize current project status including completed, ongoing, and upcoming tasks
steps.verify: A draft summary document is created and accessible in the docs wiki
inputs_required: Milestone and deliverable snapshot from step 1
expected_outputs: A draft status summary document in the docs wiki under the project space
Sections for completed work, in-progress work, and planned next steps
validation_criteria: Document is created or updated in the correct project space
All three sections (completed, ongoing, upcoming) contain at least one entry or an explicit 'none' statement
Document includes the reporting period and project identifier
guardrails: Base the summary only on information from the dashboard and team updates
Do not include speculative or unconfirmed information
If previous status exists, clearly label this as a new version with the current date
refusal_conditions: Project space in docs wiki does not exist
Required input information from steps 1 or 2 is missing

steps.action: Document any project risks or issues raised during the period
steps.verify: Risk and issue section in the status report contains new entries as reported
inputs_required: Existing risk and issue log (if any)
expected_outputs: An updated risk and issue section in the status report document
Each risk/issue entry includes description, owner, and status
validation_criteria: Each risk or issue mentioned in team updates is represented in the section or explicitly noted as 'not included' with reason
Each entry has a status from allowed_status_values
Each entry is timestamped or associated with the current reporting period
guardrails: Do not change the status of risks/issues beyond what is reported by owners
Do not delete historical risk/issue entries; append new information instead
If a risk or issue is unclear, record it with a note indicating 'details pending'
refusal_conditions: Risk and issue section cannot be edited due to permissions
Allowed status values are not recognized in the template

steps.action: Update project timeline to reflect any changes in schedule or deliverables
steps.verify: Timeline section in the report matches the latest agreed project plan
inputs_required: Current official project plan or schedule baseline
Any schedule-related updates from team members or governance forums
Existing timeline section in the status report
expected_outputs: An updated timeline section in the status report summarizing key dates and changes
Notes indicating any slippages or accelerations relative to baseline
validation_criteria: Timeline entries in the report reference dates from the official project plan
Any deviations from baseline are explicitly labeled as such
Timeline section includes at least current phase, next milestone, and expected completion date
guardrails: Do not modify the baseline plan in the project management tool; only document changes in the report
If no official change approval exists for a schedule change, mark it as 'proposed' rather than 'approved'
Do not infer new dates without explicit source information
refusal_conditions: Official project plan is not accessible
Docs wiki timeline section cannot be edited
No schedule information is available from any source

steps.action: Add relevant metrics or KPIs illustrating project progress
steps.verify: Metrics/KPIs section in the status report displays up-to-date data
inputs_required: Defined list of project KPIs (e.g., burn-down, velocity, budget usage)
Source dashboards or reports for each KPI
expected_outputs: A metrics/KPIs section in the status report with current values and, where applicable, comparison to previous period
Each KPI entry includes name, value, date, and data source
validation_criteria: Each KPI in the defined list is either populated with a value or explicitly marked as 'not available'
Values are copied exactly from source dashboards without modification
Metrics section includes the date/time of data extraction
guardrails: Do not calculate new KPIs beyond the defined list
Do not adjust or normalize KPI values manually
If a KPI source is unavailable, record the unavailability instead of estimating values
refusal_conditions: Docs wiki metrics section cannot be edited

steps.action: Request peer review of the status update from another project manager
steps.verify: Peer review comment is present on the status report in the docs wiki
expected_outputs: A review request message sent to the peer project manager
At least one comment or review acknowledgment added to the status report
validation_criteria: Review request references the correct status report and reporting period
Peer review comment or acknowledgment is visible on the report
Comment timestamp is after the review request timestamp
guardrails: Do not edit or remove peer review comments
Use only designated peer reviewers as defined by PMO
If no review is received within the defined SLA, record 'peer review pending or not received'
refusal_conditions: Docs wiki commenting is disabled or unavailable
No peer project manager is identified

steps.action: Publish the updated project status report in the docs wiki
steps.verify: Status report is visible in the designated project documentation space
inputs_required: Version or date label for the report
expected_outputs: A finalized status report document marked as published in the project space
Previous versions retained or archived according to documentation policy
validation_criteria: Report is accessible via the project documentation index
Report is labeled with the correct project identifier and reporting period
Publication state is set to 'published'
guardrails: Do not delete or overwrite previous status reports; create a new version or entry
Ensure that any sensitive internal notes not intended for stakeholders are removed before publication
If peer review from step 7 is missing, note this in the report metadata
refusal_conditions: Docs wiki project space is not writable
Draft report is missing or incomplete
Publication state cannot be set correctly

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of the updated project status report via official communication channel
steps.verify: Stakeholder notification is sent and receipt is confirmed
expected_outputs: Notification messages sent to all stakeholders in the distribution list
At least one confirmation or system delivery receipt recorded
validation_criteria: Notification includes the report link, project identifier, and reporting period
Send status is 'sent' or equivalent for each recipient
At least one delivery receipt or acknowledgment is stored
guardrails: Use only approved stakeholder lists maintained by PMO
Do not attach the full report if policy requires link-only sharing
If some recipients fail delivery, record the failures but do not resend more than once
refusal_conditions: Stakeholder list is empty or undefined
Published report link is invalid or inaccessible

abort_conditions: Team member progress updates are incomplete
Peer review is not completed or raises blocking issues
Publishing fails or report is not visible in the docs wiki

name: Routine Data Pipeline Maintenance
description: A data engineer performs scheduled maintenance on production data pipelines to ensure consistent data flow and reliability. Tasks include reviewing recent pipeline runs, checking for anomalies, and updating documentation. The process follows read-only constraints and is aimed at early detection of potential issues.

preconditions: Access to production monitoring dashboards is granted with read-only permissions
List of pipelines in scope for maintenance is defined
invariants: No changes are made to pipeline configurations or schedules
No changes are made to production data or schemas
All observations are recorded without altering operational systems
refusal_conditions: Requested action involves modifying pipeline configurations or schedules
Requested action involves rerunning or stopping pipelines
Requested action involves changing data in production systems

steps.action: Review the status dashboard for all scheduled data pipelines
steps.verify: All pipelines display recent successful runs and no critical errors
inputs_required: List of pipeline identifiers in scope
URL or access path to the pipeline status dashboard
expected_outputs: A captured view or record of latest run status for each pipeline
A list of pipelines with their last run time and status
validation_criteria: Dashboard is accessible and loads without error
Each pipeline in scope has at least one recent run entry
Status for each pipeline is recorded as reported (e.g., success, failed, running)
guardrails: Do not trigger any pipeline runs from the dashboard
Do not change any alert thresholds or dashboard settings
If a pipeline has no recent runs, record this explicitly as an observation
refusal_conditions: Pipeline list is empty or missing

steps.action: Check logs for any warnings or non-critical errors in the last 7 days
steps.verify: Log review shows only expected informational or minor warning messages
inputs_required: Log system access with read-only permissions
List of pipelines or services to filter logs
expected_outputs: A filtered log view or export containing warnings and non-critical errors for the specified period
A summary of log message counts by severity and pipeline
validation_criteria: Log queries return results for the specified time window
Severity levels are correctly identified (e.g., INFO, WARN, ERROR)
If log count exceeds max_log_entries, sampling or summarization is applied and noted
guardrails: Do not change log retention or configuration settings
Do not acknowledge or close alerts from the logging system
If no logs are found for a pipeline, record this as a potential issue
refusal_conditions: Log query returns errors or incomplete data

steps.action: Compare pipeline runtime durations against historical baselines
steps.verify: Observed runtimes are within normal range and no significant deviations are present
inputs_required: Recent runtime metrics for each pipeline
Historical baseline metrics (e.g., average and standard deviation over past N runs)
expected_outputs: A comparison table listing current runtime, baseline, and deviation for each pipeline
A list of pipelines whose runtime deviation exceeds the defined threshold
validation_criteria: Baseline metrics are computed or retrieved consistently for all pipelines
Deviation is calculated as percentage difference between current and baseline
Pipelines exceeding deviation_threshold_percent are correctly flagged
guardrails: Do not adjust baselines during this maintenance process
Do not modify alerting rules based on deviations
If baseline data is missing for a pipeline, mark deviation as 'not_available'
refusal_conditions: Baseline metrics are unavailable for all pipelines

steps.action: Validate data freshness metrics for key tables and outputs
steps.verify: Metrics confirm data is up to date within expected timeframes
inputs_required: List of key tables and outputs with expected freshness thresholds
Data freshness metrics from monitoring or metadata system
expected_outputs: A table listing each key table/output, last updated time, and freshness status (within_threshold or out_of_threshold)
A list of any items that are out_of_threshold
validation_criteria: Each key table/output has a recorded last updated timestamp
Freshness status is computed by comparing last updated time to expected threshold
Items exceeding threshold are clearly flagged
guardrails: Do not change freshness thresholds or monitoring configuration
Do not trigger backfills or manual data loads
If metrics are missing for an item, mark status as 'unknown' and record this
refusal_conditions: Metadata or monitoring system is not accessible
List of key tables/outputs is missing

steps.action: Inspect recent schema changes or data model updates
steps.verify: Change logs match approved modifications and no unauthorized changes are detected
inputs_required: Schema change logs or migration history
List of approved schema changes for the period
List of pipelines and tables in scope
expected_outputs: A comparison record showing which schema changes occurred and whether they are approved
A list of any schema changes not found in the approved list
validation_criteria: Each detected schema change is cross-referenced with the approved change list
Unauthorized or unexpected changes are clearly identified
No modifications are made to schema or change records
guardrails: Do not apply or roll back any schema changes
Do not modify change management tickets or approvals
If change logs are incomplete, record this limitation in the findings
refusal_conditions: Schema registry or change logs are not accessible
Approved change list for the period is missing
Scope of pipelines and tables cannot be determined

steps.action: Check for any failed or retried pipeline jobs and document findings
steps.verify: Failures and retries are logged with explanations or linked to pre-approved exceptions
inputs_required: Pipeline run history for the maintenance period
List of known exceptions or accepted failure patterns
expected_outputs: A list of failed or retried jobs with timestamps, error messages, and pipeline IDs
Indication for each whether it is covered by a known exception
validation_criteria: All failed or retried jobs in the period are included in the list
Each job entry includes at least pipeline ID, run ID, and status
Known exceptions are correctly matched based on predefined rules
guardrails: Do not rerun or cancel any jobs from the monitoring interface
Do not close or acknowledge alerts related to these failures
If an error cannot be classified, mark it as 'unclassified' and record details
refusal_conditions: Monitoring or logging systems are not accessible
Run history for the period is incomplete

steps.action: Review monitoring alerts or notifications related to pipeline health
steps.verify: No unresolved alerts are present; all have been acknowledged or closed
inputs_required: Alerting system access with read-only permissions
List of alert rules relevant to the pipelines in scope
expected_outputs: A list of alerts triggered during the maintenance period with status and timestamps
A subset list of alerts that remain unresolved at the time of review
validation_criteria: Alerts list covers the full maintenance period
Each alert has a status such as 'open', 'acknowledged', or 'closed'
Unresolved alerts are clearly identified
guardrails: Do not change alert statuses or configurations
Do not disable or mute any alerts
If alert data is incomplete, record this limitation in the findings
refusal_conditions: Alert query for the period fails

steps.action: Update pipeline maintenance documentation with recent findings and actions taken
steps.verify: Documentation reflects current pipeline status and any notable observations
inputs_required: Outputs from steps 1 through 7 (status snapshot, logs summary, runtime comparison, freshness report, schema change comparison, failure list, alerts list)
expected_outputs: An updated maintenance log or document summarizing observations for the maintenance period
Sections for status overview, anomalies, risks, and recommended follow-ups
validation_criteria: Documentation entry includes the maintenance date, pipelines in scope, and engineer identifier
All notable findings from previous steps are referenced or summarized
No configuration or operational commands are included in the documentation
guardrails: Do not include credentials or sensitive operational details in the documentation
Do not instruct readers to perform unapproved changes; limit to observations and recommended follow-ups
If any step could not be completed, explicitly note this with reasons
refusal_conditions: Outputs from previous steps are missing or incomplete

abort_conditions: Unable to access pipeline monitoring dashboards
Critical errors or data quality issues are detected
Verification of key metrics fails

name: Pipeline Data Quality Review
description: Review and ensure the quality of sales pipeline data in the CRM system as part of a routine maintenance cycle. The sales operations user will check for inconsistencies, update missing information, and document improvements to maintain accurate sales reporting. This supports reliable forecasting and decision-making for the sales team.

steps.action: Access the CRM system and navigate to the sales pipeline module
steps.verify: CRM dashboard displays current pipeline data

steps.action: Export the latest pipeline data for review
steps.verify: Exported data file is available and complete

steps.action: Identify incomplete or inconsistent records in the pipeline
steps.verify: Report of data inconsistencies is generated

steps.action: Notify relevant sales representatives about missing or unclear data
steps.verify: Notification logs are updated and recipients are listed

steps.action: Collect updated information from sales representatives
steps.verify: Updated data is received and logged

steps.action: Update the CRM pipeline records with the corrected information
steps.verify: CRM records reflect the latest, corrected data

steps.action: Review recently updated records for accuracy and completeness
steps.verify: Spot check confirms accurate updates in CRM

steps.action: Document changes and improvements made during the review
steps.verify: Change log or documentation is updated and accessible

steps.action: Communicate summary of data quality improvements to sales leadership
steps.verify: Summary report is sent and receipt is acknowledged

abort_conditions: Verification of data export fails
CRM access is unavailable
Significant data discrepancies cannot be resolved

name: Production IAM Security Audit
description: A security engineer conducts a comprehensive audit of Identity and Access Management (IAM) permissions within production environments. The goal is to identify excessive privileges, misconfigurations, and ensure compliance with security policies. This process is performed with high urgency due to a recent compliance requirement.

steps.action: Review current IAM user and role inventory in the production environment
steps.verify: Inventory report is generated and accessible

steps.action: Identify accounts with elevated privileges such as admin or write access
steps.verify: List of privileged accounts is documented

steps.action: Cross-reference privileged accounts with approved access lists
steps.verify: Discrepancies between privileges and approved lists are noted

steps.action: Check for inactive or unused IAM accounts in production
steps.verify: Inactive account list is compiled

steps.action: Analyze access policies for overly permissive rules or wildcards
steps.verify: Summary of permissive policies is available

steps.action: Assess multi-factor authentication enforcement for all privileged accounts
steps.verify: MFA compliance report is reviewed

steps.action: Evaluate recent changes to IAM permissions for unauthorized modifications
steps.verify: Change logs are reviewed for anomalies

steps.action: Examine group memberships for excessive privilege aggregation
steps.verify: Group membership analysis is completed

steps.action: Validate access review attestation records for compliance requirements
steps.verify: Attestation records are matched with audit criteria

steps.action: Inspect external integrations for least privilege adherence
steps.verify: External integration permissions are summarized

steps.action: Document all identified risks, misconfigurations, and policy violations
steps.verify: Risk register is updated with new findings

steps.action: Draft remediation recommendations for each identified issue
steps.verify: Remediation plan is attached to audit documentation

steps.action: Submit audit findings and recommendations for manager approval
steps.verify: Submission confirmation is received

steps.action: Schedule follow-up session to review remediation progress
steps.verify: Calendar invite is sent to relevant stakeholders

steps.action: Archive audit documentation according to security policy
steps.verify: Audit documentation is stored in approved secure location

abort_conditions: Manager approval is denied
Verification fails at any audit step
Access to required IAM data is unavailable

name: Scale Application Deployment in Production
description: A site reliability engineer on the SRE team needs to scale up a production application deployment in Kubernetes in response to increased load, as part of responding to a high-priority alert. The operation must be performed safely, with appropriate checks to avoid disruption of live traffic.

steps.action: Review the incoming alert and identify the affected deployment and namespace.
steps.verify: Alert documentation and monitoring dashboard confirm the target deployment and namespace.

steps.action: Check current deployment status and replica count for the affected application.
steps.verify: Deployment status and replica count are visible in the cluster management dashboard.

steps.action: Assess current resource usage and cluster capacity for scaling feasibility.
steps.verify: Resource utilization metrics show available headroom for scaling.

steps.action: Notify relevant stakeholders and team channels of the intent to scale the deployment.
steps.verify: Notification is posted and acknowledged in the designated communication channel.

steps.action: Update the desired replica count for the deployment to the calculated target.
steps.verify: Deployment configuration reflects the new desired replica count.

steps.action: Monitor rollout progress and pod creation events for the updated deployment.
steps.verify: All new pods transition to running and ready states as shown in the dashboard.

steps.action: Check application-level health endpoints for all new pods.
steps.verify: Health checks for each new pod return successful status.

steps.action: Monitor service response times and error rates during and after scaling.
steps.verify: Dashboards show response times and error rates remain within acceptable thresholds.

steps.action: Confirm that load balancer is distributing traffic evenly across all replicas.
steps.verify: Load balancer metrics show even traffic distribution to all pods.

steps.action: Document the scaling action and observed results in the incident response log.
steps.verify: Incident log is updated with details of the scaling operation and outcome.

steps.action: Inform stakeholders that scaling is complete and system is stable.
steps.verify: Stakeholders acknowledge completion and no further issues are reported.

steps.action: Schedule a follow-up review to assess long-term capacity needs.
steps.verify: Follow-up review is scheduled and visible on the team calendar.

abort_conditions: Resource usage assessment indicates insufficient cluster capacity.
Verification of pod health checks fails for new replicas.
Error rates or latency increase beyond acceptable limits after scaling.

name: Respond to Customer Escalation
description: A customer support agent addresses a customer escalation by reviewing the ticket, gathering relevant information, coordinating with internal teams, and providing timely updates to the customer. The process ensures that the customer's concerns are resolved efficiently while maintaining internal documentation and communication standards.

steps.action: Review the escalated customer ticket and associated communication history
steps.verify: Ticket details and escalation notes are visible in the ticketing system

steps.action: Acknowledge receipt of the escalation to the customer and log the response
steps.verify: Customer receives notification and acknowledgment is recorded in the ticket

steps.action: Gather all relevant customer account and case information from internal systems
steps.verify: All required case details are added to the ticket for reference

steps.action: Consult internal knowledge base or documentation for similar issues and solutions
steps.verify: Relevant documentation is referenced in the ticket activity log

steps.action: Coordinate with appropriate internal teams or subject matter experts for input
steps.verify: Internal team responses and recommendations are documented in the ticket

steps.action: Update the escalation status and assign ticket priority according to internal guidelines
steps.verify: Ticket is updated with correct status and priority in the ticketing system

steps.action: Draft a response to the customer summarizing findings and next steps
steps.verify: Drafted response is added to the ticket and pending review

steps.action: Submit the drafted customer response for manager approval
steps.verify: Manager approval or feedback is recorded in the ticket workflow

steps.action: Send the approved response to the customer and document communication
steps.verify: Customer receives response and message is logged in the ticketing system

steps.action: Monitor the ticket for customer follow-up or additional questions
steps.verify: Ongoing ticket activity is tracked and visible in the system

steps.action: Update internal documentation with any new insights or resolutions from the escalation
steps.verify: Knowledge base or documentation reflects the newly added information

steps.action: Close the ticket when the customer confirms resolution or after a defined period without response
steps.verify: Ticket is marked as closed and closure reason is logged in the ticketing system

abort_conditions: Manager approval is denied for customer communication
Verification of internal information fails
Customer requests escalation to a higher authority

name: Respond to Critical Platform Alert
description: An on-call site reliability engineer receives a high-priority alert regarding a production platform issue. The goal is to investigate, triage, and mitigate the incident while ensuring all relevant stakeholders are informed and post-incident actions are initiated. This process emphasizes thorough monitoring, clear communication, and incident documentation.

steps.action: Acknowledge the incoming critical alert in the monitoring system
steps.verify: Alert status is updated to acknowledged in monitoring dashboard

steps.action: Review the alert details and impacted systems on the monitoring dashboard
steps.verify: Impacted resources and alert metadata are clearly identified

steps.action: Check recent alert history and related incidents for context
steps.verify: Relevant historical alerts and incidents are reviewed in the incident log

steps.action: Assess current key metrics and system health indicators
steps.verify: Metric anomalies or degradations are observed and noted

steps.action: Notify the on-call team and relevant stakeholders of the incident
steps.verify: Stakeholders receive notification and acknowledge receipt

steps.action: Document the initial alert details and impact in the ticketing system
steps.verify: Incident ticket is created and contains all initial information

steps.action: Correlate alert data with logs and traces to identify possible root causes
steps.verify: Relevant log entries and traces are linked to the incident ticket

steps.action: Cross-reference current alert with known issues or runbooks
steps.verify: Existing runbooks or known issues are consulted and referenced

steps.action: Escalate incident to additional experts or teams as required
steps.verify: Escalation is logged and appropriate team members are engaged

steps.action: Monitor system status for changes or further degradation
steps.verify: System metrics and dashboards reflect ongoing status

steps.action: Coordinate response actions with team members based on findings
steps.verify: Response actions are documented and assigned in the incident ticket

steps.action: Update incident ticket with progress notes and findings
steps.verify: Ticket reflects up-to-date status and investigative steps

steps.action: Communicate ongoing status to stakeholders at regular intervals
steps.verify: Stakeholder updates are logged and acknowledged

steps.action: Confirm resolution of the alert and system stabilization
steps.verify: System health returns to normal and alert clears from dashboard

steps.action: Document root cause analysis and remediation steps in ticket
steps.verify: Root cause and remediation summary is present in the incident record

steps.action: Initiate post-incident review and assign follow-up actions
steps.verify: Post-incident review is scheduled and follow-up tasks are assigned

abort_conditions: Verification of system health fails
Incident escalates beyond platform team capability
Stakeholder or management requests halt

name: Routine Database Maintenance
description: A senior backend developer performs routine maintenance on the production payments database. The goal is to ensure data integrity, optimize performance, and proactively address potential issues before they impact the system. Activities are conducted with read-only access to avoid unintended modifications.

invariants: No changes are made to any database schema objects.
No changes are made to any data rows.
No changes are made to database users, roles, or permissions.
No changes are made to database configuration or parameters.
refusal_conditions: Requested operation requires write, modify, or delete permissions.
Target system is not the production payments database.
Any step would require deviation from read_only constraint.

steps.action: Review the current health status of the production payments database
steps.verify: Database health dashboard reflects up-to-date status with no critical alerts
refusal_conditions: Health dashboard does not correspond to production payments database.
Health dashboard data is older than 5 minutes and cannot be refreshed.

steps.action: Analyze recent database logs for errors or warnings
steps.verify: Log review summary documents any findings and is attached to the maintenance ticket
refusal_conditions: Logs cannot be accessed in read_only mode.
Maintenance ticket identifier is not provided.

steps.action: Check replication status and lag for read replicas
steps.verify: Replication metrics show consistent synchronization with acceptable lag
refusal_conditions: Replication information is not available for all listed replicas.
Access requires any write or configuration change.

steps.action: Evaluate current database query performance using monitoring tools
steps.verify: Performance dashboard indicates no abnormal slow queries or bottlenecks
refusal_conditions: Performance dashboard does not correspond to production payments database.
Only write-capable tools are available for performance evaluation.

steps.action: Assess storage utilization and growth trends
steps.verify: Storage report is generated and filed in the maintenance documentation
refusal_conditions: Historical storage data for the last 30 days is unavailable.
Maintenance documentation location is not writable for report storage.

steps.action: Review database backup logs and retention status
steps.verify: Backup logs confirm recent successful backups, and retention policy is met
refusal_conditions: Backup logs cannot be accessed without modification permissions.
Retention policy definition is not available.

steps.action: Inspect user access logs for unusual activity
steps.verify: Access log analysis report is added to the audit trail with no anomalies flagged
refusal_conditions: Access logs are only available through tools that allow modification.
Audit trail location is not accessible for writing the report.

steps.action: Summarize findings and recommended actions for the next maintenance window
steps.verify: Summary report with recommendations is reviewed and shared with the backend team
refusal_conditions: Outputs from any prior step are missing.
Backend team distribution list is not available.

abort_conditions: Verification step reveals a critical error requiring escalation
Required monitoring tools are unavailable
Database health status indicates unsafe conditions for maintenance

name: Production Issue Triage and Response
description: A site reliability engineer on-call responds to a critical alert indicating a potential service outage in the production environment. The engineer will triage the issue, coordinate with relevant teams, and restore service while documenting findings for post-incident analysis. Actions must be performed with full administrative access and careful adherence to safety and approval protocols.

invariants: No destructive action is executed without recorded approval from a second person.
All actions are logged in the incident management system.
Production access is limited to the cluster(s) associated with the triggering alert.
refusal_conditions: Requested operation cannot be associated with an active incident record.
Two-person approval cannot be recorded for destructive actions.
Target environment is not confirmed as production.

steps.action: Acknowledge receipt of the production alert and notify the incident response channel
steps.verify: Incident channel reflects alert acknowledgment and notification message is visible
refusal_conditions: Incident response channel is not reachable.

steps.action: Gather initial impact assessment from monitoring and alerting dashboards
steps.verify: Dashboard shows affected services and scope of outage is identified
refusal_conditions: Monitoring dashboards for the relevant cluster are unavailable.
Affected services cannot be determined from available data.

steps.action: Escalate to on-call engineering and platform teams as required
steps.verify: Escalation is logged in the incident management system and additional responders are paged
refusal_conditions: Oncall contact lists are not available.

steps.action: Review recent changes and deployments in the target cluster
steps.verify: Change log entries are identified and correlated with incident timeline
refusal_conditions: Change logs for the target cluster are unavailable.
Target cluster identifier is not provided.

steps.action: Collect relevant logs and error traces from the affected pods and nodes
steps.verify: Log repository contains entries for the incident timeframe and error patterns are documented
refusal_conditions: Logs cannot be collected without modifying log configuration.
Affected pod or node lists are empty.

steps.action: Isolate failing components to prevent further impact
steps.verify: Isolation actions are confirmed on the cluster dashboard and service impact does not worsen
refusal_conditions: Two-person approval is not recorded for required isolation actions.
Requested isolation action is not in allowed_isolation_actions.

steps.action: Request approval for destructive remediation actions per two-person rule
steps.verify: Approval is granted and documented in the incident management system
refusal_conditions: Approver identity is the same as requester identity.

steps.action: Apply approved remediation actions to restore service functionality
steps.verify: Service health indicators improve and recovery is reflected in monitoring dashboards
refusal_conditions: Approved actions require capabilities outside allowed_operations.

steps.action: Monitor system metrics for stability and absence of regression
steps.verify: Metrics remain within normal thresholds for a sustained period
refusal_conditions: Baseline threshold definitions are not available.

steps.action: Communicate status updates to stakeholders and leadership
steps.verify: Status updates are posted in incident and stakeholder channels
refusal_conditions: Incident status summary is not available.

steps.action: Document all actions taken, observations, and contributing factors
steps.verify: Incident timeline and action log are complete in the incident record
refusal_conditions: Incident record is read_only and cannot be updated.
Chronological list of actions is incomplete.

steps.action: Coordinate with security and compliance teams if sensitive data exposure is suspected
steps.verify: Security and compliance involvement is logged and any required steps are initiated
refusal_conditions: Security or compliance contact information is unavailable.

steps.action: Initiate post-incident review scheduling and handoff to follow-up owner
steps.verify: Post-incident review is scheduled and assigned in the incident management system
refusal_conditions: Incident management system cannot create new review entries.

steps.action: Update incident status to resolved and notify all responders
steps.verify: Incident record shows resolved status and responders confirm receipt

abort_conditions: Approval for remediation actions is denied
Verification of remediation fails or causes further service degradation
Incident scope expands beyond initial assessment and requires escalation to higher authority

name: Routine Maintenance Review
description: A site reliability engineer performs a scheduled review of platform service health and applies routine maintenance tasks. The engineer ensures that all platform components are operating within expected parameters, applies minor updates, and documents actions taken. This process maintains system reliability and prepares the environment for upcoming workloads.

invariants: Only changes explicitly listed as preapproved minor updates may be applied.
All applied changes are recorded in the maintenance log.
No change may reduce redundancy or high-availability configuration.
refusal_conditions: Requested update is not in the preapproved minor updates list.
Cloud console access is not scoped to production platform services.

steps.action: Review the current service health dashboards for key platform components
steps.verify: Dashboards display all system components as healthy and within thresholds
refusal_conditions: Key platform components list is incomplete.

steps.action: Check for any unresolved alerts or incidents from the previous maintenance window
steps.verify: Incident management system shows all previous alerts resolved or documented
refusal_conditions: Previous maintenance window identifier is missing.

steps.action: Validate the current version and patch levels of critical platform services
steps.verify: Service inventory documents reflect up-to-date versions and patches
refusal_conditions: Service inventory is outdated or unavailable.
List of critical platform services is missing.

steps.action: Apply approved minor updates or patches to platform services as scheduled
steps.verify: Update logs and version tracking confirm successful application of patches
refusal_conditions: Requested update is not in preapproved_update_list.
Maintenance schedule does not indicate current time as within window.

steps.action: Monitor logs and metrics for anomalies following updates
steps.verify: Monitoring tools show normal operation and absence of new errors
refusal_conditions: List of updated services is empty.

steps.action: Perform configuration drift checks to ensure system state matches desired configuration
steps.verify: Configuration management reports no drift or discrepancies

steps.action: Document all maintenance actions and observed results in the maintenance log
steps.verify: Maintenance log entry is complete and accessible to the SRE team
refusal_conditions: List of actions performed is incomplete.

steps.action: Notify relevant stakeholders of maintenance completion and any follow-up actions required
steps.verify: Stakeholder notification is sent and acknowledged

abort_conditions: Verification of service health fails during review
Unexpected anomalies or errors detected post-update
Required update or patch cannot be applied safely

name: Troubleshoot Model Inference Latency in Production
description: An MLOps engineer investigates and resolves elevated model inference latency observed in the production environment. The process includes validating monitoring data, reviewing deployment configurations, and collaborating with relevant teams to restore optimal model serving performance. Actions are performed with safe write permissions and respect for data sensitivity.

invariants: Only resource-related configuration parameters may be adjusted.
No changes are made to model artifacts or training data.
All configuration changes are recorded and attributable.
refusal_conditions: Requested change affects model version or artifacts.
Platform engineering approval is not recorded for configuration changes.

steps.action: Acknowledge the reported inference latency issue in the monitoring system
steps.verify: Incident is visible in monitoring dashboard and assigned for investigation

steps.action: Review recent alerts and logs related to model serving pods
steps.verify: Relevant logs and alert history are collected and summarized
refusal_conditions: List of model serving pods is empty.
Logs cannot be accessed in read_only mode.

steps.action: Check current resource utilization of inference pods
steps.verify: Resource metrics such as CPU and memory usage are available and graphed

steps.action: Validate that the model version deployed matches expected production configuration
steps.verify: Deployment and model versions are confirmed in deployment records
refusal_conditions: Expected production model version is not provided.

steps.action: Assess pod health and restart counts for inference services
steps.verify: Pod status and restart metrics are reviewed and documented

steps.action: Evaluate recent changes or deployments to the model or serving infrastructure
steps.verify: Change logs and deployment history are reviewed for correlations
refusal_conditions: Change logs or deployment history are unavailable.

steps.action: Examine network latency and connectivity between model pods and upstream data sources
steps.verify: Network metrics and latency data are analyzed for anomalies
refusal_conditions: Upstream data source list is missing.

steps.action: Check for resource throttling or quota limits being reached
steps.verify: Quota usage reports and throttling events are documented

steps.action: Collaborate with the platform engineering team to review node health and cluster status
steps.verify: Cluster health and node availability reports are obtained
refusal_conditions: Cluster health dashboard cannot be accessed.

steps.action: Implement configuration adjustments to allocate additional resources to inference pods if warranted
steps.verify: Resource allocation changes are reflected in pod specifications and confirmed in the dashboard
refusal_conditions: Platform engineering approval record is missing.

steps.action: Monitor inference latency metrics after adjustments
steps.verify: Post-adjustment metrics show latency trend and are compared to baseline
refusal_conditions: Baseline latency thresholds are not available.

steps.action: Document the investigation steps and resolution actions in the incident tracking system
steps.verify: Incident ticket is updated with investigation notes and resolution summary

abort_conditions: Verification of resource changes fails
Incident is escalated due to unresolved performance degradation
Approval for configuration change is denied by platform engineering

name: Access Review and Remediation
description: A security engineer initiates a routine access review to ensure that user permissions in the IAM system are appropriate and compliant. The process includes identifying over-privileged accounts, documenting findings, and remediating any access that does not adhere to policy. Results are verified and reviewed for audit readiness.

invariants: No access changes are made before manager approval is recorded.
All remediation actions are traceable to specific policy violations.
Audit logs are preserved and not modified.
refusal_conditions: IAM access is not scoped to production.

steps.action: Retrieve the current list of all user accounts and their assigned roles from the IAM system.
steps.verify: User and role inventory is generated and available for review.
refusal_conditions: Scope definition for accounts is missing.

steps.action: Cross-reference user roles with the latest access policy documentation.
steps.verify: Discrepancies between assigned roles and policy are clearly identified.
refusal_conditions: Access policy documentation is unavailable or outdated.
User role assignment list is incomplete.

steps.action: Catalog accounts with elevated privileges for further scrutiny.
steps.verify: List of privileged accounts is compiled and confirmed.
refusal_conditions: Definition of elevated privileges is missing.
User role assignment list is unavailable.

steps.action: Identify accounts with unused or dormant access over the last 90 days.
steps.verify: Dormant accounts are flagged in the review report.
refusal_conditions: Access activity logs for last 90 days are incomplete.

steps.action: Document all instances of excessive or inappropriate permissions.
steps.verify: Remediation candidates are listed with supporting evidence.
refusal_conditions: Any of the required input lists are missing.

steps.action: Draft remediation actions for each policy violation or risk area.
steps.verify: Remediation proposals are documented and ready for approval.

steps.action: Submit remediation plan to management for approval.
steps.verify: Approval request is tracked and status is updated.

steps.action: Upon receiving manager approval, coordinate with system owners to remove or adjust excessive permissions.
steps.verify: Permissions are updated and changes are logged in the IAM system.
refusal_conditions: Manager approval record is missing or indicates denial.

steps.action: Notify affected users of any changes to their access.
steps.verify: Notifications are sent and delivery is confirmed.
refusal_conditions: List of affected users is empty.

steps.action: Update access review documentation with details of remediation actions taken.
steps.verify: Documentation reflects all completed actions and is audit-ready.
refusal_conditions: Access review document repository is read_only.

steps.action: Schedule a follow-up review to confirm that remediated access remains compliant.
steps.verify: Follow-up review is logged and scheduled in the compliance calendar.
refusal_conditions: Follow-up review interval is outside allowed range.

abort_conditions: Manager approval is denied for remediation plan.
Verification of user and role inventory fails.
Access to IAM system is unavailable.

name: Fulfill User Access Request
description: A security engineer receives a request to grant a team member additional access to a production system. The process involves validating the request, ensuring compliance, and updating permissions in the identity management system. All actions must adhere to internal governance and require security approval.

invariants: requested_user_must_not_be_the_executor
all_permission_changes_must_be_tied_to_single_ticket_id
no_changes_made_if_any_abort_condition_is_true
all_approvals_must_be_recorded_with_timestamp_and_approver_id

steps.action: Receive and review the access request submitted by the requester
steps.verify: Access request is logged and all required information is present in the ticketing system
guardrails: do_not_modify_any_permissions_in_this_step
do_not_change_ticket_status_in_this_step
if_any_required_ticket_field_missing_mark_step_as_failed

steps.action: Validate the identity of the requester and confirm their role and team alignment
steps.verify: User identity and team membership are confirmed in the user directory
guardrails: do_not_update_directory_records
do_not_proceed_if_no_active_record_found
do_not_proceed_if_multiple_records_found_for_requester_user_id

steps.action: Assess the business justification and intended access scope provided in the request
steps.verify: Justification aligns with documented business needs and is recorded in the ticket
guardrails: do_not_modify_business_justification_text
do_not_add_or_remove_permissions_from_request
if_any_requested_permission_not_in_access_matrix_mark_step_as_failed

steps.action: Check if the requested access exceeds the principle of least privilege
steps.verify: Access scope does not grant unnecessary permissions as confirmed in access matrix
guardrails: do_not_modify_current_permissions_in_this_step
do_not_auto_reduce_requested_permissions
if_least_privilege_compliant_equals_false_mark_step_as_failed

steps.action: Review recent security incidents or policy violations related to the requester
steps.verify: No outstanding incidents or policy flags are found for the requester
guardrails: read_only_access_to_incident_system
do_not_close_or_modify_incidents
if_any_open_incident_or_policy_flag_found_mark_step_as_failed

steps.action: Submit the request for security approval as required by policy
steps.verify: Approval request is visible in the designated approval queue
guardrails: do_not_mark_approval_as_granted_in_this_step
do_not_bypass_security_approval_queue
if_approval_request_cannot_be_created_mark_step_as_failed

steps.action: Receive security approval for the access change
steps.verify: Approval is formally documented in the ticket or workflow system
guardrails: do_not_change_approval_status
do_not_proceed_if_approval_status_not_equal_approved
if_approval_not_received_within_max_wait_time_treat_as_denied

steps.action: Update the user’s permissions in the identity and access management system
steps.verify: Permissions are updated and reflected in the IAM audit log
guardrails: do_not_modify_permissions_for_any_user_other_than_target_user_id
do_not_add_permissions_not_listed_in_requested_permissions
if_iam_update_fails_do_not_retry_automatically

steps.action: Notify the requester and their manager of the access change
steps.verify: Notification is sent and acknowledged by relevant parties via ticket or email
guardrails: do_not_include_sensitive_credentials_in_notifications
do_not_notify_any_users_other_than_requester_and_manager
do_not_change_ticket_status_in_this_step

steps.action: Log the completed access change and update the access request ticket with actions taken
steps.verify: Ticket is closed with a detailed audit trail and completion notes
guardrails: do_not_reopen_ticket_after_closing_in_this_step
do_not_modify_past_audit_entries
do_not_change_any_permissions_in_this_step

abort_conditions: Approval is denied or not granted within required timeframe
Verification of requester identity or justification fails
Detected policy violation or suspicious activity during review

name: Rollback a Failed Release in Kubernetes
description: A site reliability engineer needs to rollback a recent release in the production Kubernetes cluster after detecting critical errors. The rollback must be executed safely, with proper validation and communication to stakeholders, ensuring system stability and compliance with production change controls.

invariants: rollback_target_version_must_exist_in_deployment_history
all_actions_must_be_tied_to_single_incident_id
no_changes_made_if_any_abort_condition_is_true

steps.action: Identify the failed release and impacted services in the Kubernetes cluster
steps.verify: Incident report and monitoring dashboards confirm release version and affected services
guardrails: do_not_modify_any_kubernetes_resources_in_this_step
do_not_change_incident_severity

steps.action: Notify the engineering manager and relevant stakeholders about the intended rollback
steps.verify: Stakeholders acknowledge notification in incident channel or ticket
guardrails: do_not_include_sensitive_credentials_in_notifications
do_not_change_incident_status_in_this_step

steps.action: Request manager approval for the rollback operation as per production change policy
steps.verify: Approval is documented in the change management system
guardrails: do_not_modify_change_request_status
do_not_proceed_if_status_not_equal_approved

steps.action: Review the deployment history for the affected Kubernetes workloads
steps.verify: Deployment logs and history show last known good version
guardrails: read_only_access_to_deployment_history
do_not_change_any_deployments_in_this_step

steps.action: Validate that pre-rollback health checks and system dependencies are in a stable state
steps.verify: Health dashboards and dependency status reports are green
guardrails: do_not_modify_monitoring_configuration
do_not_ignore_critical_alerts

steps.action: Update incident documentation with rollback plan and expected outcomes
steps.verify: Incident ticket reflects rollback plan and timeline
guardrails: do_not_change_incident_severity
do_not_close_incident_in_this_step

steps.action: Execute the rollback to the previously stable release version for all affected workloads
steps.verify: Deployment status in Kubernetes reflects previous version as active
guardrails: do_not_modify_services_not_listed_in_impacted_services
do_not_change_namespace_configuration
do_not_change_cluster_level_resources

steps.action: Monitor application and service health metrics post-rollback for anomalies
steps.verify: Monitoring dashboards show improvement and no new critical alerts
guardrails: read_only_access_to_monitoring_system
do_not_silence_alerts

steps.action: Validate that user-facing endpoints and APIs are functioning as expected
steps.verify: Synthetic tests and user reports confirm service restoration
guardrails: do_not_modify_test_definitions
do_not_contact_end_users_directly

steps.action: Update stakeholders with rollback completion status and next steps
steps.verify: Stakeholders confirm receipt and understanding of rollback summary
guardrails: do_not_include_sensitive_internal_credentials
do_not_change_incident_status_in_this_step

steps.action: Document lessons learned and preliminary root cause in the incident report
steps.verify: Incident ticket includes updated documentation and action items
guardrails: do_not_close_incident_in_this_step
do_not_assign_blame_to_named_individuals

steps.action: Schedule a post-incident review to address process or technical gaps
steps.verify: Calendar invite sent and accepted by required participants
guardrails: do_not_modify_existing_calendar_events
do_not_invite_external_participants

abort_conditions: Manager approval for rollback is denied
Pre-rollback health checks fail or indicate unsafe environment
Critical system dependencies are unavailable

name: Schema Update for New Feature
description: A senior backend developer needs to perform a schema update in the production database to support a new payments feature. The process must follow safe write constraints to minimize risk and ensure data integrity. This operation is part of a routine maintenance window and requires careful verification at each step.

invariants: all_schema_changes_must_be_executed_via_versioned_migrations
no_data_loss_operations_are_permitted
no_changes_made_if_any_abort_condition_is_true

steps.action: Review the schema change request and associated documentation
steps.verify: Documentation is up-to-date and change request is approved in the ticketing system
guardrails: do_not_modify_ticket_approval_status
do_not_edit_schema_change_documentation

steps.action: Notify relevant stakeholders and schedule a maintenance window
steps.verify: Stakeholders acknowledge notification and maintenance window is visible on shared calendar
guardrails: do_not_modify_existing_calendar_events
do_not_invite_external_recipients

steps.action: Obtain manager approval for the schema update
steps.verify: Approval is recorded in the ticketing or approval system
guardrails: do_not_change_approval_status
do_not_proceed_if_status_not_equal_approved

steps.action: Validate current database state and take a logical backup
steps.verify: Backup file is stored in the designated backup location and backup logs confirm completion
guardrails: do_not_perform_physical_backup_operations
do_not_delete_existing_backups

steps.action: Apply schema migrations to a staging environment for a dry run
steps.verify: Schema updates are reflected in staging and automated tests pass
guardrails: do_not_modify_production_database_in_this_step
do_not_change_test_definitions

steps.action: Review application logs and metrics in staging for anomalies
steps.verify: No errors or unexpected warnings are present in staging logs
guardrails: read_only_access_to_logs_and_metrics
do_not_silence_staging_alerts

steps.action: Prepare and document a rollback plan
steps.verify: Rollback steps are reviewed and accessible to the incident response team
guardrails: do_not_delete_previous_rollback_plans
do_not_modify_backup_id

steps.action: Apply schema changes to the production database during the scheduled window
steps.verify: Schema version in production matches the intended update
guardrails: do_not_execute_migration_outside_maintenance_window
do_not_execute_unapproved_scripts

steps.action: Monitor application health and database metrics post-update
steps.verify: No performance degradation or error spikes are observed on monitoring dashboards
guardrails: read_only_access_to_monitoring_system
do_not_silence_production_alerts

steps.action: Validate that new feature functionality is working as expected in production
steps.verify: Feature passes acceptance criteria and basic user flows are successful
guardrails: do_not_modify_acceptance_tests
do_not_trigger_load_tests

steps.action: Update documentation and communicate completion to stakeholders
steps.verify: Documentation reflects the new schema and stakeholders confirm receipt of completion notice
guardrails: do_not_remove_historical_schema_versions_from_documentation
do_not_include_sensitive_credentials_in_notifications

abort_conditions: Verification fails at any step
Manager approval is denied
Unexpected errors or anomalies detected during staging or post-deployment monitoring

name: Quarterly Compliance Reporting
description: The executive assistant coordinates the quarterly compliance reporting process for leadership review. This involves gathering status updates from key departments, compiling documentation, and submitting the report for executive approval. The process ensures the organization meets its regulatory obligations and maintains a clear audit trail.

invariants: all_status_updates_must_be_tied_to_specific_department
final_report_must_reference_quarter_and_year
no_changes_made_if_any_abort_condition_is_true

steps.action: Review compliance reporting requirements for the quarter
steps.verify: Checklist of requirements is updated and accessible
guardrails: do_not_edit_compliance_requirements
do_not_use_checklist_from_prior_quarter

steps.action: Collect supporting documentation from each department
