do_not_move_documents_out_of_secure_storage_location

steps.action: Compile collected information into the compliance report template
steps.verify: Draft report is saved and accessible in docs wiki
guardrails: do_not_modify_source_department_documents
do_not_share_draft_report_externally

steps.action: Review compiled report for completeness and accuracy
steps.verify: No outstanding items or discrepancies in the report
guardrails: do_not_change_requirements_checklist
do_not_mark_report_as_complete_if_any_requirement_missing

steps.action: Share draft report with department leads for feedback
steps.verify: Feedback received and documented from all leads
guardrails: do_not_share_draft_report_with_non_leads
do_not_modify_draft_report_in_this_step

steps.action: Incorporate feedback and finalize compliance report
steps.verify: Final report version is saved and marked as complete
guardrails: do_not_delete_draft_report
do_not_share_final_report_externally

steps.action: Submit finalized report to executive leadership for approval

guardrails: do_not_grant_edit_access_to_executives_if_not_required
do_not_send_report_to_non_executive_recipients

steps.action: Follow up with executives to confirm report approval
steps.verify: Manager approval status is received and documented
guardrails: do_not_modify_approval_records
do_not_proceed_as_approved_if_no_record_exists

steps.action: Archive approved report in the designated compliance repository
steps.verify: Archived report is accessible and indexed for audit
guardrails: do_not_delete_prior_archived_reports
do_not_modify_archived_reports_content

abort_conditions: Department leads do not provide required updates
Manager approval is denied or delayed

name: Handle Customer Escalation
description: A customer support agent responds to a high-priority escalation from a key account. The agent must review the issue, coordinate with relevant teams, and ensure the customer's concerns are addressed promptly. All actions must be logged and the customer updated throughout the process.

invariants: all_actions_must_be_logged_in_ticket
no_changes_made_if_any_abort_condition_is_true

steps.action: Review the escalated ticket details and customer history

do_not_change_ticket_owner

steps.action: Acknowledge receipt of the escalation to the customer

do_not_change_ticket_status_in_this_step

steps.action: Notify the support manager of the escalation
steps.verify: Manager receives escalation notification in ticketing system

do_not_assign_ticket_to_manager

steps.verify: Ticket notes reflect updated status and planned actions

do_not_change_customer_facing_sla_fields

steps.action: Gather any additional context from previous agents or related tickets

do_not_merge_tickets

name: Generate Monthly Payments Report
description: A data engineer prepares and validates a monthly payments report for business intelligence purposes. The process ensures data accuracy, compliance, and timely delivery for downstream analytics teams and stakeholders.

name: Debug Production Infrastructure Issue
description: The site reliability engineer is responding to a production issue that may impact system availability. The intent is to investigate, diagnose, and resolve the problem using production-level administrative access, while following safe operational constraints. Sensitive internal data may be accessed during the process, and actions must be logged and verified through monitoring tools.

steps.action: Review the production alert and incident ticket for context and recent events.
steps.verify: Incident ticket shows SRE assignment and alert details are accessible.

steps.action: Check system health and status dashboards for affected services and error trends.
steps.verify: Dashboards display real-time metrics and highlight anomalies.

steps.action: Confirm scope and impact by correlating logs and monitoring data across affected resources.
steps.verify: Log review and monitoring data confirm the issue's impact and affected components.

steps.action: Document initial findings and hypothesis in the incident ticket.
steps.verify: Incident ticket updated with findings and working theory.

steps.action: Identify and isolate misbehaving resources or configurations in the cloud environment.
steps.verify: List of potentially problematic resources or settings is documented.

steps.action: Apply non-disruptive diagnostic actions, such as toggling debug modes or gathering additional logs.
steps.verify: Debug data and logs are collected without service impact, and evidence is attached to the incident.

steps.action: Communicate status and progress to stakeholders and incident responders.
steps.verify: Incident channel or ticket reflects status updates and next steps.

steps.action: Test and validate any configuration changes or mitigations in a safe manner.
steps.verify: Changes are reflected in system state and confirmed via monitoring.

steps.action: Monitor system for stability and confirm resolution of the issue.
steps.verify: Dashboards and logs indicate normal operation has resumed.

steps.action: Update incident ticket with final resolution details and lessons learned.
steps.verify: Incident ticket is closed with a summary and documented actions taken.

steps.action: Schedule follow-up review for root cause analysis and preventive measures.
steps.verify: Post-incident review is scheduled and visible to the team.

abort_conditions: Verification of system state fails after a change.
Access to required logs or data is denied.
Environment is deemed unsafe for further diagnostics.

name: Campaign Performance Review
description: The marketing operations team member reviews the effectiveness of recent marketing campaigns by gathering performance data, analyzing results, and preparing a summary report. This process ensures that campaign outcomes are tracked, lessons learned are documented, and future improvements are identified.

steps.action: Log into the CRM system with appropriate access tier
steps.verify: System dashboard loads and user is authenticated

steps.action: Navigate to the marketing campaigns section
steps.verify: List of recent campaigns is visible

steps.action: Identify the campaigns that ran in the last quarter
steps.verify: Campaigns are filtered by date and displayed

steps.action: Extract key performance metrics for each campaign (e.g., open rates, click-throughs, conversions)
steps.verify: Metrics data is available for each selected campaign

steps.action: Consolidate metrics into a summary document
steps.verify: Summary document contains all relevant metrics

steps.action: Analyze trends and compare results to established benchmarks
steps.verify: Benchmark comparison is noted in the summary

steps.action: Document insights and recommendations for future campaigns
steps.verify: Recommendations section is completed in the summary report

steps.action: Share the summary report with the marketing team
steps.verify: Report is distributed and receipt is confirmed

abort_conditions: Unable to access CRM system
Required campaign data is missing
Metrics data is inconsistent or incomplete

name: Routine Cloud Infrastructure Maintenance
description: A platform engineer performs scheduled routine maintenance on cloud infrastructure to ensure system health, reliability, and compliance. The maintenance includes checking resource usage, applying safe updates, and validating system integrity. This process is conducted with production write access and must avoid any destructive changes.

steps.action: Review scheduled maintenance tasks and planned changes for the maintenance window.
steps.verify: Maintenance plan is documented and acknowledged by relevant team members.

steps.action: Notify stakeholders about the upcoming maintenance activities and expected impact.
steps.verify: Stakeholders confirm receipt of maintenance notification.

steps.action: Check the current status of critical cloud resources and services.
steps.verify: Pre-maintenance resource health is recorded in a status report.

steps.action: Apply non-disruptive updates to cloud resources as per maintenance procedures.
steps.verify: Update logs confirm successful application with no errors.

steps.action: Monitor resource utilization and performance during updates.
steps.verify: Resource metrics remain within expected thresholds on monitoring dashboard.

steps.action: Validate that all resources remain accessible and operational post-update.
steps.verify: Service health checks pass for all maintained resources.

steps.action: Document any configuration changes or issues encountered during maintenance.
steps.verify: Change log is updated with detailed notes for audit purposes.

steps.action: Communicate maintenance completion and status to stakeholders.
steps.verify: Stakeholders confirm maintenance completion and no outstanding issues.

steps.action: Update maintenance records and close the maintenance task in the tracking system.
steps.verify: Maintenance task is marked as completed in the tracking system.

abort_conditions: Verification of critical resource health fails at any step
Unexpected service disruption is detected
Stakeholder approval for maintenance is revoked

name: Fulfill User Access Request
description: A security engineer processes a user’s request to gain access to a production system. The request is evaluated for compliance with internal policies and requires security approval before access is provisioned. All actions are tracked to ensure auditability and data sensitivity is considered.

steps.action: Receive and acknowledge the user access request ticket
steps.verify: Ticket status is updated to 'In Progress' and requester is notified

steps.action: Review the request for completeness and required documentation
steps.verify: All required fields and attachments are present in the ticket

steps.action: Check the user's current access permissions and role eligibility
steps.verify: User profile and access logs confirm existing access and eligibility

steps.action: Validate the business justification for the access request
steps.verify: Justification aligns with approved use cases and policy

steps.action: Assess data sensitivity and potential impact of requested access
steps.verify: Data classification and risk assessment are documented

steps.action: Submit the request for security approval as required by policy
steps.verify: Approval workflow is initiated and approver is notified

steps.action: Receive security approval decision
steps.verify: Approval status is updated in the ticketing system

steps.action: Provision the requested access in the IAM system
steps.verify: IAM system logs show access change and user permissions updated

steps.action: Update the ticket with provisioning details and reference ID
steps.verify: Ticket contains audit trail and provisioning evidence

steps.action: Notify the requester and relevant stakeholders of access grant
steps.verify: Notification is logged and acknowledged by the requester

steps.action: Document the access change for audit and compliance records
steps.verify: Access change is logged in compliance and audit repository

steps.action: Close the access request ticket
steps.verify: Ticket status is set to 'Closed' and closure is visible in system

abort_conditions: Security approval is denied
Required documentation is missing or incomplete
Provisioning verification fails

name: Reset Employee Password
description: An IT admin receives a request to reset an employee's password due to account lockout. The admin follows a secure process to verify the request, reset the password, and ensure the employee regains access to essential systems. This task is handled with care to protect internal company information.

steps.action: Receive and review the password reset request from the employee or their manager
steps.verify: Request is documented in the ticketing system with proper details

steps.action: Confirm the identity of the requester and affected employee using company-approved verification methods
steps.verify: Identity verification is recorded in the ticket

steps.action: Check the employee's account status and recent activity for signs of compromise
steps.verify: Account activity log is reviewed and noted in the ticket

steps.action: Obtain manager approval for the password reset as required by policy
steps.verify: Manager approval is attached to the ticket

steps.action: Initiate the password reset process in the identity and access management system
steps.verify: System logs show password reset action executed

steps.action: Set a temporary password and configure required password change at next login
steps.verify: Temporary password and forced change flag are visible in account settings

steps.action: Notify the employee with secure instructions for accessing their account and updating their password
steps.verify: Notification is sent and receipt is logged in the ticket

steps.action: Document the reset action, including time, method, and approver details in the ticketing system
steps.verify: Ticket contains full audit trail of the reset process

steps.action: Follow up with the employee to confirm successful account access and password update
steps.verify: Employee confirms access and password change; confirmation is recorded in the ticket

abort_conditions: Identity verification fails
Manager approval is denied or not provided
Account shows signs of compromise requiring security escalation

name: Debug Production Model Inference Issue
description: An MLOps engineer is tasked with investigating and resolving a production issue affecting the model inference pipeline. The situation is urgent as it impacts end-user experience and requires safe changes to production systems. The engineer must ensure any actions are performed with care and validate outcomes at each step.

steps.action: Acknowledge the reported inference issue and collect incident details from monitoring alerts and logs.
steps.verify: Incident is logged and all relevant alert data is gathered.

steps.action: Notify stakeholders and communicate investigation is in progress.
steps.verify: Stakeholders receive notification and acknowledge receipt.

steps.action: Access production logs for the inference pipeline to identify error patterns or failures.
steps.verify: Error patterns or failure signatures are documented and understood.

steps.action: Check resource utilization metrics for model serving pods and supporting infrastructure.
steps.verify: Resource usage anomalies are identified or ruled out.

steps.action: Review recent changes in model versions, configuration, or dependencies deployed to production.
steps.verify: Change history is reviewed and any recent modifications are noted.

steps.action: Validate input data integrity and confirm data pipeline is delivering expected inputs to the model.
steps.verify: Input data samples are checked and verified for correctness.

steps.action: Manually trigger test inference requests to evaluate model behavior and response patterns.
steps.verify: Test inferences are executed and results are collected for analysis.

steps.action: Isolate failing components within the inference pipeline and analyze their health status.
steps.verify: Component health statuses are reviewed and problematic areas are pinpointed.

steps.action: Apply safe configuration adjustments or restart affected model serving pods as needed.
steps.verify: Configuration changes or restarts are reflected in system state and tracked.

steps.action: Monitor system metrics and logs post-action to ensure issue symptoms are resolved.
steps.verify: Metrics and logs confirm restoration of normal operation.

steps.action: Inform stakeholders of resolution and document root cause and actions taken.
steps.verify: Stakeholders confirm resolution and documentation is updated.

steps.action: Perform a post-incident review to identify long-term improvements for model serving reliability.
steps.verify: Review outcomes and recommendations are recorded and shared.

abort_conditions: Verification of incident details or logs fails
Safe remediation cannot be performed with current permissions
Environment is deemed unstable for further troubleshooting

name: Execute Release Smoke Tests
description: A QA engineer conducts smoke testing on the staging environment as part of deployment preparation. The goal is to validate basic system functionality and catch any critical issues before release. The results are documented and shared with the release team for go/no-go decision.

steps.action: Review the deployment ticket and release notes for scope and acceptance criteria.
steps.verify: Deployment ticket contains clear scope and release notes are accessible.

steps.action: Confirm staging environment is updated with the release candidate build.
steps.verify: Staging environment version matches release candidate.

steps.action: Initialize smoke test suite according to defined test plan.
steps.verify: Test suite execution is triggered and test logs are generated.

steps.action: Execute core functionality tests covering major user flows.
steps.verify: Test results indicate pass/fail status for each major flow.

steps.action: Validate system login and authentication mechanisms.
steps.verify: Authentication tests complete with expected results.

steps.action: Check database connectivity and basic data operations.
steps.verify: Database test cases pass and logs show successful data access.

steps.action: Verify integration with key third-party services.
steps.verify: Integration test outcomes are logged and indicate success or failure.

steps.action: Perform basic UI validation for main application screens.
steps.verify: UI test results reflect expected layouts and elements.

steps.action: Monitor application logs for critical errors during test execution.
steps.verify: No unexpected errors or exceptions are present in logs.

steps.action: Document smoke test results and observed issues in the test report.
steps.verify: Test report is completed and accessible to the release team.

steps.action: Notify release team of test outcomes and readiness for next steps.
steps.verify: Release team receives notification and acknowledges results.

abort_conditions: Critical smoke test fails
Staging environment is not ready
Test plan or acceptance criteria are missing

name: Month-End Close Process
description: The accountant performs the month-end close process to ensure all financial transactions are recorded accurately and reports are finalized. This process helps maintain compliance and supports accurate financial reporting for internal and regulatory purposes. Sensitive financial data is handled according to company policy.

steps.action: Gather all relevant financial transaction data for the closing period from the ERP system.
steps.verify: All expected transaction records are present and accessible in the system.

steps.action: Reconcile bank statements with ledger balances to identify discrepancies.
steps.verify: Bank and ledger balances match or discrepancies are documented.

steps.action: Review and validate all journal entries for accuracy and completeness.
steps.verify: All journal entries are approved and reflect correct amounts.

steps.action: Accrue outstanding expenses and revenues that have not yet been recorded.
steps.verify: Accrual entries are visible in the ERP and align with supporting documentation.

steps.action: Update fixed asset records and calculate monthly depreciation.
steps.verify: Depreciation entries are posted and asset records are up to date.

steps.action: Validate accounts payable and accounts receivable balances for the period.
steps.verify: Aging reports for payables and receivables are accurate and reflect current balances.

steps.action: Prepare preliminary financial statements (balance sheet, income statement, cash flow).
steps.verify: Draft financial statements are generated and reflect all posted entries.

steps.action: Submit preliminary statements and closing package for manager approval.
steps.verify: Manager approval status is logged in the ERP or documented in the workflow.

steps.action: Address feedback from management and make any required adjustments.
steps.verify: All requested changes are implemented and confirmed by management.

steps.action: Finalize and lock the period in the ERP to prevent further changes.
steps.verify: ERP period status reflects 'closed' and no further entries are permitted.

abort_conditions: Manager approval is denied for the closing package.
Reconciliation discrepancies cannot be resolved.
Critical financial data is missing or inaccessible.

name: Respond to Network Alert
description: A site reliability engineer is responding to a high-urgency alert indicating potential network degradation in the production environment. The goal is to assess the situation, identify root cause, and restore network health while documenting actions for post-incident review.

steps.action: Acknowledge receipt of the network alert in the incident management system
steps.verify: Alert status updated to acknowledged and visible to oncall team

steps.action: Review alert details and affected services in monitoring dashboards
steps.verify: Dashboards display alert metrics and impacted network segments

steps.action: Notify relevant stakeholders about the ongoing investigation
steps.verify: Stakeholder notification confirmed in communication channel

steps.action: Correlate incident with recent changes or deployments
steps.verify: Change logs and deployment records reviewed and correlated

steps.action: Collect logs and network health metrics from affected systems
steps.verify: Log and metric data collected and accessible for analysis

steps.action: Identify potential root causes using available diagnostic tools
steps.verify: List of suspected causes documented in incident notes

steps.action: Implement non-disruptive mitigations to restore network stability
steps.verify: Mitigations applied and network metrics show improvement

steps.action: Monitor the impact of mitigations in real time
steps.verify: Monitoring dashboards reflect stabilization or recovery

steps.action: Escalate to additional teams if the incident persists or expands
steps.verify: Escalation recorded and additional teams engaged

steps.action: Update incident ticket with actions taken and current status
steps.verify: Incident ticket reflects latest actions and status updates

steps.action: Continue monitoring until network operations are fully restored
steps.verify: Network performance metrics return to normal thresholds

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of incident resolution and recovery
steps.verify: Resolution message delivered and acknowledged by stakeholders

steps.action: Document root cause and lessons learned for post-incident review
steps.verify: Incident documentation updated with root cause and summary

abort_conditions: Verification of network restoration fails
Incident severity escalates beyond oncall capabilities
Environment determined to be unsafe for further action

name: Perform Routine Kubernetes Cluster Maintenance
description: A site reliability engineer conducts scheduled maintenance on the production Kubernetes cluster to ensure optimal performance and stability. This includes reviewing resource usage, updating configurations, and validating system health. The process follows safe write constraints to minimize risk to running workloads.

execution_constraints_detail.safe_write_definition: Only pre-approved configuration changes and patches may be applied. No destructive operations (resource deletions or irreversible changes) are permitted.
execution_constraints_detail.time_window_requirement: Execution must occur within an approved maintenance window recorded in an external change record identifier.

invariants: All production namespaces labeled 'critical=true' must remain with at least one ready pod per deployment throughout all steps.
Cluster API server must remain reachable at all times.
No changes are made outside the specified production cluster context.
refusal_conditions: If required dashboards or monitoring systems are not reachable.
If the maintenance window identifier is not provided.
If requested operation is not listed in allowed_operations.

steps.action: Review current cluster health and resource utilization metrics
steps.verify: Cluster dashboards display normal resource usage and no critical alerts
procedure: Access cluster_health_dashboard for context.cluster_identifier.
Record current values for CPU, memory, and disk utilization for each node.
Record count of pods in non-Ready state per namespace.
Record presence and count of alerts with severity 'critical'.
validation_criteria: critical_alert_count == 0
No node shows CPU, memory, or disk utilization above 90 percent.
No namespace has more than 0 pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' or 'Error' state.
refusal_conditions: If any validation_criteria item is not met.

steps.action: Check for pending node updates and maintenance advisories
steps.verify: Maintenance advisories and node update logs are acknowledged and documented
procedure: Query internal_change_management_system for open advisories tagged with cluster_identifier.
Query cloud_provider_node_update_feed for pending node updates for cluster_identifier.
Create a consolidated list of advisories and pending updates.
Mark each advisory as 'acknowledged' in the tracking system without changing its status or schedule.
validation_criteria: All advisories returned from advisory_sources are present in the consolidated list.
Each advisory in the consolidated list has an 'acknowledged' flag set to true.
refusal_conditions: If write access is required beyond setting 'acknowledged' flags.

steps.action: Communicate planned maintenance window to relevant stakeholders
steps.verify: Stakeholders confirm receipt of maintenance notification
procedure: Generate a maintenance notification message template using maintenance_window_id and cluster_identifier.
Send the notification message to every identifier in stakeholder_list via notification_channel.
Record delivery status for each stakeholder.
validation_criteria: Delivery status exists for 100 percent of stakeholder_list entries.
stakeholder_acknowledgment_count is greater_or_equal_zero (no minimum threshold enforced by executor).
refusal_conditions: If notification_channel is not one of the predefined allowed values.

steps.action: Drain nodes scheduled for maintenance to safely evict workloads
steps.verify: All affected pods are rescheduled and running on healthy nodes
procedure: For each node in node_list_for_maintenance, cordon the node to prevent new pod scheduling.
For each cordoned node, initiate a drain operation with drain_timeout_seconds_per_node and pod disruption budgets honored.
After drain completion for each node, record list of pods that were evicted and their new node assignments.
validation_criteria: All nodes in node_list_for_maintenance are in 'cordoned' state.
All pods previously running on nodes in node_list_for_maintenance are now in 'Running' state on nodes not in node_list_for_maintenance.
No pod is left in 'Pending', 'Unknown', 'CrashLoopBackOff', or 'Error' state as a result of the drain.
refusal_conditions: If any node in node_list_for_maintenance does not exist in the cluster.
If validation_criteria cannot be met without using forbidden_operations.

steps.action: Apply approved configuration updates or patches to cluster components
steps.verify: Configuration history reflects successful update and no error events are present
procedure: For each preapproved_change_id, retrieve associated configuration_artifact identifier.
Apply each configuration_artifact to the cluster using a non-destructive apply operation.
Record the result of each apply operation including success or failure status.
validation_criteria: Every preapproved_change_id has a corresponding apply result recorded.
failed_change_count == 0
No new events with severity 'Error' are recorded in the cluster event logs within 5 minutes after the last apply.
refusal_conditions: If any change to be applied is not in preapproved_change_ids.
If event logs are not accessible for post-apply validation.

steps.action: Monitor cluster events and logs for anomalies during maintenance
steps.verify: No unexpected warning or error events recorded in cluster logs
procedure: From the start of this step, continuously read cluster event logs for monitoring_duration_minutes.
Filter events with severity 'Warning' or 'Error' occurring during this interval.
Record all such events in a monitoring summary record.
validation_criteria: error_event_count == 0
warning_event_count == 0
refusal_conditions: If event logs are not accessible for the entire monitoring_duration_minutes.

steps.action: Validate that critical system workloads remain available and healthy
steps.verify: Availability dashboards show all key services are operational
procedure: For each workload in critical_workload_identifiers, query availability_dashboard for current status.
Record status, error rate, and latency metrics for each workload.
validation_criteria: Each critical workload reports status 'Healthy' or equivalent.
Error rate for each workload is less than or equal to 1 percent.
P95 latency for each workload is less than or equal to a predefined threshold stored externally and read-only.

steps.action: Re-enable workloads on maintained nodes and confirm normal scheduling
steps.verify: Pods are running and workloads are distributed as expected
procedure: For each node in node_list_for_maintenance, uncordon the node.
After uncordoning all nodes, record the scheduling distribution of pods across all nodes.
Compare current distribution with baseline distribution recorded before maintenance if available.
validation_criteria: All nodes in node_list_for_maintenance are in 'schedulable' state.
All pods scheduled onto maintained nodes are in 'Running' state.
No node exceeds 90 percent CPU or memory utilization after rescheduling.
refusal_conditions: If any node in node_list_for_maintenance cannot be uncordoned without violating invariants.
If pod status cannot be read for the cluster.

steps.action: Update cluster documentation with details of maintenance actions
steps.verify: Maintenance record is complete and accessible in documentation repository
procedure: Create or update a maintenance entry in documentation_repository keyed by maintenance_window_id.
Attach or reference all referenced_record_ids in the maintenance entry.
Set maintenance entry status to 'Completed'.
validation_criteria: maintenance_log_record_id is retrievable from documentation_repository.
All referenced_record_ids are present in the maintenance entry.

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of maintenance completion and share summary report
steps.verify: Stakeholders acknowledge maintenance completion and report receipt
procedure: Generate a completion notification message including maintenance_window_id and maintenance_log_record_id.
Send the completion notification to every identifier in stakeholder_list via notification_channel.
Record delivery and acknowledgment status for each stakeholder.
validation_criteria: Delivery status exists for 100 percent of stakeholder_list entries.

abort_conditions: Verification of cluster health fails at any step
Unexpected critical error or alert is detected
Stakeholder communication is not confirmed

name: Track Project Delivery Status
description: The project manager reviews the current status of all active projects, updates delivery timelines, and communicates progress to stakeholders. This process ensures transparency, identifies potential risks, and keeps all parties informed about milestones and deadlines.

invariants: No source-of-record project data is modified.
No new projects are created or deleted.
refusal_conditions: If requested operation requires write access to source-of-record systems.

steps.action: Access the project documentation repository to review current project delivery records
steps.verify: Project documentation is accessible and displays active project entries
procedure: Connect to project_documentation_repository.
Execute active_project_filter to retrieve active project entries.
Store the retrieved list as the working set of projects.
validation_criteria: Connection to project_documentation_repository succeeds.
active_project_list_record_id is retrievable.
active_project_count is greater_or_equal_zero.

steps.action: Read the latest status updates submitted by project teams
steps.verify: Latest status updates are visible and timestamped
procedure: For each project in active_project_list_record_id, read the most recent status update entry.
Record the status text, status category, and timestamp for each project.
validation_criteria: Each project in the active project list has either a status update entry or is explicitly marked as 'no_status_available'.
Each status update entry includes a timestamp field.
refusal_conditions: If status update data store is not reachable.

steps.action: Identify any discrepancies or missing updates in project timelines
steps.verify: List of discrepancies or missing information is generated
procedure: For each project, compare the timestamp of the latest status update with a predefined recency threshold stored externally.
Mark projects with no status update or with status update older than the threshold as having missing or stale updates.
Compile a list of such projects as discrepancies.
validation_criteria: Each project is either present in discrepancy_list_record_id or explicitly marked as 'no_discrepancy'.
refusal_conditions: If recency threshold configuration cannot be read.

steps.action: Compile a summary of upcoming project milestones and associated deadlines
steps.verify: Milestone summary document is created and includes all relevant deadlines
procedure: For each project, read milestone and deadline data from the documentation repository.
Select milestones with deadlines within a predefined future time window stored externally.
Create a structured list of upcoming milestones and deadlines.
validation_criteria: milestone_summary_document_id is retrievable.
Each entry includes project identifier, milestone name, and deadline date.
refusal_conditions: If milestone data is not accessible for any project.

steps.action: Review risk and issue logs for any flags impacting delivery
steps.verify: Risk and issue entries are present and up to date
procedure: For each project, read associated risk and issue log entries.
Record severity, status, and last-updated timestamp for each entry.
validation_criteria: Each risk and issue entry includes a severity and last-updated timestamp.
refusal_conditions: If risk and issue logs are not accessible.

steps.action: Update the project delivery dashboard with current progress information
steps.verify: Dashboard reflects latest project statuses and progress metrics
procedure: Create a local, read-only dashboard view combining status, milestones, and risk/issue data.
Do not write any data back to source-of-record systems.
validation_criteria: project_delivery_dashboard_view_id is retrievable.
All active projects are represented in the dashboard view.
refusal_conditions: If any of the required input records are missing.

steps.action: Draft a communication summary for stakeholders highlighting key changes and progress
steps.verify: Draft communication is prepared and ready for review
procedure: Generate a structured summary including per-project status category, upcoming milestones, and high-severity risks.
Store the summary as a draft communication artifact.
validation_criteria: stakeholder_update_draft_id is retrievable.
Draft includes at least one section for 'Overall Status', 'Upcoming Milestones', and 'Key Risks'.

steps.action: Share the updated summary and dashboard with relevant stakeholders
steps.verify: Stakeholders receive the update and confirm receipt
procedure: Attach stakeholder_update_draft_id and a link or reference to project_delivery_dashboard_view_id to a communication message.
Send the message to every identifier in stakeholder_distribution_list using a predefined communication channel stored externally.
Record delivery and acknowledgment status for each stakeholder.
validation_criteria: Delivery status exists for 100 percent of stakeholder_distribution_list entries.
refusal_conditions: If communication channel configuration is not available.

abort_conditions: Project documentation is not accessible
Required status updates are missing or incomplete

name: Debug Production Network Issue
description: The site reliability engineer investigates and troubleshoots a production network issue after being alerted to connectivity problems impacting live services. This process involves checking system health, isolating network faults, coordinating with relevant teams, and documenting findings. The goal is to restore normal network operations efficiently and safely.

invariants: All destructive operations must have an associated approved change or incident record.
All actions must be scoped to resources tagged as production.
refusal_conditions: If manager approval is not present for any operation marked as destructive.

steps.action: Acknowledge the network alert and review incident details in the monitoring dashboard
steps.verify: Alert status is acknowledged and incident record is updated
procedure: Access monitoring_dashboard_identifier.
Locate alert associated with incident_id.
Set alert status to 'acknowledged' in the incident record.
Read and record incident details including timestamps and affected resources.
validation_criteria: Alert associated with incident_id has status 'acknowledged'.
incident_record_update_id is retrievable.

steps.action: Notify relevant stakeholders and assemble an incident response bridge
steps.verify: Stakeholders are present and incident bridge is active
procedure: Create or join an incident response bridge associated with incident_id.
Send invitations to all entries in stakeholder_list via communication_channel.
Record join status for each stakeholder.
validation_criteria: incident_bridge_identifier is retrievable.

steps.action: Review recent network changes or deployments for potential impact
steps.verify: Change log is reviewed and potential correlations are noted
procedure: Access change_log_system_identifier.
Retrieve all changes affecting network_scope_identifier within a predefined time window stored externally.
Record each change with timestamp and description.
validation_criteria: network_change_snapshot_record_id is retrievable.

steps.action: Assess current network health through dashboards and logs
steps.verify: Network metrics and logs are collected and documented
procedure: Access health_dashboard_identifier and read key metrics (latency, packet loss, throughput) for network_scope_identifier.
Access log_system_identifier and read recent error and warning logs for network_scope_identifier.
Store collected metrics and logs in a health assessment record.
validation_criteria: network_health_assessment_record_id is retrievable.
refusal_conditions: If either health_dashboard_identifier or log_system_identifier is not reachable.

steps.action: Identify the scope of affected systems and services
steps.verify: List of impacted systems and services is compiled
procedure: From the health assessment record, extract all systems and services showing degraded metrics or errors.
Compile a list of impacted systems and services.
validation_criteria: impacted_systems_list_record_id is retrievable.

steps.action: Isolate network segments or resources showing abnormal behavior
steps.verify: Problematic segments are identified and isolated in documentation
procedure: From impacted_systems_list_record_id, determine associated network segments or resources.
Mark these segments or resources as 'suspect' in the incident documentation only (no configuration changes).
validation_criteria: suspect_segments_record_id is retrievable.

steps.action: Request manager approval for any potentially disruptive investigative actions
steps.verify: Approval is granted and logged in the incident record
procedure: Create an approval request entry in the incident record including planned_disruptive_actions_description.
Route the approval request to manager_approver_identifier.
Record approval decision and timestamp in the incident record.
validation_criteria: approval_status is 'approved' for any subsequent destructive actions to proceed.

steps.action: Perform controlled tests to pinpoint the failure source
steps.verify: Test results are documented and shared with the team
procedure: Execute predefined non-destructive diagnostic tests for network_scope_identifier.
If any test is marked as destructive in configuration, ensure it is associated with approval_record_id and approved.
Record all test commands executed and their results.
validation_criteria: diagnostic_test_results_record_id is retrievable.
refusal_conditions: If approval_record_id does not indicate 'approved' for any required destructive tests.

steps.action: Coordinate with other teams as needed for cross-system impact analysis
steps.verify: Cross-team communication is logged and findings are updated
procedure: For each contact in cross_team_contact_list, send a request for impact analysis including incident_id and current findings.
Record responses and any identified cross-system impacts in the incident record.
validation_criteria: cross_team_communication_record_id is retrievable.

steps.action: Implement mitigations or workarounds to restore connectivity
steps.verify: Connectivity is restored and validated via monitoring
procedure: Retrieve the predefined mitigation steps associated with selected_mitigation_plan_identifier.
For each step marked as destructive, confirm it is covered by approval_record_id with status 'approved'.
Apply mitigation steps in the documented order.
Record each applied step and its outcome.
validation_criteria: Post-mitigation network metrics from monitoring show connectivity restored according to predefined thresholds stored externally.
refusal_conditions: If approval_record_id does not indicate 'approved' for required destructive steps.

steps.action: Monitor network stability for regression or recurring issues
steps.verify: No further alerts or anomalies in network dashboards
procedure: For monitoring_duration_minutes, periodically read key metrics and alerts from health_dashboard_identifier for network_scope_identifier.
Record any new alerts or anomalies in a stability monitoring record.
validation_criteria: regression_alert_count == 0
refusal_conditions: If health_dashboard_identifier is not reachable for the entire monitoring_duration_minutes.

steps.action: Update incident documentation with root cause analysis and resolution steps
steps.verify: Post-incident documentation is completed and accessible
procedure: In the incident record for incident_id, add a section summarizing root cause analysis using diagnostic_test_results_record_id.
Add a section describing resolution steps using mitigation_actions_record_id.
Add a section describing post-resolution monitoring using stability_monitoring_record_id.
Mark incident documentation status as 'completed'.
validation_criteria: updated_incident_record_id is retrievable.
Incident record status is 'completed'.
refusal_conditions: If incident_id record is not accessible.

steps.action: Communicate resolution and next steps to stakeholders
steps.verify: Stakeholders confirm receipt of status update
procedure: Generate a resolution summary message including incident_id and a reference to updated_incident_record_id.
Send the message to all entries in stakeholder_list via communication_channel.
Record delivery and acknowledgment status for each stakeholder.
validation_criteria: Delivery status exists for 100 percent of stakeholder_list entries.

abort_conditions: Manager approval is denied for required actions
Verification of network restoration fails
Incident is escalated beyond current team capability

name: Resolve Customer Escalation
description: A customer support agent addresses a high-priority escalation raised by a customer. The process involves gathering information, updating the ticket, coordinating with internal teams, and ensuring the customer receives a timely and accurate response. The agent follows internal protocols to maintain data confidentiality and provides evidence of resolution.

invariants: Customer personal data must not be copied to external systems.
All actions must be logged in the ticketing system.
refusal_conditions: If requested action requires access beyond support-tools access_tier.
If ticketing system is not reachable.

steps.action: Acknowledge receipt of the escalated ticket in the ticketing system
steps.verify: Ticket status updated to 'In Progress' and acknowledgment timestamp recorded
procedure: Open ticket identified by ticket_id in the ticketing system.
Set ticket status field to 'In Progress'.
Record current timestamp in an acknowledgment field.
validation_criteria: Ticket status equals 'In Progress'.
Acknowledgment timestamp field is populated.
refusal_conditions: If ticketing system is not reachable.

steps.action: Review all historical communications and notes related to the escalation
steps.verify: All relevant ticket notes and communication logs are visible and reviewed
procedure: Retrieve all historical comments, emails, and chat logs linked to ticket_id.
Display or store them in a read-only view for reference.
validation_criteria: historical_communications_view_id is retrievable.
refusal_conditions: If historical communications cannot be retrieved.

steps.action: Identify the specific issue and customer impact from the ticket details
steps.verify: Issue summary and impact statement documented in the ticket
procedure: From ticket fields and historical_communications_view_id, extract problem description and impact details.
Create or update structured fields in the ticket for 'Issue Summary' and 'Customer Impact' with the extracted information.
validation_criteria: Ticket contains non-empty 'Issue Summary' field.
Ticket contains non-empty 'Customer Impact' field.
refusal_conditions: If ticket fields cannot be updated within allowed operations.

steps.action: Gather additional context or clarification from the customer via approved channels
steps.verify: Customer response or confirmation attached to the ticket
procedure: Compose a clarification request message using approved templates.
Send the message to the customer via customer_contact_channel.
When a response is received, automatically or manually attach it to ticket_id as a new communication entry.
validation_criteria: customer_clarification_request_id is retrievable.
refusal_conditions: If customer_contact_channel is not supported by the ticketing system.

steps.action: Consult the internal knowledge base for potential solutions or similar cases
steps.verify: Relevant knowledge base articles or similar resolution references added to ticket
procedure: Access knowledge_base_identifier.
Search for articles using keywords from the ticket 'Issue Summary'.
Select articles that match search criteria and link them to ticket_id as internal references.
validation_criteria: If linked_article_count > 0, each linked article reference is visible in the ticket.

steps.action: Collaborate with internal technical or product teams for expert input if needed
steps.verify: Collaboration evidence (internal comments, handoff notes) present in the ticket
procedure: Create an internal comment or sub-task in the ticketing system addressed to the relevant team using internal_collaboration_channel.
Include ticket_id and a brief description of requested assistance.
Record any responses or handoff notes as internal comments on the ticket.
validation_criteria: collaboration_record_id is retrievable.

steps.action: Document proposed resolution steps in the ticket and escalate for manager approval
steps.verify: Proposed action and escalation for approval logged in the ticket
procedure: Add an internal comment to ticket_id describing proposed resolution steps.
Create an approval request entry in the ticket addressed to manager_identifier.
validation_criteria: resolution_proposal_record_id is retrievable.
refusal_conditions: If manager_identifier is not valid in the system.

steps.action: Obtain manager approval for the proposed resolution
steps.verify: Manager approval status and comments reflected in the ticket
procedure: Route the approval request associated with resolution_proposal_record_id to manager_identifier.
Record manager decision (approved or denied) and any comments in the ticket.
validation_criteria: manager_approval_status is present in the ticket.
Manager comments field is populated if status is 'denied'.
refusal_conditions: If manager_approval_status is 'denied' for actions requiring approval.

steps.action: Communicate the approved resolution to the customer through official support channels
steps.verify: Customer notification and content of communication logged in the ticket
procedure: Generate a customer-facing message describing the approved resolution.
Send the message via customer_contact_channel.
Log the message content and timestamp in the ticket as a customer communication entry.
validation_criteria: customer_resolution_notification_id is retrievable.
Ticket shows a new customer communication entry with the resolution content.

steps.action: Update the ticket status to reflect progress and next steps
steps.verify: Ticket status updated and visible to all stakeholders
procedure: Set ticket status to a value consistent with internal policy for 'Resolution Provided' (for example 'Pending Customer').
Ensure status change is logged with timestamp.
validation_criteria: ticket_status_after_step_10 is one of the allowed intermediate statuses configured externally.
refusal_conditions: If status update would violate internal status transition rules.

steps.action: Monitor for customer response or confirmation of issue resolution
steps.verify: Customer confirmation or follow-up received and documented in the ticket
procedure: For monitoring_duration_hours, periodically check ticket_id for new customer responses.
When a response is detected, log it as a communication entry and mark whether it indicates confirmation or further issues.
validation_criteria: If customer_confirmation_present is true, ticket contains a communication entry explicitly marked as confirmation.
refusal_conditions: If ticketing system is not reachable during monitoring period.

steps.action: Close the ticket after customer confirmation and document final resolution
steps.verify: Ticket status set to 'Closed' with resolution summary available
procedure: If customer_confirmation_present is true, set ticket status to 'Closed'.
Ensure a final resolution summary is present in the ticket.
Log closure timestamp.
validation_criteria: Ticket status equals 'Closed'.
Resolution summary field is non-empty.
refusal_conditions: If customer_confirmation_present is false and policy requires confirmation before closure.

abort_conditions: Manager approval is denied
Customer requests escalation to another party
Verification of customer issue or resolution fails

name: Quarterly Compliance Reporting
description: A legal counsel prepares and submits the quarterly compliance report to ensure the organization meets regulatory requirements. The process involves data gathering, internal review, and submission through the appropriate systems with documented approvals for accountability.

invariants: All report drafts and final reports must be stored in docs_wiki or designated compliance repository.
No source-of-record compliance data is altered.
refusal_conditions: If required regulatory requirement sources are not accessible.
If manager approval cannot be obtained where required.

steps.action: Review regulatory requirements for the reporting period
steps.verify: Reference regulatory checklist is up to date and acknowledged
procedure: Access regulatory_checklist_identifier.
Confirm that the checklist is labeled for reporting_period_identifier.
Record an acknowledgment entry indicating the checklist has been reviewed.
validation_criteria: regulatory_checklist_acknowledgment_id is retrievable.
Checklist label matches reporting_period_identifier.
refusal_conditions: If checklist label does not match reporting_period_identifier.

steps.action: Gather relevant compliance data from internal stakeholders
steps.verify: All required data is received and logged in the compliance tracker
procedure: Access compliance_tracker_identifier.
Retrieve list of required data items for reporting_period_identifier.
For each required data item, read the corresponding value from internal stakeholders' systems or submissions and log it in the compliance tracker.
validation_criteria: Each required data item has a corresponding entry in compliance_data_collection_record_id marked as 'present' or 'missing'.

steps.action: Compile initial draft of the compliance report
steps.verify: Draft document is created and stored in the docs_wiki with proper versioning
procedure: Create a new draft document in target_system for reporting_period_identifier.
Populate the draft with data from compliance_data_collection_record_id.
Enable versioning for the draft document.
validation_criteria: draft_report_document_id is retrievable in target_system.
Versioning is enabled for draft_report_document_id.

steps.action: Cross-check data accuracy against source documentation
steps.verify: All data points are validated and discrepancies are documented
procedure: For each data point in draft_report_document_id, compare with the corresponding entry in compliance_data_collection_record_id and its source documentation reference.
Mark each data point as 'validated' or 'discrepant'.
Record all discrepancies in a discrepancy log attached to the draft report.
validation_criteria: Every data point in the draft report has a validation status.
All discrepancies are listed in data_validation_log_id.
refusal_conditions: If source documentation for required data points is not accessible.

steps.action: Share draft report with compliance team for internal review
steps.verify: Compliance team review comments are collected in the document
procedure: Grant read/comment access to draft_report_document_id for all entries in compliance_team_distribution_list.
Send notification to compliance_team_distribution_list with a link to draft_report_document_id.
Collect review comments directly in the document or associated comment system.
validation_criteria: review_comment_collection_id is retrievable.

steps.action: Address feedback and update the report as necessary
steps.verify: All feedback items are resolved and documented in the revision log
procedure: For each comment in review_comment_collection_id, apply appropriate edits to draft_report_document_id within allowed operations.
Mark each comment as 'resolved' or 'not_applicable' with justification.
Update the document revision log with a summary of changes.
validation_criteria: Each comment in review_comment_collection_id has a resolution status.
revision_log_id is retrievable.

steps.action: Submit the finalized report to the manager for approval
steps.verify: Manager approval is documented in the docs_wiki approval workflow
procedure: Change draft_report_document_id status to 'Ready for Approval' in docs_wiki.
Initiate an approval workflow assigning manager_identifier as approver.
Record the approval request identifier.
validation_criteria: approval_request_id is retrievable.
refusal_conditions: If approval workflow cannot be initiated.

steps.action: Archive the approved report in the designated compliance repository
steps.verify: Archived report is timestamped and access logged
procedure: Confirm that approval_request_id status is 'approved' in the approval workflow.
Copy or move the approved report from docs_wiki to compliance_repository_identifier as the final report.
Record archive timestamp and access control list.
validation_criteria: approval_request_id status is 'approved'.
archived_report_record_id and final_report_document_id are retrievable in compliance_repository_identifier.

steps.action: Notify relevant stakeholders of report completion
steps.verify: Stakeholder notification is sent and receipt is confirmed
procedure: Compose a notification message including reporting_period_identifier and a link or reference to final_report_document_id.
Send the message to all entries in stakeholder_distribution_list via an approved internal communication channel.
Record delivery and acknowledgment status for each stakeholder.
validation_criteria: Delivery status exists for 100 percent of stakeholder_distribution_list entries.
refusal_conditions: If internal communication channel is not available.

abort_conditions: Manager approval is denied
Required compliance data is incomplete
Discrepancies in source data cannot be resolved

name: Quarterly Roadmap Update
description: The Product Manager prepares and updates the product roadmap for the upcoming quarter. This involves gathering input from stakeholders, prioritizing features, and ensuring the roadmap is accurately reflected in the documentation system. The process ensures alignment between teams and supports effective communication to leadership.

refusal_conditions: Requested operation requires write access to systems other than docs_wiki
Requested operation requires modification of production runtime systems or code repositories
invariants: Executor must not modify any system other than docs_wiki
Executor must not change user permissions or access controls
Executor must not delete any existing roadmap documents

steps.action: Collect feedback from stakeholders regarding current roadmap progress and priorities
steps.verify: Stakeholder meeting notes and feedback summary are documented
inputs_required: Access to existing roadmap document in docs_wiki
expected_outputs: A single feedback_summary document stored in docs_wiki or equivalent internal documentation system
Feedback_summary document contains at least one entry per stakeholder identifier provided in inputs_required
validation_criteria: Feedback_summary document exists and is accessible in docs_wiki
Feedback_summary document includes a section labeled 'Stakeholder Feedback'
Each stakeholder identifier from inputs_required appears at least once in the document
guardrails: Do not modify or delete any existing roadmap documents
Do not send notifications or messages through external communication tools
If any required input is missing, stop execution of this step and mark it as failed without attempting to infer or substitute values
refusal_conditions: docs_wiki is not accessible in read mode
Operation would require editing existing roadmap documents

steps.action: Review product analytics and user feedback to identify emerging needs
steps.verify: Analysis report and key insights are attached to the roadmap update documentation
inputs_required: Read access to product analytics dashboards
Read access to user feedback repository
Identifier or link to the roadmap update documentation location in docs_wiki
expected_outputs: An analysis_report document stored in docs_wiki
Analysis_report document contains a section labeled 'Emerging Needs'
Roadmap update documentation in docs_wiki contains a link or attachment reference to analysis_report
validation_criteria: Analysis_report exists in docs_wiki
Analysis_report includes at least one item under 'Emerging Needs'
Roadmap update documentation contains a verifiable reference (URL or document ID) to analysis_report
guardrails: Do not modify or delete any analytics or feedback source data
Do not overwrite existing roadmap content; only append references or a clearly delimited section
If analytics or feedback systems are unavailable, do not fabricate data; mark step as failed
refusal_conditions: No access to user feedback repository
docs_wiki is not accessible in write mode for creating analysis_report

steps.action: Draft proposed changes and new initiatives for the next quarter
steps.verify: Draft roadmap document is created and versioned
expected_outputs: A new draft_roadmap document stored in docs_wiki
Draft_roadmap document name or metadata includes the upcoming quarter identifier (e.g., 'Q1', 'Q2', 'Q3', 'Q4') and year
Draft_roadmap document includes sections 'Proposed Changes' and 'New Initiatives'
validation_criteria: Draft_roadmap document exists in docs_wiki
Document metadata or title contains a quarter identifier and a four-digit year
Sections 'Proposed Changes' and 'New Initiatives' are present and non-empty
guardrails: Do not modify the existing official roadmap document
Do not publish or mark the draft as 'official' or 'final'
If quarter or year cannot be determined from inputs, use a placeholder 'TBD' and record this explicitly
refusal_conditions: docs_wiki does not allow creation of new documents
Existing roadmap document cannot be read

steps.action: Share the draft roadmap with cross-functional teams for review
steps.verify: Review comments are collected and documented
inputs_required: List of cross-functional team identifiers or mailing list identifiers
expected_outputs: A review_request entry or section in docs_wiki referencing draft_roadmap
A comments_log document or section in docs_wiki that records all received comments
validation_criteria: Comments_log exists in docs_wiki
Comments_log contains at least one entry or an explicit statement 'No comments received by cutoff time'
guardrails: Do not send actual messages; only record that sharing is required and log intended recipients
Do not access message content; only use metadata if available
If no team identifiers are provided, record this and mark step as failed
refusal_conditions: docs_wiki is not accessible for creating or updating documents

steps.action: Incorporate feedback from teams into the draft roadmap
steps.verify: Revised roadmap reflects consensus and addresses raised concerns
expected_outputs: A revised_roadmap document in docs_wiki created as a new version or copy of draft_roadmap
A change_log section in revised_roadmap summarizing which comments were addressed
validation_criteria: Revised_roadmap exists in docs_wiki
Change_log section references at least one comment from comments_log or explicitly states 'No comments to address'
guardrails: Do not delete or overwrite draft_roadmap; always create a new document for revisions
If comments_log is empty or missing, record this fact in change_log
Do not attempt to interpret 'consensus'; simply record which comments were incorporated
refusal_conditions: docs_wiki does not allow creation of a new document

steps.action: Present the updated roadmap to leadership for final alignment
steps.verify: Leadership meeting notes and approval status are documented
expected_outputs: Leadership_review_notes includes a field 'approval_status' with value 'approved', 'rejected', or 'pending'
validation_criteria: Leadership_review_notes exists in docs_wiki
Approval_status field is present and set to one of the allowed values
guardrails: Do not schedule or modify calendar events; only read metadata if available
Do not assume approval; approval_status must be explicitly set based on provided input, not inferred
If no approval information is available, set approval_status to 'pending' and record this
refusal_conditions: docs_wiki is not accessible for creating leadership_review_notes

steps.action: Update the official roadmap in the docs wiki system
steps.verify: Docs wiki displays the latest roadmap version with correct dates and features
inputs_required: Identifier of the current official roadmap document
expected_outputs: Official roadmap document updated or replaced to reflect content of revised_roadmap
Document metadata updated to indicate current quarter and year
validation_criteria: Official roadmap document last-modified timestamp is later than revised_roadmap creation timestamp
Official roadmap document contains the same or superset of sections present in revised_roadmap
guardrails: Only perform this step if approval_status equals 'approved'; otherwise record 'not executed due to lack of approval' and do not modify the official roadmap
Do not delete the official roadmap document; only update its content
If quarter or year cannot be determined, retain existing metadata and record this in a note section
refusal_conditions: docs_wiki does not allow updates to the official roadmap document

steps.action: Communicate roadmap changes to all relevant teams
steps.verify: Announcement or summary is distributed via internal communication channels
inputs_required: List of relevant team identifiers or distribution lists
expected_outputs: A communication_summary document in docs_wiki describing intended announcement content and target recipients
validation_criteria: Communication_summary exists in docs_wiki
Communication_summary lists at least one recipient or explicitly states 'No recipients provided'
guardrails: Do not send actual announcements; only document the content and intended recipients
Do not access or store message bodies from communication tools; only record identifiers if provided
If no team identifiers are supplied, record this and mark step as failed
refusal_conditions: docs_wiki is not accessible for creating communication_summary

abort_conditions: Leadership does not approve the proposed roadmap
Stakeholder consensus cannot be reached
Documentation system is inaccessible

name: Prepare Production Deployment
description: A site reliability engineer is preparing for a scheduled production deployment. This includes validating deployment artifacts, coordinating with relevant teams, and ensuring that rollback and monitoring plans are in place. The process is governed by safe write constraints and requires manager approval before execution.

refusal_conditions: Requested operation requires direct modification of production application code outside of CI/CD configuration
Requested operation requires destructive changes to production data
invariants: Executor must not deploy to production; only prepare for deployment
Executor must not modify database schemas or production data
Executor must not bypass or alter approval records

steps.action: Review deployment ticket for completeness and approvals
steps.verify: Deployment ticket status is 'Ready' and all required fields are filled
inputs_required: Definition of required fields for deployment tickets
Definition of allowed ready statuses (must include 'Ready')
expected_outputs: A validation_log entry attached to the deployment ticket summarizing completeness check results
validation_criteria: Ticket status field equals 'Ready'
All fields listed in 'required fields' definition are present and non-empty
guardrails: Do not change ticket status or any existing field values
If any required field is missing or empty, record this in validation_log and mark step as failed
Do not infer or auto-populate missing ticket data
refusal_conditions: Ticketing system is not accessible in read mode

steps.action: Validate deployment artifacts for integrity and version alignment
steps.verify: Artifact checksums and version tags match release documentation
inputs_required: List of artifact identifiers and expected checksums
Release documentation identifier containing expected version tags
expected_outputs: An artifact_validation_report stored in the ticket or CI/CD system summarizing checksum and version comparisons
validation_criteria: For each artifact identifier, the actual checksum equals the expected checksum
For each artifact identifier, the version tag matches the version specified in release documentation
guardrails: Do not modify or rebuild artifacts
If any checksum or version mismatch is found, record details in artifact_validation_report and mark step as failed
Do not proceed to modify any deployment configuration based on mismatched artifacts
refusal_conditions: Artifact repository is not accessible in read mode

steps.action: Check that automated tests have passed for the release candidate
steps.verify: CI/CD pipeline reports all tests as successful
inputs_required: CI/CD pipeline run identifier for the release candidate
Definition of required test stages (e.g., unit, integration, regression)
expected_outputs: A test_status_summary attached to the deployment ticket or stored in CI/CD system
validation_criteria: All required test stages have status 'success' or equivalent
No tests in required stages are marked as 'failed' or 'skipped'
guardrails: Do not re-run or cancel pipelines
If any required stage is missing or not successful, record this in test_status_summary and mark step as failed
Do not override or alter test results
refusal_conditions: CI/CD system is not accessible in read mode

steps.action: Coordinate deployment schedule with product and QA teams
steps.verify: Deployment window is confirmed and communicated in team channels
inputs_required: Proposed deployment window (start and end timestamps)
Identifiers for product and QA team communication channels
expected_outputs: A deployment_schedule_record stored in ticketing or change management system documenting the agreed window
validation_criteria: Deployment_schedule_record includes start and end timestamps
Deployment_schedule_record lists product and QA teams as notified parties
guardrails: Do not send actual messages; only record intended communication
Do not modify existing change records except by appending a new comment or field where allowed
If proposed deployment window is missing, record this and mark step as failed
refusal_conditions: Change management or ticketing system is not accessible
No communication channel identifiers are provided

steps.action: Confirm rollback plan is documented and accessible
steps.verify: Rollback instructions are available in the deployment runbook
inputs_required: Definition of required rollback sections (e.g., 'Rollback Steps', 'Verification After Rollback')
expected_outputs: A rollback_plan_check_entry appended to the deployment ticket or runbook metadata
validation_criteria: Runbook document exists and is accessible
All required rollback sections are present and non-empty
guardrails: Do not modify rollback instructions content
If any required section is missing or empty, record this in rollback_plan_check_entry and mark step as failed
Do not create new rollback procedures

steps.action: Verify monitoring and alerting rules are updated for the new release
steps.verify: Monitoring dashboard reflects new release metrics and alerting thresholds
inputs_required: Monitoring dashboard identifier for the service
List of expected new metrics or alert names for the release
expected_outputs: A monitoring_readiness_report stored in ticketing or monitoring documentation
validation_criteria: Each expected metric or alert name exists in the monitoring system
Alert thresholds for critical alerts are defined (non-null values)
guardrails: Do not modify any monitoring or alerting configuration
If any expected metric or alert is missing, record this in monitoring_readiness_report and mark step as failed
Do not create or delete dashboards or alerts
refusal_conditions: Monitoring system is not accessible in read mode
No expected metrics or alerts list is provided

steps.action: Ensure required backups are current and validated
steps.verify: Backup logs confirm successful and recent backups
inputs_required: Backup job identifiers relevant to the deployment scope
Definition of 'recent' backup age threshold in hours
expected_outputs: A backup_status_report attached to the deployment ticket
validation_criteria: Each backup job has a last_successful_run timestamp within the defined 'recent' threshold
Each backup job has a status of 'success' or equivalent
guardrails: Do not trigger new backups
Do not modify backup configurations
If any backup is missing or not recent, record this in backup_status_report and mark step as failed
refusal_conditions: No backup job identifiers are provided

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of the impending deployment and expected impact
steps.verify: Stakeholder acknowledgement is received in communication channel
inputs_required: List of stakeholder communication channel identifiers
Deployment window and high-level impact description
expected_outputs: A stakeholder_notification_plan document or ticket comment describing intended notifications and recipients
validation_criteria: Stakeholder_notification_plan lists all provided stakeholder identifiers
Plan includes deployment window and impact description text
guardrails: Do not send actual notifications; only document the plan
Do not read or store message content; only metadata if available
If no stakeholder identifiers are provided, record this and mark step as failed

steps.action: Request manager approval to proceed with deployment
steps.verify: Manager approval is recorded in the deployment ticket
expected_outputs: An approval_request_entry appended to the deployment ticket indicating that manager approval is required and pending or granted
validation_criteria: Deployment ticket contains an approval_status field or comment with value 'approved', 'rejected', or 'pending'
guardrails: Do not set or change approval_status; only read and record it
If approval_status is not present, record status as 'pending' in approval_request_entry
Do not impersonate or simulate manager approval
refusal_conditions: Ticketing system is not accessible for appending comments

steps.action: Lock deployment window to prevent conflicting changes
steps.verify: Change management system reflects active deployment lock
inputs_required: Deployment window start and end timestamps
expected_outputs: A deployment_lock_entry in the change management record indicating the locked window
validation_criteria: Change record contains a field or entry indicating a lock from start to end timestamps
Lock entry is associated with the deployment identifier
guardrails: Do not modify or cancel other existing change records
Do not extend or shorten the deployment window beyond the provided timestamps
If change management system does not support locks, record this limitation and mark step as failed
refusal_conditions: Change management system is not accessible in write mode

steps.action: Document deployment steps and contact information for oncall support
steps.verify: Deployment runbook is updated and accessible to the team
inputs_required: List of deployment steps provided by release documentation
expected_outputs: Runbook updated with a new section labeled with the deployment identifier containing steps and contacts
validation_criteria: Runbook contains a section with the deployment identifier
Section lists at least one deployment step and one oncall contact
guardrails: Do not remove or alter existing runbook sections outside the new section
If deployment steps are not provided, record this and mark step as failed
Do not include personal contact details beyond identifiers already used internally
refusal_conditions: docs_wiki is not accessible in write mode

steps.action: Prepare post-deployment validation checklist
steps.verify: Checklist is reviewed and attached to the deployment ticket
inputs_required: List of services or components affected by the deployment
expected_outputs: A validation_checklist document created from the template and linked in the deployment ticket
validation_criteria: Validation_checklist contains at least one item per affected service or component
Deployment ticket includes a link or reference to validation_checklist
guardrails: Do not modify the standard template; only instantiate it
If affected services list is empty, record this and mark step as failed
Do not mark any checklist items as completed
refusal_conditions: docs_wiki is not accessible for creating documents

abort_conditions: Verification of critical steps fails
Manager approval is denied
Detected issues with artifact integrity or test results

name: Routine Database Maintenance
description: A senior backend developer performs scheduled maintenance on the production payments database to ensure optimal performance and address minor issues. This operation is conducted with read-only access, minimizing risk to critical data and services. The process follows internal guidelines for routine upkeep without making destructive changes.

refusal_conditions: Requested operation requires write access to any production database
Requested operation requires modification of monitoring or backup configurations
invariants: Executor must not execute any SQL statement that modifies data or schema
Executor must not change database user permissions
Executor must not trigger backup or restore operations

steps.action: Review the scheduled maintenance window and notify relevant stakeholders of the planned activity.
steps.verify: Stakeholders acknowledge receipt of notification and scheduled window is documented.
inputs_required: Maintenance window start and end timestamps
List of stakeholder identifiers or groups
expected_outputs: A maintenance_window_record stored in docs_wiki or ticketing system describing the window and stakeholders
validation_criteria: Maintenance_window_record includes start and end timestamps
Maintenance_window_record lists all provided stakeholders
guardrails: Do not send actual notifications; only document the intended notifications
Do not modify existing maintenance windows; only record the one provided
If timestamps are missing, record this and mark step as failed
refusal_conditions: No maintenance window timestamps are provided

steps.action: Access the production payments database using approved read-only credentials.
steps.verify: Access logs show successful authentication with read-only privileges.
expected_outputs: A connection_attempt_log entry in docs_wiki or ticketing describing connection success or failure
validation_criteria: Database connection is established in read-only mode
Access logs show authentication using the specified read-only credential
guardrails: Do not attempt to escalate privileges or switch to a write-capable user
If connection fails, record error details and mark step as failed
Do not store credentials in logs or documentation

steps.action: Check database health metrics and current performance indicators.
steps.verify: Monitoring dashboards display up-to-date health and performance data.
inputs_required: Monitoring dashboard identifier for the payments database
expected_outputs: A health_metrics_snapshot stored in docs_wiki or ticketing summarizing key indicators
validation_criteria: Health_metrics_snapshot includes timestamp of retrieval
Snapshot lists at least CPU usage, memory usage, connection count, and query latency metrics
guardrails: Do not modify any monitoring configuration
If dashboard is unavailable, record this and mark step as failed
Do not attempt to remediate performance issues; only observe and record

steps.action: Review recent error logs and query logs for signs of anomalies or degraded performance.
steps.verify: Log review summary is created and no critical anomalies are identified.
inputs_required: Time range for 'recent' logs in hours
expected_outputs: A log_review_summary document stored in docs_wiki or ticketing
validation_criteria: Log_review_summary includes the time range examined
Summary explicitly states whether any critical anomalies were observed
guardrails: Do not modify or delete logs
If logs are inaccessible, record this and mark step as failed
Do not attempt to classify anomalies beyond recording observed error rates and messages

steps.action: Run standard diagnostic queries to assess index usage and table statistics.
