name: Troubleshoot Employee Access Issue
description: An IT admin investigates and resolves an employee's report of being unable to access a corporate system. The process includes validating the user's identity, confirming permissions, and ensuring secure restoration of access. The goal is to efficiently restore service while maintaining compliance with internal data security policies.

invariants: no_permission_change_may_be_made_without_documented_manager_approval
all_identity_verification_steps_must_be_completed_before_account_changes
all_actions_must_be_logged_in_the_ticket

steps.action: Acknowledge the access issue ticket and review its details
steps.verify: Ticket status updated to 'In Progress' and key information is documented
validation_criteria: ticket_status_value_after_update_equals_in_progress
snapshot_record_contains_reporter_contact_information_and_issue_description
guardrails: do_not_change_ticket_priority_in_this_step
do_not_reassign_ticket_owner

steps.action: Contact the affected employee to confirm the specifics of the access problem
steps.verify: Direct confirmation from employee and documented description of issue
validation_criteria: ticket_contains_comment_referencing_contact_attempt
issue_description_in_ticket_reflects_employee_reported_symptoms
guardrails: do_not_request_sensitive_credentials_from_employee
do_not_share_internal_system_details_beyond_policy

steps.action: Validate the employee’s identity using standard verification methods
steps.verify: Identity confirmed per IT policy and logged in ticket
validation_criteria: identity_verification_log_references_employee_identifier_and_ticket_identifier
boolean_flag_identity_verified_is_true_only_if_all_required_checks_pass
guardrails: do_not_proceed_to_account_changes_if_boolean_flag_identity_verified_is_false
do_not_store_sensitive_verification_answers_in_plain_text

steps.action: Check the employee’s current account status in the IAM system
steps.verify: Account status (active, locked, suspended) is known and recorded
validation_criteria: ticket_contains_comment_or_field_with_account_status_value
account_status_log_references_iam_system_tenant_identifier
guardrails: do_not_modify_account_status_in_this_step
do_not_access_accounts_other_than_employee_identifier

steps.action: Review recent login attempts and error logs for the employee’s account
steps.verify: Log entries are reviewed and any anomalies or lockouts are noted
validation_criteria: snapshot_includes_all_login_attempts_for_employee_identifier_within_lookback_period
ticket_comment_references_any_lockout_or_error_codes_if_present
guardrails: do_not_modify_or_delete_log_entries
do_not_expand_search_to_other_users

steps.action: Confirm the employee’s group memberships and permission assignments
steps.verify: Group and permission lists are compared against role requirements
validation_criteria: comparison_result_indicates_for_each_required_group_and_permission_whether_present_or_missing
guardrails: do_not_modify_group_memberships_in_this_step
do_not_assign_new_roles

steps.action: Request manager approval for any required permission changes or account unlock
steps.verify: Approval is documented in the ticketing system
validation_criteria: ticket_contains_reference_to_approval_request_record_location
approval_status_value_is_one_of_pending_approved_or_denied
guardrails: do_not_execute_permission_changes_before_approval_status_is_approved
do_not_request_approval_for_changes_outside_scope_of_issue

steps.action: Apply approved changes in the IAM system (reset password, unlock account, adjust group memberships)
steps.verify: Changes are reflected in IAM system and audit logs updated
validation_criteria: iam_account_status_and_group_memberships_match_approved_change_set_definition
audit_log_contains_entries_for_each_change_with_reference_to_ticket_identifier
guardrails: do_not_change_global_security_policies
do_not_disable_mfa_or_other_security_controls

steps.action: Notify the employee of the resolution and next steps
steps.verify: Employee confirms ability to access required systems
validation_criteria: ticket_contains_comment_referencing_notification_message_identifier
employee_confirmation_log_indicates_access_restored_or_follow_up_required
guardrails: do_not_share_internal_admin_urls_or_credentials
do_not_send_notification_to_addresses_not_matching_employee_contact_identifier

steps.action: Update the ticket with actions taken and mark as resolved
steps.verify: Ticket is closed with a resolution summary
validation_criteria: ticket_history_contains_resolution_comment_referencing_all_items_in_list_of_actions_taken_with_timestamps
final_ticket_status_value_equals_resolved
guardrails: do_not_close_ticket_if_employee_confirmation_indicates_issue_persists
do_not_delete_or_modify_previous_ticket_comments

abort_conditions: Employee identity cannot be verified
Manager approval is denied
IAM system is unavailable

name: Release Candidate Validation
description: A QA engineer performs validation of a release candidate in the staging environment before deployment. The process ensures all critical features function as expected and no regressions are found. The intent includes manual and automated testing, documentation of results, and communication with relevant stakeholders.

preconditions: Release candidate build identifier is provided
Access to CI/CD and test reporting tools is available
invariants: No changes are made to production systems
All findings are recorded in the designated tracking system
All timestamps are recorded in UTC
refusal_conditions: Required tools or systems are not listed in allowed_systems

steps.action: Review the release notes and change logs for the candidate build
steps.verify: Release notes accurately reflect implemented changes and known issues are documented
validation_criteria: Every item in change_log is referenced in release_notes or explicitly marked as internal_only
Known issues section in release_notes is non-empty or explicitly marked as 'none'
review_summary_record_id is created in the tracking system

steps.action: Trigger automated test suites on the candidate build in the staging environment
steps.verify: Automated test reports show all critical and high priority tests pass
validation_criteria: All test_suite_identifier_list are executed against staging_environment_identifier
automated_test_report_location is generated and accessible
All tests tagged 'critical' or 'high' have status 'pass'

refusal_conditions: Any test suite requires production credentials

steps.action: Perform manual exploratory testing on new and updated features
steps.verify: Manual test results are documented and no critical issues are found
validation_criteria: Each feature in list_of_new_features and list_of_updated_features has at least one test entry in manual_test_session_log_id
All found issues are recorded in list_of_found_issues with severity
No issue with severity 'critical' is left without a tracking record

refusal_conditions: list_of_new_features is empty AND list_of_updated_features is empty

steps.action: Validate integration points with external and internal services
steps.verify: Integration test results confirm successful communication with all dependencies
validation_criteria: Each integration in list_of_integrations has at least one executed test case
integration_test_report_location is accessible
All tests for mandatory integrations have status 'pass'

steps.action: Check for performance regressions using standard test scenarios
steps.verify: Performance metrics are within acceptable thresholds compared to previous releases
validation_criteria: All standard performance_test_scenarios are executed
performance_comparison_report_location is generated
For each key metric, current_value <= baseline_value * 1.10

steps.action: Verify user interface consistency and accessibility standards
steps.verify: UI review checklist is completed and accessibility issues are logged or resolved
validation_criteria: completed_ui_review_checklist_location exists and is accessible
Each reviewed screen has a corresponding checklist entry
All identified accessibility issues are present in accessibility_issue_list with severity

steps.action: Confirm that rollback procedures are documented and tested
steps.verify: Rollback plan is present and a successful rollback test has been observed
validation_criteria: rollback_plan_document_location is accessible and not empty
rollback_test_evidence_location contains at least one successful rollback test record
rollback_verification_record_id is created with links to both locations

steps.action: Document any defects or blockers identified during testing
steps.verify: Defects are logged in the tracking system and communicated to the development team
validation_criteria: Each issue in list_of_found_issues has a corresponding entry in defect_record_ids
Each defect record includes severity, steps_to_reproduce, and environment='staging'
A single summary notification is sent to development_team_contact_channel referencing all defect_record_ids

refusal_conditions: development_team_contact_channel is not allowed in context.allowed_systems

steps.action: Compile the validation results and prepare a summary report
steps.verify: Summary report is reviewed and shared with stakeholders
validation_criteria: validation_summary_report_location is accessible
Report includes sections for scope, test_results, defects, risks, and recommendation
All referenced artifact locations are valid URLs or system paths

refusal_conditions: Any required input report location is missing or inaccessible

steps.action: Notify the release manager of the validation outcome and readiness status
steps.verify: Release manager receives notification and acknowledges the QA signoff
validation_criteria: Notification message includes link to validation_summary_report_location and overall_readiness_status
At least one acknowledgment from release_manager_contact_identifier is recorded as release_manager_acknowledgment_record_id

abort_conditions: Critical defects are discovered that block release
Automated tests fail for key functionality
Environment instability prevents completion of validation steps

name: Reset User Password
description: A support agent receives a request from an employee to reset their corporate account password due to a forgotten password. The agent must follow standard procedures to verify the user's identity, reset the password, and confirm access restoration, while ensuring compliance with internal security policies.

preconditions: User account identifier (e.g., username or email) is provided
Access to IAM system is available
invariants: All identity verification evidence is recorded in the ticket
Passwords are never visible to the agent in plain text
All timestamps are recorded in UTC
refusal_conditions: Identity verification cannot be completed according to policy
Request comes from an unverified communication channel

steps.action: Open the user’s support ticket and review the password reset request details
steps.verify: Ticket contains a clear password reset request from the user
validation_criteria: Ticket requester identifier is present
Ticket text contains an explicit password reset request indicator (e.g., category='password_reset' or tag='password_reset')
password_reset_request_detected_flag is set to true if such indicator exists

steps.action: Confirm the identity of the requester using approved verification methods
steps.verify: Identity verification status is documented in the ticket
validation_criteria: At least one method from allowed_verification_methods is used
identity_verification_status is set to 'passed' or 'failed'
identity_verification_log_entry_id is stored in the ticket with method and outcome

refusal_conditions: approved_verification_method_list contains methods not in allowed_verification_methods

steps.action: Acknowledge the request and inform the user of the reset process via official communication channel
steps.verify: User receives acknowledgment message
validation_criteria: Acknowledgment message references ticket_id
Message does not contain any password or temporary password
acknowledgment_message_id is recorded in the ticket

steps.action: Access the identity and access management (IAM) system using support tools
steps.verify: IAM system dashboard displays user account details
validation_criteria: iam_session_id is established
IAM dashboard main page is loaded without error

steps.action: Locate the user’s account in the IAM system
steps.verify: Correct user account is selected and visible
validation_criteria: Search results contain at least one entry matching user_account_identifier
iam_user_account_record_id corresponds to the entry whose primary identifier equals user_account_identifier

refusal_conditions: More than one exact match is found

steps.action: Initiate the password reset process for the user’s account
steps.verify: IAM system indicates password reset is in progress
validation_criteria: IAM system displays a status message indicating reset initiation for iam_user_account_record_id
password_reset_operation_id is captured from IAM audit log or UI

refusal_conditions: IAM UI requires manual password entry by agent

steps.action: Set a temporary password or trigger the password reset email to the user’s registered address
steps.verify: System logs show reset action and user receives reset instructions
validation_criteria: IAM audit log contains an entry for password_reset_operation_id with success status
If reset email is used, email_trigger_id is recorded and associated with user_registered_email_address
If temporary password is used, it is stored only in a secure, ephemeral field not visible after completion

refusal_conditions: user_registered_email_address is missing in IAM record
System requires sending password via insecure channel

steps.action: Document the reset action and confirmation details in the support ticket
steps.verify: Ticket contains timestamped reset action and notes
validation_criteria: Ticket update includes password_reset_operation_id and outcome (success/failure)
No actual password value is included in the ticket
ticket_update_entry_id is stored with current UTC timestamp

refusal_conditions: Attempt is made to include password value in ticket notes

steps.action: Notify the user that the password reset is complete and provide next steps for secure access
steps.verify: User confirms receipt of notification
validation_criteria: Notification references ticket_id and does not contain any password
reset_completion_notification_message_id is recorded in the ticket

steps.action: Request confirmation from the user that they have regained access to their account
steps.verify: User replies with confirmation of successful login
validation_criteria: At least one explicit question about successful login is sent
If user responds affirmatively, user_confirmation_status is set to 'confirmed'
user_confirmation_message_id is stored in the ticket when response is received

refusal_conditions: User requests additional access changes beyond password reset

steps.action: Close the support ticket after confirming access restoration
steps.verify: Ticket status is updated to closed and resolution is documented
validation_criteria: If user_confirmation_status is 'confirmed', ticket status is set to 'closed'
Resolution summary includes reference to password_reset_operation_id and user_confirmation_status
ticket_status_closed is recorded with UTC timestamp

abort_conditions: Identity verification fails
IAM system is unavailable
User does not respond to verification or reset notifications

name: Update Project Delivery Status
description: A project manager needs to update the current delivery status of an active project in the project tracking system. This ensures stakeholders are informed of progress, blockers, and next steps, supporting transparent program execution.

preconditions: Latest project updates are accessible from project_tracking_system or team reports
invariants: No changes are made outside documentation and communication tools
All status updates include a date and author
All timestamps are recorded in UTC
refusal_conditions: Project identifier cannot be resolved in project_tracking_system

steps.action: Review the latest project progress from team updates and reports
steps.verify: All updates are collected and documented in a summary
validation_criteria: All locations in list_of_team_update_locations are accessed successfully or marked as unavailable
raw_updates_collection_id contains references to all successfully accessed sources
progress_summary_draft_id includes key items: completed_work, in_progress_work, upcoming_work

steps.action: Summarize key milestones achieved since the last update
steps.verify: Milestone summary is ready for inclusion in the documentation
validation_criteria: milestone_summary_section_id lists only milestones with completion_date > previous_status_update_date
Each milestone entry includes name, completion_date, and owner

steps.action: Document current project status, including timeline and resource allocation
steps.verify: Project status section in the documentation is updated
validation_criteria: project_status_section_id includes fields: overall_status, schedule_status, scope_status, resource_status
Timeline information includes current_phase and next_major_date
Resource allocation is described at team or role level, not individual capacity percentages if policy forbids

refusal_conditions: docs_wiki_project_page_location is not editable by user_profile.id

steps.action: List any identified risks or blockers impacting delivery
steps.verify: Risks and blockers are visible in the project documentation
validation_criteria: Each risk entry includes description, likelihood, impact, and owner
Each blocker entry includes description, impact, and owner
risk_and_blocker_section_id is linked from the main project_status_section_id

refusal_conditions: No risks or blockers can be derived from raw_updates_collection_id and policy forbids speculative entries

steps.action: Record next planned actions and responsible team members
steps.verify: Next steps and owners are listed in the update
validation_criteria: Each action entry includes description, target_date, and responsible_owner
All target_date values are in the future relative to current UTC date

refusal_conditions: Responsible_owner identifiers cannot be resolved to valid team members in project context

steps.action: Review the draft update for clarity and completeness
steps.verify: Draft is free of gaps and clearly communicates status
validation_criteria: All required sections (status, milestones, risks/blockers, next_actions) are present
No placeholder text such as 'TBD' or 'Lorem ipsum' remains
Overall_status field is one of: 'on_track', 'at_risk', 'off_track'

refusal_conditions: Overall_status is not set to an allowed value

steps.action: Publish the updated delivery status to the docs wiki
steps.verify: New delivery status appears on the project wiki page
validation_criteria: published_status_version_id is created in docs_wiki version history
Content of docs_wiki_project_page_location matches final_status_update_draft_id for updated sections

refusal_conditions: Version conflict is detected and cannot be auto-merged according to system rules

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of the update via the designated communication channel
steps.verify: Stakeholders confirm receipt of the notification
validation_criteria: Notification message includes link to docs_wiki_project_page_location and mentions published_status_version_id
stakeholder_notification_message_id is recorded
stakeholder_acknowledgment_summary_id lists which stakeholders acknowledged receipt

abort_conditions: Latest project updates or reports are unavailable
Documentation system is inaccessible
Verification of published update fails

name: Execute Release Validation Suite
description: A QA engineer initiates and validates a release candidate by running a suite of automated and manual tests in the staging environment. The goal is to ensure all features meet quality standards before deployment, documenting any defects and confirming readiness for production.

preconditions: Release candidate build identifier is provided
Staging environment is reachable and has required dependencies
Access to CI/CD, test runner, and defect tracking system is available
invariants: No changes are made to production systems
All tests are executed against staging environment only
All timestamps are recorded in UTC
refusal_conditions: Release candidate build cannot be located
Staging environment does not meet preconditions
Required tools or systems are not listed in allowed_systems

steps.action: Review the release notes and acceptance criteria for the release candidate
steps.verify: Release notes and criteria are fully understood and documented for the test cycle
validation_criteria: test_cycle_scope_document_id lists all acceptance criteria with unique identifiers
Each acceptance criterion is mapped to at least one planned test case
All items in release_notes are referenced in test_cycle_scope_document_id or explicitly marked as 'out_of_scope'

steps.action: Confirm the staging environment is synchronized with the latest release candidate build
steps.verify: Build tags and environment status match the intended release version
validation_criteria: Staging environment build tag equals release_candidate_build_id
Health check for staging_environment_identifier returns success
environment_sync_verification_record_id includes evidence of build tag and health check results

refusal_conditions: Build tag in staging does not equal release_candidate_build_id

steps.action: Initiate the full suite of automated regression tests on the staging environment
steps.verify: Automated test results are collected and all tests have executed
validation_criteria: All test cases in regression_test_suite_identifier are executed once
regression_test_report_location is generated and accessible
regression_test_run_id is recorded with start and end timestamps

steps.action: Analyze automated test results to identify failures or anomalies
steps.verify: Test report shows pass/fail status for all cases with failures flagged
validation_criteria: Each test case in regression_test_report_location has a status of 'pass' or 'fail'
list_of_failed_or_anomalous_tests includes all tests with status 'fail' or with error signatures
automated_test_analysis_summary_id references regression_test_run_id and counts of pass/fail

steps.action: Execute manual exploratory and acceptance tests as outlined in the test plan
steps.verify: Manual test logs are completed and results are recorded in the tracking system
validation_criteria: All acceptance criteria from test_cycle_scope_document_id have at least one executed manual test case
manual_test_execution_log_id includes result (pass/fail) and evidence for each executed test
Environment field in all log entries is 'staging'

steps.action: Log any new defects or issues found during testing in the defect tracking system
steps.verify: Defect records are created and visible to the development team
validation_criteria: Each unique issue from automated and manual tests has a corresponding entry in defect_record_ids
Each defect record includes severity, steps_to_reproduce, environment='staging', and affected_build=release_candidate_build_id
All defect_record_ids are visible in the project specified by defect_tracking_system_project_key

steps.action: Communicate critical or blocking defects to the release manager and development team
steps.verify: Release stakeholders acknowledge receipt of defect notifications
validation_criteria: Notification includes links to all defects with severity >= severity_threshold_for_notification
critical_defect_notification_message_id is recorded
stakeholder_acknowledgment_record_id lists acknowledgments from release_manager_contact_identifier and at least one development_team representative

refusal_conditions: No defects meet severity_threshold_for_notification and policy forbids sending empty notifications

steps.action: Re-test resolved defects after fixes are applied to the release candidate
steps.verify: Defect status is updated to indicate re-test results in the tracking system
validation_criteria: For each defect with status 'resolved', at least one re-test is executed
defect_retest_log_id records pass/fail for each re-test
Defect records are updated with final status 'closed' or 'reopened' based on re-test results

refusal_conditions: No defects have status 'resolved' in defect tracking system

steps.action: Validate that all high-priority test cases have passed and critical defects are addressed
steps.verify: Test summary report indicates all critical requirements are met
validation_criteria: All test cases tagged with priority in priority_levels_considered have status 'pass' in automated or manual logs
No defect with severity 'critical' or 'blocker' remains in status 'open' or 'in_progress'
release_validation_summary_id explicitly states whether release is 'ready' or 'not_ready'

refusal_conditions: Any high or critical priority test case has status 'fail'
Any critical or blocker defect is not in a terminal status

steps.action: Document overall release readiness in the test report and share with stakeholders
steps.verify: Final test summary is distributed and acknowledged by relevant parties
validation_criteria: final_test_report_location includes release_validation_summary_id and references to key defect_record_ids
readiness_notification_message_id corresponds to a message containing link to final_test_report_location and explicit readiness status
stakeholder_acknowledgment_summary_id lists acknowledgments from release_manager_contact_identifier and other key stakeholders

refusal_conditions: release_validation_summary_id indicates 'not_ready' and policy forbids declaring readiness

abort_conditions: Critical defect blocks release readiness
Automated test suite fails to complete
Staging environment does not match release candidate

name: Debug Production Model Issue
description: An MLOps engineer investigates and resolves a production issue affecting a deployed machine learning model. The process involves identifying symptoms, collecting logs and metrics, analyzing model behavior, and coordinating with relevant teams to restore normal operations while maintaining the integrity of production data.

preconditions: Access to logging and monitoring systems is available
Access to kubernetes cluster for the model deployment is available
invariants: All changes to production are recorded in incident and change management systems
Production data integrity is preserved
All timestamps are recorded in UTC
refusal_conditions: Manager approval is not granted where required

steps.action: Acknowledge the production issue and review incident notifications
steps.verify: Incident ticket is updated with acknowledgment and initial assessment
validation_criteria: Incident ticket status is updated to 'in_progress' or equivalent
incident_acknowledgment_entry_id includes assignee=user_profile.id and timestamp
initial_assessment_summary_id briefly describes observed symptoms from notifications

steps.action: Collect recent logs from the affected model deployment
steps.verify: Relevant logs are attached to the incident ticket and reviewed
validation_criteria: Logs are queried for model_service_identifier within time_window_minutes not exceeding max_time_window_minutes
log_export_location is generated and accessible
incident_log_attachment_id links log_export_location to incident_ticket_id

steps.action: Gather model performance metrics and error rates from monitoring dashboards
steps.verify: Metrics are compared to baseline and anomalies are documented
validation_criteria: current_metrics_snapshot_id includes key metrics (latency, error_rate, throughput) for model_service_identifier
Comparison is made between current_metrics_snapshot_id and baseline_metrics_reference_location over same time_window_minutes
metrics_anomaly_summary_id lists metrics where deviation exceeds predefined thresholds in baseline

steps.action: Check model input and output data samples for irregularities
steps.verify: Sample data is reviewed and findings are recorded in the incident ticket
validation_criteria: input_output_sample_location contains at most sample_size_limit records
Samples are restricted to time_window_minutes not exceeding max_time_window_minutes
data_irregularity_summary_id describes any detected anomalies (e.g., null_rates, distribution_shifts) without including raw sensitive values

refusal_conditions: data_sampling_query_template would retrieve more than sample_size_limit records

steps.action: Consult with data engineering to confirm data pipeline health
steps.verify: Confirmation from data team is logged in the communication channel
validation_criteria: A message is sent to the data engineering group referencing data_pipeline_identifier and incident_ticket_id
data_pipeline_health_status is recorded as 'healthy', 'degraded', or 'down' based on data team response
data_team_consultation_message_id is linked in the incident ticket

steps.action: Validate model container status and resource utilization in the orchestration platform
steps.verify: Resource usage reports and pod status are reviewed for anomalies
validation_criteria: container_status_snapshot_id lists all pods for model_deployment_name with status and restart_count
resource_utilization_report_id includes CPU, memory, and GPU (if applicable) usage for each pod
Anomalies are defined as pods not in 'Running' state or resource usage exceeding configured limits

refusal_conditions: User_profile.access_tier does not permit required read operations

steps.action: Identify potential recent changes to the model or serving infrastructure
steps.verify: Change logs and deployment records are checked and summarized
validation_criteria: recent_changes_list_id includes all deployments, configuration changes, and dependency updates for model_service_identifier within time_window_hours
Each change entry includes timestamp, author, and change_type

steps.action: Escalate to manager for approval to apply any temporary mitigations
steps.verify: Manager approval is documented in the incident ticket
validation_criteria: Mitigation request message references incident_ticket_id and proposed_mitigation_plan_location
manager_approval_status is set to 'approved' or 'rejected'
manager_approval_record_id is stored in the incident ticket with timestamp and approver identifier

steps.action: Apply safe mitigations, such as reverting to a previous model version or scaling resources
steps.verify: Mitigation actions are executed and status is logged
validation_criteria: All actions in approved_mitigation_actions_list are contained in allowed_mitigation_actions
For each action, corresponding change is reflected in kubernetes state (e.g., deployment image tag, replica count)
mitigation_execution_record_id is logged in incident ticket and, if applicable, change_management_system

refusal_conditions: approved_mitigation_actions_list contains actions not in allowed_mitigation_actions

steps.action: Monitor system and model metrics post-mitigation for signs of recovery
steps.verify: Metrics return to expected thresholds and issue resolution is confirmed
validation_criteria: post_mitigation_metrics_snapshot_id includes same key metrics as baseline
For each key metric, deviation from baseline is within predefined acceptable thresholds
recovery_assessment_summary_id states whether service is 'recovered', 'partially_recovered', or 'not_recovered'

steps.action: Document root cause analysis and actions taken in the incident ticket
steps.verify: Incident ticket contains a clear summary and timeline of events
validation_criteria: root_cause_analysis_document_id includes sections: symptoms, investigation, root_cause, mitigations, verification
Timeline lists key events with UTC timestamps
Incident ticket is updated to include link to root_cause_analysis_document_id

refusal_conditions: Any required input summary identifier is missing

steps.action: Communicate resolution and next steps to stakeholders
steps.verify: Stakeholders acknowledge receipt of resolution update
validation_criteria: Resolution message references incident_ticket_id and root_cause_analysis_document_id
Message clearly states current service status from recovery_assessment_summary_id
stakeholder_acknowledgment_summary_id records acknowledgments from defined stakeholder_distribution_list

abort_conditions: Manager approval is denied for mitigation actions
Verification of model health post-mitigation fails
Environment is deemed unsafe for changes

name: Respond to Production Alert
description: A site reliability engineer is responding to a high-urgency alert in the production environment. The goal is to quickly assess, diagnose, mitigate, and document the incident affecting a critical platform system. The response must adhere to operational constraints and ensure minimal disruption to users.

operational_invariants: Do not modify production configuration outside documented safe_write procedures.
Do not execute any destructive operations (e.g., delete, drop, irreversible changes).
Do not access data outside the systems listed in allowed_systems.
Do not change user data or business records as part of incident response.
refusal_conditions: Requested action requires access to systems not listed in allowed_systems.

steps.action: Acknowledge the incoming production alert in the monitoring system
steps.verify: Alert status updated to acknowledged in monitoring dashboard

refusal_conditions: monitoring_dashboard is not reachable after max_retries

steps.action: Notify the incident response channel and assemble the required on-call team
steps.verify: Incident response channel contains notification and team members join

steps.action: Gather initial incident details from logs and monitoring dashboards
steps.verify: Incident timeline and affected metrics are documented in the incident ticket

steps.action: Assess the current impact on end users and business operations
steps.verify: Impact assessment is recorded in the incident management system

steps.action: Escalate to additional experts or stakeholders if incident scope requires broader expertise
steps.verify: Escalation notes and additional responders are logged

steps.action: Isolate the affected system components to prevent further impact
steps.verify: Isolation actions are confirmed in system dashboards and incident logs

steps.action: Mitigate immediate risks using available safe-write operational procedures
steps.verify: Mitigation steps are reflected in system state and incident updates

steps.action: Monitor system health and key performance indicators for signs of stabilization
steps.verify: Stabilization is observed through improved metrics in monitoring dashboards

steps.action: Communicate status updates to stakeholders and user-facing channels
steps.verify: Status updates are posted in the designated communication platforms

steps.action: Document all actions and findings in the incident ticket
steps.verify: Incident ticket contains a complete action and observation log

steps.action: Initiate root cause analysis using logs and system state information
steps.verify: Preliminary root cause notes are added to the incident documentation

steps.action: Coordinate with platform and application teams for further investigation if needed
steps.verify: Collaboration steps and findings are recorded in the incident management system

steps.action: Develop and apply a verified remediation plan to resolve the incident
steps.verify: Remediation steps are executed and their success is observed in system health metrics

steps.action: Confirm full system recovery and return to normal operation
steps.verify: Monitoring dashboards show normal performance and no recurring alerts

steps.action: Close the incident in the monitoring and incident tracking systems
steps.verify: Incident status is marked as resolved and closed

steps.action: Schedule and communicate a post-incident review meeting with all relevant teams
steps.verify: Post-incident review is scheduled and invitations are sent

abort_conditions: Verification of mitigation or recovery steps fails
System environment is deemed unsafe for further actions
Required incident response resources are unavailable

name: Initiate Database Restore from Backup
description: A site reliability engineer on call is tasked with restoring a production database from backup in response to a production issue. This process must be performed with care due to the high sensitivity of the data and potential impact on service availability. The restoration requires approval due to its destructive nature and regulated data classification.

operational_invariants: Do not perform restore without explicit recorded manager approval.
Do not restore from backups that have failed integrity checks.
Do not restore to a different environment than specified production_database_only.
Do not export or copy regulated data outside approved systems.
refusal_conditions: Selected backup does not have a successful integrity status.
Target database identifier is ambiguous or missing.

steps.action: Notify relevant stakeholders about the planned database restore operation
steps.verify: Stakeholder notifications are acknowledged in the incident channel

steps.action: Document the current state of the production database and affected systems
steps.verify: Incident documentation includes current state snapshots

steps.action: Submit a formal request for manager approval to initiate the database restore
steps.verify: Approval request is logged and awaiting manager response

steps.action: Receive and record manager approval for the restore operation
steps.verify: Approval is documented in the incident management system

steps.action: Identify and select the most recent valid backup appropriate for restoration
steps.verify: Selected backup is referenced in the recovery plan

steps.action: Isolate the affected database to prevent new writes during the restoration process
steps.verify: Database connectivity restricted as confirmed by monitoring

steps.action: Initiate the database restore process using the selected backup
steps.verify: Restore job status is tracked and in progress

steps.action: Monitor the progress of the restore operation and log status updates
steps.verify: Progress updates are visible in incident and monitoring tools

steps.action: Validate the integrity of the restored database upon completion
steps.verify: Integrity checks pass and are logged in recovery records

steps.action: Re-enable database access for application services and users
steps.verify: Database connectivity is confirmed operational in monitoring

steps.action: Perform post-restore functional tests on critical application workflows
steps.verify: Test results are documented and show successful operations

steps.action: Communicate restoration completion and system status to stakeholders
steps.verify: Stakeholder acknowledgements received in incident channel

steps.action: Update incident records with detailed timeline and outcomes
steps.verify: Incident ticket reflects restoration details and resolution

steps.action: Schedule a post-incident review to capture lessons learned
steps.verify: Post-incident review is scheduled and visible on the team calendar

abort_conditions: Manager approval is denied
Backup integrity verification fails
Production environment is deemed unsafe for restoration

name: Prepare Staged Production Release
description: A site reliability engineer prepares and coordinates a staged deployment of a new production release to the cloud infrastructure. This process ensures that the release is properly validated, approved, and monitored before full rollout. The workflow includes pre-deployment checks, controlled launch, verification, and rollback readiness.

operational_invariants: Do not deploy without successful required tests in CI/CD.
Do not proceed to production without recorded manager approval.
Do not modify rollback artifacts during deployment.
Do not change production configuration outside the release artifacts.
refusal_conditions: Required CI/CD jobs are not in successful state.
Manager approval record is missing for production deployment.
Release candidate identifier is missing or ambiguous.

steps.action: Review the change log and release documentation for completeness and accuracy.
steps.verify: Release documentation is up-to-date and approved in the docs portal.

steps.action: Validate that all automated tests have passed in the CI/CD pipeline.
steps.verify: CI/CD dashboard shows green status for all required test jobs.

steps.action: Confirm that the release candidate has been signed off by the QA team.
steps.verify: QA sign-off is documented in the release ticket.

steps.action: Check that rollback procedures and artifacts are ready and accessible.
steps.verify: Rollback plan is linked and artifacts are available in the release documentation.

steps.action: Request manager approval for the staged production deployment.
steps.verify: Approval is recorded in the deployment tracking system.

steps.action: Announce the planned deployment window to relevant stakeholders.
steps.verify: Deployment announcement is posted in the incident channel and stakeholder mailing list.

steps.action: Deploy the release to the staging environment for a final smoke test.
steps.verify: Staging environment passes smoke test checklist.

steps.action: Monitor key application metrics and error logs in staging post-deployment.
steps.verify: No critical errors or abnormal metrics detected in staging monitoring dashboards.

steps.action: Initiate the staged deployment to production, targeting a small subset of instances.
steps.verify: Deployment begins and limited production instances are updated as tracked in deployment dashboard.

steps.action: Monitor production metrics and logs for any anomalies during initial rollout.
steps.verify: Production monitoring dashboards show normal operation with no new critical alerts.

steps.action: Gradually increase rollout to additional production instances in defined stages.
steps.verify: Each stage of the rollout is completed and tracked with successful health checks.

steps.action: Collect feedback from stakeholders and oncall teams during deployment.
steps.verify: Deployment feedback is logged in the release ticket and communication channels.

steps.action: Finalize deployment and update release status in tracking systems.
steps.verify: Deployment status is set to completed and all records are updated.

abort_conditions: Automated or manual verification fails at any step.
Manager approval is denied or delayed beyond the deployment window.
Critical errors or anomalies are detected during staged rollout.

name: Routine Schema Migration
description: A senior backend developer needs to perform a routine schema migration on a production payments database as part of scheduled maintenance. The process ensures minimal disruption and data integrity, following safe write practices and internal data sensitivity controls.

operational_invariants: Do not run migration without a recent successful backup.
Do not modify data beyond schema structure and default values.
Do not change permissions or roles as part of schema migration.
Do not perform destructive schema changes without explicit prior approval recorded elsewhere.
refusal_conditions: Backup integrity check has not been performed or has failed.
Target database identifier is missing or ambiguous.
Migration plan does not have recorded approval.

steps.action: Review the approved schema migration plan and documentation
steps.verify: Migration plan matches documentation and has necessary approvals

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of planned schema migration window
steps.verify: Notification sent and acknowledged by stakeholders

steps.action: Confirm current database health and performance baselines
steps.verify: Health and performance metrics are within normal ranges

steps.action: Initiate a backup of the current production database
steps.verify: Backup completed and integrity validated

steps.action: Apply schema migration changes using safe migration procedures
steps.verify: Migration scripts run successfully with no errors reported

steps.action: Monitor real-time database logs for anomalies during migration
steps.verify: No critical errors or anomalies detected in logs

steps.action: Validate new schema against expected structure and constraints
steps.verify: Schema matches design and passes all integrity checks

steps.action: Run application-level integration tests to ensure compatibility
steps.verify: Integration tests pass and application functions as expected

steps.action: Monitor database performance metrics post-migration
steps.verify: Performance metrics remain stable and within thresholds

steps.action: Update migration status in maintenance tracking system
steps.verify: Status updated and visible to all relevant teams

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of migration completion and results
steps.verify: Stakeholders confirm receipt and review of results

steps.action: Archive migration logs and related documentation
steps.verify: Logs and docs stored in the designated repository

abort_conditions: Verification of backup integrity fails
Schema migration scripts encounter unrecoverable errors
Critical performance degradation detected during or after migration

name: Respond to Production Infrastructure Alert
description: A site reliability engineer is tasked with responding to a high-urgency alert impacting production infrastructure. The process involves triaging the alert, assessing system health, applying safe changes, and ensuring system stability while adhering to operational constraints. Actions are performed on the cloud console with production-admin access.

operational_invariants: Do not perform destructive infrastructure changes (e.g., delete clusters) as part of initial remediation.
Do not change security groups or firewall rules beyond documented safe_write patterns.
Do not modify billing or account-level settings.
Do not disable monitoring or alerting.
refusal_conditions: Target resource identifier is missing or ambiguous.
Manager approval is required but not recorded for the specific remediation.

steps.action: Acknowledge the alert in the incident management system
steps.verify: Alert status is updated to acknowledged and visible to incident stakeholders

steps.action: Review the alert details and affected resources in the monitoring dashboard
steps.verify: Monitoring dashboard displays relevant metrics and affected services

steps.action: Communicate incident status and initial findings to stakeholders
steps.verify: Incident communication channel contains up-to-date status messages

steps.action: Assess system logs for anomalies or error patterns related to the alert
steps.verify: Log analysis reveals source or contributing factors for the alert

steps.action: Check current system health metrics and resource utilization
steps.verify: Resource dashboards reflect accurate, real-time system health data

steps.action: Identify and document potential remediation actions
steps.verify: Incident notes include proposed actions and expected outcomes

steps.action: Request manager approval for remediation actions requiring elevated privileges
steps.verify: Approval is documented in the incident management system

steps.action: Apply remediation action in the cloud console with safe write constraints
steps.verify: Remediation action is executed and system logs reflect the change

steps.action: Monitor system behavior and health metrics post-remediation
steps.verify: Metrics return to normal operating ranges and no new alerts are triggered

steps.action: Update incident stakeholders on remediation actions and current status
steps.verify: Incident communication channel contains a summary of actions and status

steps.action: Document the timeline of events and actions taken during the incident
steps.verify: Incident report contains a complete and accurate event log

steps.action: Close the alert in the incident management system
steps.verify: Alert status is set to resolved and incident is archived for review

abort_conditions: Approval for remediation is denied
Verification of remediation step fails
System environment is deemed unsafe for changes

name: Quarterly Roadmap Update
description: The product manager reviews, updates, and communicates the quarterly product roadmap. This process ensures alignment with business priorities and stakeholder expectations while keeping all documentation up to date and accessible.

steps.action: Review input from previous roadmap cycle and gather feedback from key stakeholders.
steps.verify: Stakeholder feedback is documented and accessible in the docs wiki.

success_criteria: feedback_collection_page_current_quarter contains at least 1 feedback entry OR a note 'no_new_feedback'
all feedback entries include stakeholder_id, stakeholder_role, feedback_text, timestamp_iso8601
failure_conditions: any feedback entry missing required fields

steps.action: Aggregate completed, in-progress, and backlogged initiatives from current product documentation.
steps.verify: All initiatives are listed and status is updated in the docs wiki.

success_criteria: every initiative listed has initiative_id and initiative_status
initiative_status for every initiative is one of allowed_status_values_in_output

steps.action: Align roadmap items with current business objectives and priorities.
steps.verify: Roadmap items are mapped to business objectives in the documentation.

success_criteria: each initiative_id from initiative_aggregation_current_quarter appears at least once OR is explicitly marked 'unmapped'
priority_rank_integer is within priority_rank_range for all mapped items
failure_conditions: any mapping entry missing initiative_id or objective_id (unless explicitly marked 'unmapped')

refusal_conditions: business_objectives_current_year document is older than 365 days based on timestamp

steps.action: Draft updated roadmap for the upcoming quarter in the docs wiki.
steps.verify: Draft roadmap is saved and versioned in the docs wiki.

success_criteria: version number of roadmap_draft_upcoming_quarter is greater than any previous version for same quarter
failure_conditions: versioning metadata missing or not incremented

refusal_conditions: existing approved roadmap document for upcoming quarter already exists and is locked

steps.action: Share the draft with relevant stakeholders for feedback.
steps.verify: Stakeholders are notified and feedback requests are logged in the docs wiki.

success_criteria: each stakeholder in stakeholder_list_document up to max_stakeholders_to_notify has a corresponding log entry
roadmap_draft_link in each entry resolves to roadmap_draft_upcoming_quarter
failure_conditions: any log entry missing required fields

steps.action: Incorporate stakeholder feedback into the roadmap draft.
steps.verify: Feedback is reflected in the updated roadmap and tracked in the docs wiki.

success_criteria: roadmap_draft_upcoming_quarter version number increased by at least 0.1
every processed feedback entry has feedback_resolution_status in allowed_feedback_resolution_status_values
resolution_notes present for all feedback entries marked 'rejected' or 'partially_accepted'
failure_conditions: any feedback entry missing feedback_resolution_status after processing

steps.action: Finalize the quarterly roadmap and mark the document as approved.
steps.verify: Roadmap status is set to approved in the docs wiki with time stamp.

success_criteria: approval_timestamp_iso8601 is present and valid ISO8601

steps.action: Announce the updated roadmap to all relevant teams via internal communication channels.
steps.verify: Announcement is visible in designated channels and acknowledged by teams.

success_criteria: each team in team_list_document up to max_teams_to_notify has a corresponding log entry
roadmap_final_link in each entry resolves to roadmap_final_upcoming_quarter
failure_conditions: any log entry missing required fields

steps.action: Archive the previous quarter's roadmap for reference.
steps.verify: Archived roadmap is accessible in the docs wiki under historical records.

abort_conditions: Stakeholder feedback is not received within the expected timeframe.
Documentation system is unavailable or read-only access is restricted.
Significant misalignment with business objectives is discovered during review.

name: Update Project Status Report
description: A project manager needs to update the status report for an ongoing project as part of a routine project management process. This includes gathering progress information, updating documentation, and sharing the latest status with stakeholders using the documentation system. The activity is performed with read-only access and involves internal data.

steps.action: Access the current project documentation in the docs wiki
steps.verify: Documentation is viewable and up to date with the last revision

steps.action: Collect recent project progress data from team updates and reports
steps.verify: Progress data is gathered and summarized for review

steps.action: Review milestone achievements and pending deliverables
steps.verify: Milestones and deliverables are clearly listed with current status

success_criteria: each entry has delivery_status in allowed_delivery_status_values
failure_conditions: status list cannot be created or updated

steps.action: Summarize project risks and issues identified since the last report
steps.verify: Risks and issues section contains new and ongoing items

success_criteria: each entry item_type is in allowed_item_types

steps.action: Update the status report section with progress, risks, and next steps
steps.verify: Status report shows new updates with timestamp and author

steps.action: Attach supporting documentation or links for reference
steps.verify: Supporting materials are linked or attached in the report

success_criteria: project_status_report_current contains links to supporting_materials_list up to max_supporting_documents_to_link
failure_conditions: any link missing document_id or document_link

steps.action: Notify stakeholders about the updated status report via the docs wiki notification system
steps.verify: Notification is sent and visible in stakeholder notification logs

success_criteria: each stakeholder in stakeholder_registry_project up to max_stakeholders_to_notify has a log entry

steps.action: Archive the previous version of the status report for record-keeping
steps.verify: Previous version is stored and accessible in the docs wiki history

abort_conditions: Unable to access project documentation
Progress data is incomplete or unavailable
Stakeholder notification system is not functioning

name: Routine Maintenance on Cloud Console
description: A site reliability engineer performs routine maintenance on core platform services through the cloud console. The goal is to ensure service health and reliability, following safe operational procedures under production constraints. All actions are tracked to maintain compliance and minimize production risk.

steps.action: Review the scheduled maintenance ticket and associated documentation.
steps.verify: Ticket contains clear maintenance objectives and documentation is up to date.

steps.action: Notify stakeholders about planned maintenance and expected impact.
steps.verify: Stakeholders confirm receipt of maintenance notification.

success_criteria: each stakeholder in stakeholder_list_source up to max_stakeholders_to_notify has a log entry

steps.action: Check service health dashboards for baseline metrics before changes.
steps.verify: Baseline metrics are recorded and within expected thresholds.

steps.action: Place affected services into maintenance mode where supported.
steps.verify: Services display maintenance status in the cloud console.

success_criteria: for each service in affected_services_list, maintenance_mode_flag_name set to true
service_state_changes_log contains entries for all affected services
failure_conditions: any service fails to enter maintenance mode

steps.action: Apply approved configuration updates or patches to the target services.
steps.verify: Configuration or patch status is updated in the cloud console.

success_criteria: each change in approved_change_plan has a corresponding entry in configuration_change_log
no additional changes beyond approved_change_plan are present

steps.action: Monitor system logs and alerts for anomalies during the maintenance window.
steps.verify: No unexpected alerts or error logs are observed.

success_criteria: all recorded alerts have alert_severity >= alert_severity_threshold OR are explicitly marked 'ignored_below_threshold'

steps.action: Validate that all dependencies and integrations remain operational.
steps.verify: Dependency health checks report successful integrations.

success_criteria: each dependency in dependency_list_source up to max_dependencies_to_check has a log entry
failure_conditions: any log entry missing required fields

steps.action: Gradually return services to normal operational mode.
steps.verify: Services show normal operational status in the cloud console.

success_criteria: for each service in affected_services_list, maintenance_mode_flag_name set to false
service_state_changes_log contains entries for all affected services
failure_conditions: any service fails to exit maintenance mode

steps.action: Compare post-maintenance metrics to baseline values.
steps.verify: Post-maintenance metrics are consistent with or improved from baseline.

success_criteria: all metrics_to_compare present in both baseline and post snapshots
failure_conditions: snapshots or report cannot be stored

steps.action: Update the maintenance ticket with notes and outcomes.
steps.verify: Ticket reflects completed actions and observed results.

steps.action: Communicate maintenance completion and outcomes to stakeholders.
steps.verify: Stakeholders acknowledge receipt of completion notification.

success_criteria: each stakeholder in stakeholder_list_source up to max_stakeholders_to_notify has a log entry

steps.action: Archive logs and relevant artifacts for compliance and audit purposes.
steps.verify: Logs and artifacts are stored in designated internal repositories.

abort_conditions: Verification step fails at any stage
Unexpected service degradation is detected
Stakeholder approval is withdrawn

name: Investigate Inference Latency Spike
description: A sudden increase in inference latency has been detected in the production environment. The MLOps engineer needs to methodically investigate the root cause, validate model service health, and ensure system stability. The process involves gathering metrics, reviewing logs, and coordinating with relevant teams to resolve the issue while maintaining safe write practices.

steps.action: Acknowledge the alert for inference latency spike in the monitoring system
steps.verify: Alert status updated in monitoring dashboard

steps.action: Review recent inference latency metrics for the affected model services
steps.verify: Latency metrics visualized and anomalies confirmed on monitoring dashboard

steps.action: Check resource utilization (CPU, memory, GPU) of model-serving pods
steps.verify: Resource graphs display current and historical usage patterns

steps.action: Inspect recent deployment history for model or infrastructure changes
steps.verify: Deployment logs reviewed and latest changes identified

steps.action: Examine application logs for errors or warnings related to inference requests
steps.verify: Relevant log entries identified and documented

steps.action: Validate health status of dependencies such as upstream data sources or model artifact storage
steps.verify: Dependency health checks reported as successful or issues noted

success_criteria: each dependency in dependency_list_source up to max_dependencies_to_check has a log entry
failure_conditions: any log entry missing required fields

steps.action: Test inference endpoint manually to measure current response times
steps.verify: Manual test results recorded and compared with baseline

steps.action: Coordinate with SRE team to assess possible platform-level issues
steps.verify: SRE input documented and any platform incidents cross-checked

success_criteria: at least one entry with participant_id belonging to sre_group_identifier

steps.action: Assess potential impact of recent traffic pattern changes or load spikes
steps.verify: Traffic analysis completed and findings summarized

steps.action: Implement safe configuration adjustments (e.g., resource limits, pod replica count) to mitigate latency
steps.verify: Configuration changes reflected in system state and monitored for impact

success_criteria: replica increases do not exceed max_replica_increase_factor
resource limit increases do not exceed max_resource_limit_increase_percent

steps.action: Monitor latency metrics post-adjustment to confirm improvement
steps.verify: Latency metrics return to normal range as seen on dashboard

steps.action: Update incident documentation with findings, actions taken, and resolution status
steps.verify: Incident ticket or document updated and marked as reviewed

abort_conditions: Verification step fails and risk of service degradation increases
Required access or approval for safe write operations is not granted
Critical dependency is unavailable, blocking further investigation

name: Generate Monthly Financial Report
description: A finance analyst needs to generate a monthly financial report by querying relevant data sources and compiling results for executive review. The process must ensure that only confidential data is accessed and all steps follow internal compliance requirements.

steps.action: Access the financial database with read-only credentials
steps.verify: Database access logs show successful read-only connection

success_criteria: connection log entry exists with connection_mode_value

refusal_conditions: approval_model.requires_approval is true AND approval not present in finance_workflow_system

steps.action: Locate and select the relevant tables for the current month's transactions
steps.verify: List of tables and date ranges are documented in the report preparation notes

steps.action: Extract transaction data for the specified month
steps.verify: Data extract summary matches expected transaction volume

success_criteria: no single table row_count exceeds max_rows_per_query

steps.action: Validate data completeness against system-generated totals
steps.verify: Validation log confirms no missing or duplicate entries

success_criteria: all validation_status values are in allowed_validation_status_values

steps.action: Aggregate transaction data by category (e.g., revenue, expenses)
steps.verify: Aggregated figures match with system dashboard summaries

steps.action: Perform data quality checks for outliers or anomalies
steps.verify: Quality check report lists no unresolved anomalies

steps.action: Compile summary statistics and key financial metrics
steps.verify: Metrics are reviewed and confirmed by a second analyst

steps.action: Draft the monthly financial report using compiled data
steps.verify: Draft report is saved in the approved document repository

steps.action: Review report for accuracy and compliance with internal guidelines
steps.verify: Checklist is completed and review is logged

failure_conditions: checklist cannot be created or saved

steps.action: Submit the completed report to executive leadership for review
steps.verify: Submission confirmation is received and archived

abort_conditions: Data validation fails and cannot be reconciled
Access to required data sources is denied
Data sensitivity controls are not enforced

name: Routine Maintenance on Cloud Infrastructure
description: A platform engineer performs scheduled routine maintenance on cloud infrastructure to ensure reliability and performance. The process involves reviewing system health, applying approved updates, and verifying that all services remain operational. Maintenance is executed with minimal risk to production workloads and includes detailed verification steps.

invariants: Do_not_modify_or_delete_resources_not_listed_in_approved_change_list
Do_not_change_global_security_policies
Do_not_change_billing_or_account_level_settings

steps.action: Review the maintenance schedule and approved change list for the current cycle
steps.verify: Maintenance plan is documented and all changes are authorized
validation_criteria: Each_change_item_has_status_approved
Maintenance_schedule_start_and_end_time_cover_current_time
guardrails: If_any_change_item_is_not_approved_mark_step_as_failed_without_modification
If_schedule_does_not_cover_current_time_mark_step_as_failed

steps.action: Notify impacted teams and stakeholders about the planned maintenance window
steps.verify: Stakeholders acknowledge receipt of notification
validation_criteria: Notification_sent_log_contains_all_recipients_from_contact_list
Each_message_body_contains_correct_maintenance_window_timestamps
guardrails: If_contact_list_is_empty_mark_step_as_failed
Do_not_retry_more_than_3_times_per_recipient_on_send_failure

steps.action: Check current health dashboards for cloud infrastructure components
steps.verify: All monitored metrics are within normal ranges
validation_criteria: Each_required_metric_has_a_current_value
Each_metric_value_is_between_defined_min_and_max_inclusive
guardrails: If_any_metric_is_outside_normal_range_set_boolean_flag_to_false
If_dashboard_not_reachable_mark_step_as_failed

validation_criteria: Each_critical_resource_has_a_corresponding_backup_job_record
Each_backup_job_record_has_status_success

Do_not_retry_backup_more_than_2_times_per_resource

steps.action: Apply approved updates and configuration changes to cloud resources
steps.verify: Change management logs reflect successful update application
validation_criteria: For_each_change_item_execution_log_status_is_success
Change_management_system_records_show_status_completed_for_each_item
guardrails: If_application_of_any_change_item_fails_mark_step_as_failed
Do_not_retry_a_failed_change_more_than_1_time

steps.action: Monitor resource status and service health post-update
steps.verify: Health dashboards show no new errors or degraded performance
validation_criteria: No_new_alerts_with_severity_greater_or_equal_to_defined_threshold
Key_health_indicators_remain_within_defined_thresholds_for_entire_duration
guardrails: If_any_indicator_exceeds_threshold_set_boolean_flag_to_false
If_monitoring_system_unreachable_for_more_than_2_minutes_mark_step_as_failed

steps.action: Confirm that access controls and security groups remain as intended
steps.verify: IAM audit logs and security group configurations are consistent with policy
validation_criteria: For_each_IAM_policy_current_configuration_equals_baseline
For_each_security_group_rule_set_equals_baseline
guardrails: If_any_difference_is_detected_set_boolean_flag_to_false
If_IAM_or_security_API_unreachable_mark_step_as_failed

steps.action: Test connectivity and functionality of key services hosted in the cloud
steps.verify: Service tests pass and operational checks are green
validation_criteria: Each_key_service_has_at_least_one_test_execution_record
All_test_execution_records_have_status_pass
guardrails: If_any_test_fails_set_aggregate_boolean_flag_to_false
Do_not_retry_any_individual_test_more_than_2_times

steps.action: Document all actions taken during the maintenance window
steps.verify: Maintenance report is updated and accessible to relevant teams
validation_criteria: Report_contains_entries_for_steps_1_through_10
Report_is_saved_in_documentation_repository_and_readable
guardrails: If_repository_not_accessible_mark_step_as_failed
If_template_missing_mark_step_as_failed

steps.action: Notify stakeholders that maintenance is complete and systems are operational
steps.verify: Stakeholders confirm receipt and report no issues
validation_criteria: Notification_sent_log_covers_all_recipients_in_contact_list
Confirmation_log_records_at_least_one_response_or_timeout_per_recipient

If_notification_system_unavailable_mark_step_as_failed

Unauthorized changes are detected
Stakeholders report unexpected service impact

name: Perform Month-End Close
description: The accountant completes the month-end close process, ensuring all financial transactions are recorded and reconciled. This includes reviewing ledgers, reconciling accounts, and preparing preliminary financial statements for management review. The process must adhere to internal controls and regulatory requirements.

invariants: Do_not_modify_prior_periods_that_are_already_locked
Do_not_create_or_edit_master_data_records
Do_not_post_entries_without_required_supporting_documents

steps.action: Review all general ledger entries for completeness and accuracy

Debits_equal_credits_for_period_in_review_log

All_bank_accounts_in_list_have_a_reconciliation_report

If_bank_statement_missing_for_any_account_mark_step_as_failed

Reconciliation_summaries_are_saved_in_erp_or_document_repository

validation_criteria: Each_required_accrual_has_a_corresponding_posted_entry
Total_debits_equal_total_credits_for_all_accrual_entries

If_trial_balance_not_updatable_mark_step_as_failed

steps.action: Review and adjust journal entries for errors or omissions
steps.verify: Journal entry audit log shows adjustments and approvals
validation_criteria: Each_adjustment_entry_references_original_entry_id
Audit_log_contains_approval_record_for_each_adjustment
