name: Routine Network Maintenance Review
description: The site reliability engineer conducts a scheduled review of network infrastructure for routine maintenance. This includes checking network health, identifying outdated configurations, and verifying connectivity across critical services. The intent ensures network reliability and preempts potential disruptions.

steps.action: Review the scheduled maintenance window and maintenance checklist.
steps.verify: Maintenance window is confirmed and checklist items are current.

steps.action: Notify stakeholders and affected teams about the planned maintenance.
steps.verify: Stakeholders acknowledge notification and maintenance window is visible on status page.

steps.action: Access the cloud console to review current network topology and configurations.
steps.verify: Topology diagram and configuration records are up-to-date and accessible.

steps.action: Check network device health status, including routers, firewalls, and load balancers.
steps.verify: Health dashboards indicate all devices are operational and within normal parameters.

steps.action: Identify and document any outdated network configurations or firmware versions.
steps.verify: List of outdated configurations or versions is compiled and logged.

steps.action: Apply approved configuration updates or patches according to the maintenance checklist.
steps.verify: Change log reflects successful application of updates or patches.

steps.action: Verify network connectivity between critical services and endpoints.
steps.verify: Connectivity reports confirm all critical paths are functional.

steps.action: Monitor network performance metrics for anomalies after updates.
steps.verify: Performance dashboards show no unexpected latency, packet loss, or errors.

steps.action: Update network documentation to reflect any configuration or topology changes made.
steps.verify: Documentation repository contains latest network diagrams and config files.

steps.action: Communicate maintenance completion to stakeholders and teams.
steps.verify: Stakeholders confirm receipt of maintenance completion notice.

abort_conditions: Critical network device health verification fails
Stakeholder notification is not acknowledged
Configuration update verification fails

name: Project Status Review and Update
description: A project manager conducts a routine review of project status, updates key stakeholders, and ensures project milestones and risks are current. This process facilitates team alignment and helps identify blockers early, supporting on-time delivery and transparency.

steps.action: Access the project documentation workspace
steps.verify: Workspace opens with the latest project files visible

steps.action: Review the current project schedule and milestone tracker
steps.verify: Milestone tracker matches most recent team updates

steps.action: Check the status of outstanding deliverables
steps.verify: List of deliverables is up to date and accurate

steps.action: Identify any overdue tasks or blockers
steps.verify: Any overdue items or blockers are clearly listed

steps.action: Summarize key risks and mitigation actions
steps.verify: Risk log includes current risks and assigned owners

steps.action: Update the project status summary section
steps.verify: Status summary reflects current project health and progress

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of the updated documentation
steps.verify: Stakeholders receive notification and acknowledge update

steps.action: Archive any obsolete project artifacts
steps.verify: Obsolete files are moved to an archive folder and documented

abort_conditions: Workspace or documentation system is inaccessible
Verification of project data fails
Stakeholder notification cannot be completed

name: Routine Platform Network Maintenance
description: A site reliability engineer performs scheduled maintenance on the platform's network infrastructure to ensure optimal performance and reliability. The process includes verifying current network health, applying approved updates, and validating system stability throughout. Maintenance is performed under safe operational constraints to minimize risk to production systems.

steps.action: Review scheduled maintenance window and confirm maintenance scope
steps.verify: Maintenance plan and window are documented and acknowledged by stakeholders

steps.action: Notify impacted teams and stakeholders about upcoming network maintenance
steps.verify: Notifications are sent and receipt is confirmed by stakeholders

steps.action: Assess current network health and capture baseline metrics
steps.verify: Baseline metrics and health status are recorded in monitoring tools

steps.action: Validate redundancy and failover mechanisms are operational
steps.verify: Redundancy and failover checks are logged as successful

steps.action: Apply approved network configuration updates via cloud console
steps.verify: Configuration changes are reflected in system state and change logs

steps.action: Monitor network status for anomalies or errors during maintenance
steps.verify: No critical anomalies are observed in real-time monitoring dashboards

steps.action: Test connectivity and performance between key platform components
steps.verify: Connectivity tests complete successfully and performance meets baseline

steps.action: Review monitoring alerts to confirm no new issues have arisen
steps.verify: Alert dashboards show no unexpected incidents

steps.action: Document all actions taken and any changes made during maintenance
steps.verify: Maintenance log is updated and available for review

steps.action: Communicate maintenance completion to all stakeholders
steps.verify: Completion notices are sent and acknowledged by recipients

steps.action: Schedule a post-maintenance review to assess outcomes and lessons learned
steps.verify: Review meeting is scheduled and agenda is distributed

abort_conditions: Verification of critical network health checks fails
Unexpected service degradation detected
Stakeholder approval for maintenance is withdrawn

name: Investigate and Escalate Production Alert
description: A site reliability engineer on call responds to a high urgency production alert detected by monitoring systems. The process involves verifying the alert, gathering evidence, escalating as needed, and ensuring incident tracking for resolution. This ensures prompt detection, triage, and escalation of critical issues impacting production systems.

steps.action: Acknowledge the alert in the monitoring system
steps.verify: Alert status is updated to acknowledged in the monitoring dashboard

steps.action: Review the alert details and metadata for impacted services
steps.verify: Alert metadata is visible and affected services are identified in the dashboard

steps.action: Check recent monitoring metrics for anomalies related to the alert
steps.verify: Relevant metric graphs display anomalies correlating with alert timing

steps.action: Review recent logs from the affected systems
steps.verify: Log entries corresponding to the alert timeframe are accessible in the log viewer

steps.action: Correlate the alert with any current or ongoing incidents
steps.verify: Incident tracking system reflects linkage between alert and ongoing incidents, if any

steps.action: Assess the potential impact on users and business operations
steps.verify: Impact assessment is documented in the incident or alert ticket

steps.action: Gather supporting evidence such as screenshots or metric snapshots
steps.verify: Evidence is attached to the incident or alert record

steps.action: Notify relevant on-call team members of the alert status
steps.verify: Notification is sent and receipt is logged in the communication channel

steps.action: Check for any automated remediation actions triggered by the alert
steps.verify: Automation logs or dashboards indicate successful or failed remediation attempts

steps.action: Update the incident or alert ticket with investigation findings
steps.verify: Ticket contains detailed notes and investigation summary

steps.action: Determine escalation path based on impact and urgency
steps.verify: Escalation decision is recorded in the incident or alert ticket

steps.action: Escalate the alert to the appropriate response team if required
steps.verify: Escalation action and recipient team are logged in the incident management system

steps.action: Monitor the situation for further developments or new alerts
steps.verify: Ongoing monitoring is confirmed by periodic dashboard checks

steps.action: Document all actions taken during the response process
steps.verify: Incident or alert ticket contains a chronological action log

steps.action: Confirm that stakeholders are informed of the current status
steps.verify: Stakeholder updates are logged and acknowledged in the communication platform

steps.action: Close or update the alert ticket based on resolution or further action required
steps.verify: Ticket status is set to resolved or updated for follow-up in the tracking system

abort_conditions: Alert is determined to be a false positive after investigation
Verification steps fail to confirm impact or issue presence
Monitoring system is inaccessible or unreliable

name: Project Delivery Status Update
description: A project manager needs to update the status of a delivery milestone in the project management system. This involves reviewing recent progress, documenting current status, and communicating updates to stakeholders. The process must be accurate and comply with internal reporting standards.

steps.action: Review the latest project progress reports from the delivery team.
steps.verify: Progress reports are up-to-date and accessible in the documentation system.

steps.action: Summarize key achievements and blockers since the last status update.
steps.verify: Summary is documented in the draft status update section.

steps.action: Validate milestone completion dates against the project plan.
steps.verify: Dates in the project plan match reported progress in the update.

steps.action: Draft the status update, including current milestone status and next steps.
steps.verify: Draft status update is saved in the docs wiki with correct formatting.

steps.action: Send the draft update to relevant stakeholders for review and feedback.
steps.verify: Notification of draft availability is delivered to stakeholders.

steps.action: Incorporate stakeholder feedback into the final status update.
steps.verify: Feedback items are marked as addressed in the documentation system.

steps.action: Publish the final status update in the project documentation repository.
steps.verify: Published update is visible and timestamped in the docs wiki.

steps.action: Confirm that all team members acknowledge receipt of the update.
steps.verify: Acknowledgements are recorded in the project communication log.

abort_conditions: Progress reports are missing or incomplete.
Stakeholder feedback indicates critical errors in the update.
Documentation system is unavailable.

name: Investigate High CPU Alert in Production
description: A site reliability engineer responds to a high CPU usage alert in the production environment. The intent is to investigate the root cause, assess impact, and restore system health while maintaining operational integrity. All activities are performed with production admin access and require thorough verification at each step.

preconditions: Network access to monitoring and logging systems is available
High CPU alert exists in monitoring system with status 'firing' or equivalent
invariants: No configuration changes are performed
No production workloads are modified
All observations and findings are recorded in the incident tracking system before task completion
refusal_conditions: Requested action would modify production workloads
Requested action would bypass incident tracking or audit logging
Monitoring or logging data required for a step is not accessible

steps.action: Acknowledge receipt of the high CPU usage alert in the monitoring system
steps.verify: Alert status is updated to acknowledged in the monitoring dashboard

constraints: alert_identifier must uniquely identify a single active high CPU alert

expected_outputs: Alert status field for alert_identifier set to 'acknowledged'
Timestamp of acknowledgment recorded in monitoring system
If incident_ticket_id provided, alert linked to that ticket
constraints: Only the status field of the specified alert_identifier may be changed
No other alerts may be modified
No alert thresholds or configurations may be edited
validation_criteria: Read back alert status for alert_identifier equals 'acknowledged'
Acknowledgment timestamp is not null and is >= task start time
guardrails: If more than one alert matches alert_identifier, abort step and do not change any status
If alert is already acknowledged, do not change status again; mark step as complete based on existing state
refusal_conditions: User does not have permission to acknowledge alerts

steps.action: Review alert details including affected hosts, severity, and alert history
steps.verify: Alert metadata and historical trend visible on monitoring dashboard

constraints: alert_identifier must be the same value used in step 1

expected_outputs: Structured record containing: affected_hosts list, severity level, alert_creation_time, alert_history_time_series
constraints: Only data associated with alert_identifier may be retrieved
validation_criteria: affected_hosts list is not empty
severity is one of ['low','medium','high','critical','sev1']
alert_history_time_series contains at least one data point
guardrails: Do not modify any alert configuration or thresholds
Do not query data for unrelated alerts
refusal_conditions: alert_identifier cannot be resolved to an alert
Alert metadata endpoint returns an error

steps.action: Check system health metrics for impacted services and nodes
steps.verify: Current CPU, memory, and process metrics are visible in system dashboards

constraints: affected_hosts must be derived from step 2 outputs

expected_outputs: For each host in affected_hosts: current CPU utilization, memory utilization, process count, timestamp
constraints: No more than 100 hosts queried in a single execution
validation_criteria: Metrics timestamp for each host is within the last 5 minutes of query time
CPU utilization value is between 0 and 100 inclusive
guardrails: Do not trigger any remediation actions from the monitoring system
Do not change any alert thresholds or dashboards

steps.action: Correlate CPU spike timing with recent changes or deployments
steps.verify: Timeline overlay shows deployment and change events alongside metric spikes

constraints: correlation_time_window_minutes, if provided, must be an integer between 5 and 240
If not provided, use fixed value 60

expected_outputs: List of change_events occurring within [alert_creation_time - window, alert_creation_time + window] with timestamps and identifiers
constraints: Read-only access to change management and deployment logs
validation_criteria: Time window applied exactly as specified
All returned change_events have timestamps within the computed window
guardrails: Do not infer causality; only record temporal proximity
Do not modify any deployment or change status

steps.action: Review recent logs for errors or anomalies related to high CPU usage
steps.verify: Relevant log entries are identified and timestamped in the log management system

constraints: log_search_time_window_minutes, if provided, must be an integer between 5 and 240
If not provided, use fixed value 60

expected_outputs: Log query result set limited to a maximum of 1000 entries containing host identifier, timestamp, log_level, and message
constraints: Read-only queries to log management system
Maximum 1000 log entries returned per execution
validation_criteria: All log entries have timestamps within the specified time window
Each log entry includes required fields: host, timestamp, message
guardrails: Do not delete or modify any logs
Do not execute queries that include wildcard host filters beyond affected_hosts
refusal_conditions: Log query returns more than 1000 entries without server-side limit support

steps.action: Identify which application components or processes are consuming excess CPU
steps.verify: Process or service list with CPU breakdown is displayed in monitoring tools

constraints: affected_hosts must be the same list used in step 3

expected_outputs: For each host: list of processes or services with CPU utilization percentage, sorted descending by CPU utilization
constraints: No process termination or modification allowed
validation_criteria: CPU utilization per host sums to between 90 and 110 percent inclusive when using per-core aggregation, or is documented as per-metric behavior
Top process per host has CPU utilization >= 0
guardrails: Do not initiate SSH or remote shell sessions
Do not run arbitrary commands on hosts
refusal_conditions: User lacks permission to view process metrics

steps.action: Check for known incidents or similar alerts in the incident tracking system
steps.verify: Related incident tickets or alerts are referenced in the ticketing system

constraints: search_time_window_hours, if provided, must be an integer between 1 and 168
If not provided, use fixed value 24

expected_outputs: List of incident tickets with status, creation_time, and short_description that reference the same service or hosts as alert_identifier within the time window
constraints: Read-only search in incident tracking system
validation_criteria: All incidents returned fall within the specified time window
Each incident includes a unique identifier and status
guardrails: Do not modify any incident status or fields
Do not create new incidents in this step

steps.action: Notify stakeholders and the on-call team about ongoing investigation and potential impact
steps.verify: Notification is logged in the incident management platform and recipients are confirmed

constraints: recipient_list must contain at least one and at most 50 recipients
Recipients must be valid identifiers in the communication system

expected_outputs: Notification entry associated with incident_ticket_id containing timestamp, sender, recipients, and message identifier
constraints: Only pre-approved notification channels may be used (email, chat, incident tool comments)
No paging or emergency broadcast features triggered by this step
validation_criteria: Incident management platform shows notification entry linked to incident_ticket_id
All recipients in recipient_list appear in the notification metadata
guardrails: Message content must not include confidential data beyond incident identifier, summary, and current status
Do not modify incident severity in this step
refusal_conditions: recipient_list is empty or contains invalid identifiers

steps.action: Cross-reference resource usage with current workload and scheduled jobs
steps.verify: Scheduled job logs and workload metrics are reviewed in dashboard

constraints: Time window for scheduled jobs is fixed at 60 minutes before and after alert_creation_time

expected_outputs: List of scheduled jobs and workloads running on affected_hosts within the defined time window, including start_time, end_time (if available), and resource usage summary
constraints: Read-only access to job scheduler and workload metrics
No modification of job schedules or workloads
validation_criteria: All returned jobs have start_time within the defined time window
Each job record includes host or workload identifier
guardrails: Do not pause, cancel, or reschedule any jobs
Do not trigger new workloads

steps.action: Check for resource contention or infrastructure issues in the cloud platform
steps.verify: Infrastructure resource graphs show no underlying hardware issues

constraints: affected_hosts must map to cloud instances or nodes

expected_outputs: For each affected host: infrastructure metrics including CPU credits (if applicable), network errors, disk IO saturation, and platform status events within the last 60 minutes
constraints: Read-only access to cloud platform monitoring
No changes to infrastructure configuration or scaling
validation_criteria: Metrics timestamps are within the last 60 minutes
Platform status events, if any, are associated with the same region/zone as affected_hosts
guardrails: Do not initiate scaling operations
Do not stop, start, or reboot any instances
refusal_conditions: Cloud platform monitoring API is unavailable

steps.action: Document preliminary findings and next steps in the incident ticket
steps.verify: Incident ticket is updated with investigation notes and timestamps
expected_outputs: New comment or update on incident_ticket_id containing preliminary_findings_summary and proposed_next_steps with timestamp and author
constraints: Only comment or description fields may be updated
Incident status and severity must remain unchanged in this step
validation_criteria: Incident ticket history shows a new entry at or after current time
Entry text matches the provided summaries
guardrails: Do not include sensitive personal data or secrets in the notes
Do not commit to remediation actions that require separate approval
refusal_conditions: User lacks permission to update the incident

steps.action: Monitor CPU usage for stabilization or further escalation
steps.verify: Live metrics confirm whether CPU usage returns to baseline or remains elevated

constraints: monitoring_duration_minutes, if provided, must be an integer between 5 and 120
If not provided, use fixed value 15

expected_outputs: Time series of CPU utilization for each affected host over the monitoring_duration_minutes interval
Computed summary per host: average CPU, max CPU over interval
constraints: Read-only metric polling at a fixed interval of 1 minute
validation_criteria: Number of data points per host equals monitoring_duration_minutes (±1 if system sampling differs)
Timestamps are monotonically increasing
guardrails: Do not trigger any automated remediation from monitoring tools
Do not suppress or mute alerts
refusal_conditions: Monitoring system cannot provide live metrics

steps.action: Escalate to engineering or platform teams if root cause is not immediately identifiable
steps.verify: Escalation is recorded in the incident tracking system and acknowledged by recipients

constraints: escalation_recipient_group must be one of a predefined set (e.g., 'application_team', 'platform_team') configured outside this JSON

expected_outputs: Incident ticket updated with escalation entry including recipient group, timestamp, and escalation_reason
constraints: Only escalation/comment fields may be updated
No change to incident severity in this step
validation_criteria: Incident history shows an escalation entry referencing escalation_recipient_group
Notification system logs show delivery to at least one member of escalation_recipient_group
guardrails: Do not escalate to groups outside the predefined set
Do not include confidential log contents directly; reference log locations instead

steps.action: Confirm resolution or containment of the alert condition
steps.verify: Monitoring dashboard shows alert cleared or returned to normal thresholds

constraints: alert_identifier must be the same as in step 1

expected_outputs: Current alert status and latest metric value for the monitored CPU metric
constraints: Read-only query of alert status and metrics
validation_criteria: Alert status is one of ['cleared','resolved','inactive'] OR remains 'firing'
Latest CPU metric value is between 0 and 100 inclusive
guardrails: Do not force-close alerts from the monitoring system
Do not disable the alert rule

steps.action: Close the alert and update incident documentation with resolution details
steps.verify: Incident ticket is marked as resolved and post-incident notes are added
expected_outputs: Incident ticket status set to 'resolved' or equivalent
Resolution comment added containing resolution_summary and timestamp
constraints: Only the specified incident_ticket_id may be updated
No other tickets may be modified
validation_criteria: Incident status read-back equals 'resolved' or equivalent terminal state
Resolution comment text matches resolution_summary
guardrails: Do not close alerts directly in monitoring; closure is limited to incident ticket
Do not alter incident creation time or historical entries
refusal_conditions: User lacks permission to resolve incidents

steps.action: Initiate post-incident review for further analysis and preventive actions
steps.verify: Post-incident review is scheduled and referenced in documentation

constraints: review_scheduled_time must be an ISO 8601 timestamp

expected_outputs: Post-incident review record created or updated with link to incident_ticket_id and scheduled time
Incident ticket updated with reference to post-incident review record
constraints: Only scheduling metadata and references may be created
No modification of incident resolution details
validation_criteria: Review record exists and includes incident_ticket_id and review_scheduled_time
Incident ticket contains a link or identifier referencing the review record
guardrails: Do not include detailed root cause analysis in this step; only scheduling and linkage
Do not modify project or team assignments

abort_conditions: Verification steps fail or metrics are unavailable
Alert is determined to be a false positive or already resolved
Access to monitoring or production systems is restricted

name: Production Release Deployment
description: A site reliability engineer initiates the deployment of a new software release to the production environment. The process follows established change management practices, with checks at each stage to ensure service stability and compliance. Approval is required before executing any changes that impact production workloads.

preconditions: Release candidate artifact is available in CI/CD system
Change request exists in change management system for this deployment
invariants: All production changes are executed via CI/CD system only
Rollback plan exists and is documented before deployment start
All actions are logged in change management or CI/CD systems
refusal_conditions: Manager approval is not recorded in change management system
Monitoring indicates production is in an unstable state before deployment
Requested deployment window conflicts with a frozen period defined outside this JSON

steps.action: Review the deployment plan and associated release notes for completeness and accuracy.
steps.verify: Deployment plan and release notes are up-to-date and approved in the documentation system.

constraints: deployment_plan_id and release_notes_id must reference existing documents in the documentation system

expected_outputs: Read-only view of deployment_plan_id and release_notes_id contents
Recorded timestamp of review in change management or documentation system
constraints: No modification of deployment plan or release notes in this step
validation_criteria: Both documents have an 'approved' status field set to true or equivalent
Document last_modified_time is not later than current time
guardrails: If either document is not approved, mark step as failed and do not proceed with deployment execution steps
Do not alter approval status fields
refusal_conditions: deployment_plan_id or release_notes_id does not exist

steps.action: Check production environment health and capacity for readiness.
steps.verify: Production monitoring dashboards show all systems operational and no capacity warnings.

constraints: service_identifiers list size must be between 1 and 50

expected_outputs: Snapshot of key health metrics (error rate, latency, CPU, memory) for each service identifier at check time
validation_criteria: Metrics timestamps are within 5 minutes of check time
Each service has a status indicator field
guardrails: Do not suppress existing alerts
Do not trigger any remediation actions
refusal_conditions: Any service status indicator is 'critical' or equivalent

steps.action: Validate that all automated tests have passed in the staging environment.
steps.verify: CI/CD pipeline reports all tests as successful in staging.

constraints: pipeline_run_id must correspond to the staging pipeline run for this release

expected_outputs: Test summary for pipeline_run_id including pass/fail counts and overall status
constraints: Read-only access to CI/CD pipeline data
validation_criteria: Overall pipeline status equals 'success' or equivalent
Number of failed tests equals 0
guardrails: Do not re-run tests from this step
Do not modify pipeline configuration

steps.action: Confirm that the rollback plan is documented and accessible.
steps.verify: Rollback procedures are reviewed and linked in the deployment documentation.

constraints: rollback_plan_id must reference an existing document in the documentation system

expected_outputs: Recorded linkage between rollback_plan_id and deployment_plan_id in documentation or change management system
constraints: No modification of rollback plan content
validation_criteria: Rollback plan document has status 'approved' or equivalent
Rollback plan includes at least one step referencing the current release version
guardrails: If rollback plan is missing or unapproved, do not proceed to deployment execution steps
Do not create or edit rollback plans in this step

steps.action: Request manager approval for the production deployment.
steps.verify: Manager approval is recorded in the change management system.

constraints: change_request_id must reference an existing change record
manager_identifier must be a valid approver in the system

expected_outputs: Approval record associated with change_request_id including manager_identifier, approval_status, and timestamp
constraints: This step may only record or query approval; it may not auto-approve
Approval_status must be explicitly set by manager outside this executor
validation_criteria: Approval record for change_request_id exists with status 'approved'
Approval timestamp is not later than deployment start time
guardrails: Do not modify approval_status fields directly
If approval is not present, mark step as failed and do not proceed to deployment execution steps
refusal_conditions: No approval record with status 'approved' is found

steps.action: Announce the scheduled deployment window to stakeholders.
steps.verify: Deployment notice is posted in the designated communication channel.

constraints: deployment_window_start and deployment_window_end must be ISO 8601 timestamps
deployment_window_end must be later than deployment_window_start
stakeholder_recipient_list size must be between 1 and 100

expected_outputs: Notification message posted in communication channel including deployment_window_start and deployment_window_end
constraints: Only designated communication channels may be used
validation_criteria: Notification content includes both start and end timestamps
All recipients in stakeholder_recipient_list are included in notification metadata when applicable
guardrails: Do not include sensitive technical details or secrets in the message
Do not modify stakeholder lists outside this notification

steps.action: Place production system into maintenance or pre-deployment state as required.
steps.verify: Maintenance status is visible on status dashboards and communicated to users.

constraints: maintenance_window_start and maintenance_window_end must be ISO 8601 timestamps
maintenance_window_end must be later than maintenance_window_start

expected_outputs: Status page entry for status_page_id updated to indicate maintenance during the specified window
constraints: Only status/communication fields may be updated
No changes to underlying infrastructure or routing
validation_criteria: Status page shows 'maintenance' or equivalent during the specified window
Change is logged with timestamp and actor
guardrails: Do not extend maintenance window beyond maintenance_window_end
Do not mark unrelated services as under maintenance

steps.action: Initiate the deployment process using the CI/CD system.
steps.verify: CI/CD system logs show deployment process has started.

constraints: production_pipeline_id must reference a production deployment pipeline
release_artifact_id must reference a built and immutable artifact

expected_outputs: New CI/CD deployment run identifier for production_pipeline_id using release_artifact_id
constraints: Deployment must be triggered only via CI/CD system
No manual changes to production systems outside the pipeline
validation_criteria: Deployment run status transitions from 'pending' to 'running'
Run metadata references the correct release_artifact_id
guardrails: Do not modify pipeline configuration in this step
Do not trigger multiple concurrent deployments for the same service
refusal_conditions: production_pipeline_id or release_artifact_id does not exist

steps.action: Monitor deployment progress and check for errors or warnings.
steps.verify: Deployment logs and dashboards show no critical errors during rollout.

constraints: deployment_run_id must be the identifier created in step 8

expected_outputs: Deployment run status and log summary including counts of errors, warnings, and successful steps
constraints: No modification of deployment steps from this monitoring step
validation_criteria: Deployment run status is one of ['running','success','failed']
Critical error count is accurately read from logs
guardrails: Do not retry or cancel deployments from this step
If critical errors are detected, mark step as failed and rely on abort_conditions or external procedures

steps.action: Run post-deployment health checks and smoke tests.
steps.verify: Health checks and smoke test results are successful and logged.

constraints: post_deploy_test_suite_id must reference a predefined test suite

expected_outputs: Test execution report including pass/fail status for each health check and smoke test
constraints: Tests must be read-only or non-destructive to production data
No data modification beyond what is explicitly defined in test suite outside this JSON
validation_criteria: Overall test suite status is 'success'
Number of failed tests equals 0
guardrails: Do not modify test definitions in this step
Do not run additional unapproved tests

steps.action: Remove maintenance status and notify stakeholders of deployment completion.
steps.verify: System is restored to normal operation and stakeholders receive confirmation.

constraints: stakeholder_recipient_list size must be between 1 and 100

expected_outputs: Status page entry for status_page_id updated to 'operational' or equivalent
Notification message sent to stakeholder_recipient_list indicating deployment completion
constraints: Only status/communication fields may be updated
validation_criteria: Status page no longer shows maintenance for the service
Notification logs show successful delivery attempts to all recipients
guardrails: Do not change historical maintenance records
Do not include sensitive deployment details in notification

steps.action: Update deployment records with results and any lessons learned.
steps.verify: Deployment records are updated in the change management or documentation system.
expected_outputs: Change record for change_request_id updated with deployment_outcome and lessons_learned_summary
Timestamped entry added to deployment documentation
constraints: Only descriptive and outcome fields may be updated
validation_criteria: Read-back of change_request_id shows deployment_outcome set as provided
Documentation entry text matches lessons_learned_summary
guardrails: Do not alter change_request_id scheduling fields
Do not change associated release identifiers
refusal_conditions: User lacks permission to update change records

abort_conditions: Manager approval is denied or delayed beyond the scheduled window.
Critical error detected in health checks or deployment logs.
Production environment is not in a safe or ready state for deployment.

name: Regression Testing on Staging Release
description: A QA engineer performs regression testing on a new release candidate in the staging environment. The goal is to ensure that recent code changes have not negatively impacted existing functionalities. All results are documented, and critical issues are reported before deployment approval.

preconditions: Release candidate is deployed to staging
Test plan exists for the release
invariants: All defects are logged in the defect tracking system
Test evidence is stored in the designated repository
No changes are made to application code during testing
refusal_conditions: Staging environment is not aligned with target release version
Test plan or release notes are missing

steps.action: Review the release notes and test plan for the staging build
steps.verify: Test plan is updated and release notes are accessible in documentation

constraints: release_notes_id and test_plan_id must reference existing documents

expected_outputs: Read-only view of release_notes_id and test_plan_id
Recorded timestamp of review in test management or documentation system
constraints: No modification of documents in this step
validation_criteria: Both documents have status 'current' or equivalent
Documents reference the same release version as the staging build identifier (provided externally)
guardrails: If documents are outdated or mismatched to staging build, mark step as failed
Do not change document status fields
refusal_conditions: release_notes_id or test_plan_id does not exist

steps.action: Access the staging environment using authorized credentials
steps.verify: Successful login and access to staging system is logged

constraints: staging_endpoint must point to staging_only environment
user_credentials_reference must refer to a secure credential store entry

expected_outputs: Active authenticated session to staging environment
Audit log entry indicating successful access
constraints: Credentials must not be logged in plaintext
Access limited to staging environment only
validation_criteria: Authentication response indicates success
Audit log contains entry with user_profile.id and staging_endpoint
guardrails: Do not reuse credentials for any other environment
Do not attempt privilege escalation

steps.action: Execute automated regression test suite on the new build
steps.verify: Automated test results are recorded and available in CI/CD reports

constraints: regression_test_suite_id must reference a predefined suite
staging_build_identifier must match the deployed build in staging

expected_outputs: Automated test run report including pass/fail status per test case and overall status
constraints: Tests must be non-destructive or use disposable test data
validation_criteria: Overall status is recorded as 'success' or 'failure'
All test cases in regression_test_suite_id have a result
guardrails: Do not run tests against production endpoints
Do not alter environment configuration as part of tests beyond what is predefined

steps.action: Manually validate critical user flows as described in the test plan
steps.verify: Manual test cases are marked complete in the test management tool

constraints: critical_test_case_ids list size must be between 1 and 200

expected_outputs: Updated status for each test case in critical_test_case_ids in the test management tool
constraints: Only status and evidence fields for the specified test cases may be updated
validation_criteria: Each test case in critical_test_case_ids has a status of 'passed' or 'failed'
Timestamps for updates are recorded
guardrails: Do not modify test case definitions
Do not mark tests as passed without execution evidence stored externally

steps.action: Log any defects or anomalies identified during testing
steps.verify: Defects are recorded in the tracking system with relevant details

constraints: defect_summaries must be a list of structured entries each containing title, description, severity

expected_outputs: New defect records in defect tracking system for each entry in defect_summaries
constraints: Only create new defects; do not modify existing ones in this step
validation_criteria: Each created defect has a unique identifier
Each defect includes severity and is linked to the staging_build_identifier
guardrails: Do not include sensitive data such as credentials in defect descriptions
Do not auto-assign defects to users not specified by external policy

steps.action: Re-test resolved defects in the staging environment
steps.verify: Defect status is updated to 'verified' upon successful retest

constraints: defect_ids_to_retest list size must be between 1 and 200

expected_outputs: Updated status for each defect in defect_ids_to_retest after retest
constraints: Only status and verification comment fields may be updated
validation_criteria: Each defect in defect_ids_to_retest has status set to 'verified' if retest passed or remains unchanged if failed
Verification timestamp is recorded
guardrails: Do not close defects; only set to 'verified' where applicable
Do not change defect severity
refusal_conditions: Any defect_id in defect_ids_to_retest does not exist

steps.action: Document test results and overall quality assessment
steps.verify: Test summary report is available for stakeholder review
expected_outputs: Test summary report stored in documentation or test management system with overall_quality_assessment
constraints: Only create or update the summary document for this release
validation_criteria: Summary document includes reference to staging_build_identifier
overall_quality_assessment field matches provided value
guardrails: Do not alter release approval status from this step
Do not include sensitive test data beyond anonymized examples
refusal_conditions: Documentation or test management system is unavailable

steps.action: Communicate testing status and any blocking issues to the release manager
steps.verify: Release manager acknowledges receipt of status update
expected_outputs: Notification or message sent to release_manager_identifier containing testing_status_summary
Acknowledgment record or read receipt where supported
constraints: No modification of release schedule from this step
validation_criteria: Communication system logs show message delivered to release_manager_identifier
Acknowledgment flag or response is recorded if supported
guardrails: Do not include raw logs or PII in the message
Do not change release manager assignment

steps.action: Archive test artifacts and evidence in the designated repository
steps.verify: Artifacts are accessible in the QA documentation area

constraints: artifact_references must be a list of paths or identifiers for logs, screenshots, and reports

expected_outputs: All artifact_references copied or linked into the designated QA repository location for this release
constraints: Read from source locations, write only to designated QA repository
validation_criteria: Each artifact_reference has a corresponding entry in the QA repository index
Access check confirms readability of each archived artifact
guardrails: Do not store credentials or secrets in artifacts
Do not overwrite artifacts from other releases

abort_conditions: Automated or manual tests reveal critical blocking issues
Staging environment is unavailable or unstable
Test plan or release notes are incomplete or missing

name: Perform Database Schema Migration
description: A platform engineer initiates a schema migration on the production database as part of routine maintenance. The process includes planning, communication, execution, and validation to ensure minimal disruption and data integrity.

preconditions: Migration script or tool is prepared and version-controlled
Change request for migration exists in change management system
Staging environment is available for validation
invariants: All schema changes are executed from approved migration scripts
Backups are taken and verified before production migration
All operations are logged with timestamps and operator identity
refusal_conditions: Backup cannot be completed or verified

steps.action: Review and document the planned schema changes with relevant stakeholders.
steps.verify: Stakeholder approval documented and migration plan reviewed.

constraints: migration_plan_id must reference an existing document
stakeholder_list size must be between 1 and 50

expected_outputs: Recorded review entry including stakeholder_list and timestamp in change management or documentation system
constraints: No modification of migration plan content in this step
validation_criteria: Migration plan has status 'approved' or equivalent
Review entry references migration_plan_id and all stakeholders
guardrails: Do not change stakeholder approvals
If migration plan is not approved, mark step as failed

steps.action: Obtain formal manager approval for the migration window.
steps.verify: Approval confirmation received and recorded.

constraints: change_request_id must reference an existing change record
manager_identifier must be an authorized approver

expected_outputs: Approval record for change_request_id with manager_identifier, approval_status, and timestamp
constraints: Executor may only read approval status, not set it
validation_criteria: Approval_status for change_request_id is 'approved'
Approval timestamp is before planned migration start time
guardrails: Do not modify approval records
If approval is missing or denied, do not proceed to migration execution steps

steps.action: Communicate the scheduled migration to affected teams and update status channels.
steps.verify: Notification acknowledged by stakeholders and status updated.

constraints: migration_window_start and migration_window_end must be ISO 8601 timestamps
migration_window_end must be later than migration_window_start
affected_team_recipients size must be between 1 and 100

expected_outputs: Notifications sent to affected_team_recipients with migration window details
Status page entry updated to indicate upcoming maintenance
constraints: Only communication and status fields may be updated
validation_criteria: Communication logs show messages delivered to all affected_team_recipients
Status page shows scheduled maintenance for the database service
guardrails: Do not modify unrelated services on status page
Do not include sensitive schema details in notifications
refusal_conditions: Communication or status page systems are unavailable

steps.action: Perform a backup of the production database prior to migration.
steps.verify: Backup completion verified and backup integrity confirmed.

constraints: database_identifier must reference the target production database
backup_policy_id must reference an approved backup configuration

expected_outputs: New backup artifact identifier associated with database_identifier
constraints: Backup must be a full backup or as defined in backup_policy_id
No schema changes performed in this step
validation_criteria: Backup job status is 'success'
Integrity check or checksum verification passes
guardrails: Do not delete existing backups
Do not proceed to migration if backup fails or cannot be verified

steps.action: Apply the schema migration to a staging environment for validation.
steps.verify: Staging environment reflects intended schema changes without errors.

constraints: staging_database_identifier must reference a non-production database
migration_script_reference must point to an approved migration script

expected_outputs: Execution log of migration_script_reference applied to staging_database_identifier
Updated schema version or metadata in staging
constraints: Migration must be executed only against staging_database_identifier
No data outside staging environment is modified
validation_criteria: Migration execution log status is 'success'
Schema version in staging matches expected version from migration_plan_id
guardrails: Do not run migration against production in this step
Do not modify migration_script_reference contents

steps.action: Validate key application workflows in staging post-migration.
steps.verify: Test results show no breakages or data integrity issues.

constraints: validation_test_suite_id must reference a predefined test suite for workflows

expected_outputs: Test report for validation_test_suite_id against staging_database_identifier
constraints: Tests must not modify production data
Tests must run only against staging environment
validation_criteria: All critical tests in the suite have status 'passed'
No data integrity check reports inconsistencies
guardrails: Do not modify test definitions
If critical tests fail, do not proceed to production migration

steps.action: Schedule a maintenance window for the production schema migration.
steps.verify: Maintenance window published and acknowledged by teams.

constraints: migration_window_start and migration_window_end must be ISO 8601 timestamps
migration_window_end must be later than migration_window_start
affected_team_recipients size must be between 1 and 100

expected_outputs: Status page updated with maintenance window for database service
constraints: Only communication and status fields may be updated
validation_criteria: Status page shows maintenance scheduled for the specified window
Communication logs confirm notifications sent
guardrails: Do not schedule maintenance outside approved change window from change_request_id
Do not modify unrelated services
refusal_conditions: Status page or communication systems are unavailable

steps.action: Disable non-essential database access during the migration window.
steps.verify: Access logs confirm restricted connections.

constraints: non_essential_user_roles must be a list of roles or accounts to restrict
List size must be between 1 and 200

expected_outputs: Updated access control configuration for database_identifier reflecting disabled non_essential_user_roles
Access log entries showing denied connections for disabled roles during migration window
constraints: Only roles listed in non_essential_user_roles may be modified
Administrative and migration accounts must remain enabled
validation_criteria: Access control configuration shows non_essential_user_roles as disabled or blocked
Test connection attempts using a non-essential role fail
guardrails: Do not disable critical service accounts required for application operation if specified externally
Do not alter password or credential data
refusal_conditions: Database access control system is unavailable

steps.action: Apply the schema migration to the production database.
steps.verify: Schema version updated and migration logs confirm successful execution.

constraints: database_identifier must reference the production database
migration_script_reference must be the same as used in staging (step 5)

expected_outputs: Migration execution log for production database
Updated schema version or metadata in production
constraints: Migration must be executed within the approved migration window
No modification of migration script contents
validation_criteria: Migration execution log status is 'success'
Schema version in production matches staging schema version
guardrails: Do not run additional ad-hoc SQL outside migration_script_reference
If migration fails, do not attempt automatic re-run in this step
refusal_conditions: Current time is outside approved migration window

steps.action: Monitor application and database metrics for anomalies post-migration.
steps.verify: Metrics remain within expected thresholds and no new alerts are triggered.

constraints: monitoring_window_minutes must be an integer between 5 and 120

expected_outputs: Time series metrics for database performance (latency, error rate, CPU, connections) over monitoring_window_minutes
Summary of alerts triggered during this period
constraints: Read-only access to monitoring and alerting systems
validation_criteria: Metrics timestamps are within the monitoring window
Alert list accurately reflects monitoring system state
guardrails: Do not suppress or mute alerts
Do not change monitoring thresholds

steps.action: Re-enable previously disabled database access.
steps.verify: Access restored and connection logs reflect normal activity.

constraints: non_essential_user_roles must match the list used in step 8

expected_outputs: Access control configuration updated to restore non_essential_user_roles
Access logs showing successful connections for restored roles
constraints: Only roles previously disabled in step 8 may be re-enabled
validation_criteria: Access control configuration shows roles as enabled
Test connection attempts using a restored role succeed
guardrails: Do not enable any roles that were not disabled in step 8
Do not change privileges beyond enabled/disabled state
refusal_conditions: Database access control system is unavailable

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of migration completion and system status.
steps.verify: Stakeholder acknowledgments received and incident channels updated.

constraints: affected_team_recipients size must be between 1 and 100

expected_outputs: Notification messages sent to affected_team_recipients with migration_outcome_summary
Optional update in incident or change channels
validation_criteria: Communication logs show messages delivered
If incident or change channels are used, entries reference migration_plan_id or change_request_id
guardrails: Do not include sensitive schema details or data in notifications
Do not change incident or change statuses in this step

steps.action: Document the migration outcome, including any issues and resolutions.
steps.verify: Migration report filed and accessible in documentation systems.
expected_outputs: Migration report document created or updated in documentation system
Report linked to migration_plan_id and change_request_id
constraints: Only the report for this migration may be created or updated
No modification of previous migration reports
validation_criteria: Report is accessible via documentation system
Report metadata includes references to migration_plan_id, change_request_id, and database_identifier
guardrails: Do not store credentials or sensitive data dumps in the report
Do not alter approval or change history

abort_conditions: Manager approval is denied or delayed beyond maintenance window.
Backup or staging validation fails.
Unexpected anomalies detected during migration execution.

name: Track Delivery Milestone Progress
description: A project manager reviews the progress of key delivery milestones for an active project. The manager checks status updates, verifies completion evidence, and documents any schedule or resource risks. This process ensures transparent reporting and enables timely escalation of issues.

preconditions: Project exists in project tracking system
invariants: No write operations are performed on project data
All observations are recorded in a separate status update entry
refusal_conditions: User has write access instead of read-only (policy violation)

steps.action: Access the project documentation repository
steps.verify: Project workspace is accessible and up to date

constraints: project_identifier must reference an existing project workspace

expected_outputs: Read-only access to project workspace associated with project_identifier
validation_criteria: Workspace open operation succeeds
Workspace metadata shows last_updated timestamp not older than a policy-defined threshold (configured externally)
guardrails: Do not edit any documents
If workspace appears stale per external policy, mark step as failed

steps.action: Review the latest project timeline and milestone list
steps.verify: Milestone schedule is present and reflects current targets
expected_outputs: Read-only view of project timeline and milestone list for project_identifier
constraints: No edits to timeline or milestones
validation_criteria: Milestone list is non-empty
Each milestone has a target_date and status field
guardrails: Do not adjust dates or statuses
Do not create or delete milestones
refusal_conditions: Timeline or milestone list cannot be found

steps.action: Check status updates for each active milestone
steps.verify: Status fields for milestones are populated and current

constraints: milestone_list must be derived from step 2
Active milestones are those with status not in ['completed','cancelled']

expected_outputs: For each active milestone: current status, last_updated timestamp, and owner
constraints: Read-only access to project tracking data
validation_criteria: Each active milestone has a non-null status
Each active milestone has a last_updated timestamp
guardrails: Do not modify milestone statuses
Do not reassign milestone owners

steps.action: Collect evidence of milestone deliverable completion
steps.verify: Supporting documents or links for completed milestones are attached

constraints: completed_milestones must be a subset of milestone_list with status 'completed'

expected_outputs: List of evidence references (documents or links) associated with each completed milestone
constraints: Read-only retrieval of evidence; no new attachments created in this step
validation_criteria: Each completed milestone has at least zero or more evidence references (no minimum enforced by executor)
Evidence references are valid links or document identifiers
guardrails: Do not upload or modify evidence
Do not change milestone completion status

steps.action: Identify any overdue milestones or schedule slippage
steps.verify: Overdue items are highlighted in the milestone tracker

constraints: current_date must be an ISO 8601 date

expected_outputs: Derived list of milestones where target_date < current_date and status not in ['completed','cancelled']
constraints: Computation is read-only; no changes to tracker
validation_criteria: All milestones in derived list meet overdue condition
No milestones outside this condition are included
guardrails: Do not update milestone tracker highlighting; only compute and report
Do not change milestone dates or statuses

steps.action: Note resource constraints or blockers impacting delivery
steps.verify: Risks and blockers are documented in the project risk log

constraints: risk_log_identifier must reference an existing risk log

expected_outputs: Read-only view of current risks and blockers from risk_log_identifier
constraints: No modification of risk or issue records
validation_criteria: Risk log entries include description, owner, and status fields
guardrails: Do not add, edit, or close risks
Do not change risk ratings

steps.action: Summarize progress and risks in a status update entry
steps.verify: New status update is timestamped and visible in the documentation
expected_outputs: New status update document or entry created in documentation or project tracking system linked to project_identifier
constraints: Only create a new status entry; no modification of existing entries
validation_criteria: Status update entry includes timestamp and author (user_profile.id)
Entry is retrievable via project_identifier
guardrails: Do not change project baseline data
Do not include confidential HR or personal data in the status content
refusal_conditions: Documentation or project tracking system is unavailable

steps.action: Share the updated milestone progress report with the project team
steps.verify: Notification or summary is sent to all relevant team members

constraints: project_team_recipient_list size must be between 1 and 100

expected_outputs: Notification sent to project_team_recipient_list containing link or reference to status_update_reference
validation_criteria: Communication logs show messages delivered to all recipients
Message content includes status_update_reference
guardrails: Do not modify project team membership
Do not include sensitive financial or HR data in the message

abort_conditions: Project documentation is inaccessible
Milestone status data is missing or outdated
Material discrepancies are detected in milestone evidence

name: Restore Production Database from Backup
description: A site reliability engineer is restoring the production database from the latest backup due to a critical incident. This operation is urgent, involves confidential data, and requires following destructive change controls with security approval. The process ensures business continuity and data integrity for the platform.

steps.action: Acknowledge the alert indicating database corruption or data loss
steps.verify: Incident ticket is updated to acknowledged state

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of planned database restore and expected downtime
steps.verify: Stakeholder notification is visible in incident communication channel

steps.action: Review and confirm the scope of data loss and affected services
steps.verify: Incident documentation reflects validated impact assessment

steps.action: Obtain security approval for destructive database restore operation
steps.verify: Approval is recorded in change management or ticketing system

steps.action: Identify and select the most recent valid backup for the production database
steps.verify: Backup selection is documented in the incident ticket

steps.action: Take a snapshot of the current (corrupted) database state for post-mortem analysis
steps.verify: Snapshot confirmation is logged in backup inventory

steps.action: Isolate database access for all non-essential users and systems
steps.verify: Access logs show only authorized restore operations in progress

steps.action: Initiate the database restore process using the selected backup
steps.verify: Restore progress is visible in monitoring or backup dashboard

steps.action: Monitor restore process for errors or integrity warnings
steps.verify: No critical errors are reported in restore logs

steps.action: Run post-restore integrity and consistency checks on the database
steps.verify: Integrity check results are attached to the incident ticket

steps.action: Re-enable database access for essential services
steps.verify: Service status dashboard shows application connectivity restored

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of successful database restoration and service recovery
steps.verify: Stakeholder communication is updated with recovery confirmation

steps.action: Document actions taken, lessons learned, and update disaster recovery runbook
steps.verify: Incident post-mortem and documentation are completed and accessible

steps.action: Schedule a follow-up review to improve backup and recovery processes
steps.verify: Follow-up task is created in team planning system

abort_conditions: Approval for destructive restore is denied
Backup integrity cannot be verified
Critical errors occur during restore process

name: Scale Deployment in Response to Alert
description: An SRE oncall is tasked with scaling a Kubernetes deployment in the production environment after receiving an alert about resource saturation. This operation must be performed safely, ensuring service availability and compliance with internal access protocols. Actions and verifications are structured to minimize risk and document the process for post-incident review.

steps.action: Acknowledge the alert in the monitoring system
steps.verify: Alert status updated to acknowledged in monitoring dashboard

steps.action: Review alert details and impacted deployment information
steps.verify: Incident timeline and deployment metadata are documented

steps.action: Assess current resource utilization and deployment health
steps.verify: Resource graphs and health indicators reflect current state

steps.action: Inform the relevant stakeholders about the scaling operation
steps.verify: Stakeholders receive notification in the incident channel

steps.action: Determine the appropriate new replica count based on load
steps.verify: Scaling plan documented and reviewed in ticket or runbook

steps.action: Apply scaling change to the deployment in the production cluster
steps.verify: Deployment status reflects updated replica count

steps.action: Monitor rollout progress and verify all pods become healthy
steps.verify: All new pods report ready state in cluster dashboard

steps.action: Check application logs for errors or anomalies post-scaling
steps.verify: No new errors detected in central logging system

steps.action: Validate service endpoints remain available to users
steps.verify: Synthetic monitoring checks pass and service is reachable

steps.action: Update the incident or alert ticket with actions taken and outcomes
steps.verify: Ticket contains detailed post-action notes and timestamps

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of scaling completion and current status
steps.verify: Status update delivered in incident channel and acknowledged

steps.action: Document any lessons learned for post-incident review
steps.verify: Incident post-mortem draft includes scaling summary

abort_conditions: Verification step fails or deployment health degrades after scaling
Stakeholder or policy approval for scaling is explicitly denied
Production environment is deemed unstable or unsafe to proceed
